There is currently\\nno constructor that produces these certificates from the standing\\n`ForwardRecoveryInputs f d` package (which carries\\n`candidates_eq : Hex.bhksIndicatorCandidates? f d expectedIndicators = some\\nexpectedFactors`).\\n\\nThe executable check inside `Hex.bhksIndicatorCandidate?`\\n([HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean:4886](../HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean#L4886))\\nbuilds each candidate as\\n\\n```lean\\nselected = bhksIndicatorSelectedFactors d.liftedFactors indicator\\nraw      = DensePoly.scale (DensePoly.leadingCoeff f) (Array.polyProduct selected)\\ncandidate = normalizeFactorSign (normalizeCandidateFactor\\n              (centeredLiftPoly (ZPoly.reduceModPow raw d.p d.k) modulus))\\n```\\n\\nso the candidate matches `raw` mod `d.p ^ d.k` after the centred-lift and\\nsign-normalisation layer. For canonical indicators\\n`expectedIndicatorArrayOfSupports trueSupports`, the existing identifications\\n\\n- `Hex.bhksIndicatorSelectedFactors_expectedIndicatorArrayOfSupports`\\n  ([Recovery.lean:1170](../HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean#L1170)),\\n- `selectedFactorsOfMembers_polyProduct`\\n  ([Recovery.lean:937](../HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean#L937)),\\n- `liftedFactorSubsetsOfSupports_getD`\\n  ([Recovery.lean:1021](../HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean#L1021)),\\n\\nidentify `Array.polyProduct selected = liftedFactorProduct d\\n(liftedFactorSubsetsOfSupports d trueSupports).getD i ∅`, so the per-class\\n`raw` equals `scaledLiftedFactorProduct core d S_i`. The remaining content is\\nthe reverse of `bhksIndicatorCandidate?_eq_some_of_mignottePrecision`\\n([Basic.lean:5032](../HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean#L5032)): from a\\nsuccessful candidate output, recover the\\n`reduceModPow scaledLiftedFactorProduct = reduceModPow expectedFactor`\\nequality.\\n\\nThe sibling-issue split called out in\\n[`progress/20260603T031618Z_9a09327e.md`](../progress/20260603T031618Z_9a09327e.md):\\n\\n> construct the per-canonical-class `RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift`\\n> certificates and the `ExpectedTrueFactors` package at\\n> `factorFastCapLiftData f primeData` from the squarefree-core\\n> factorisation data\\n\\nThis issue is the certificates piece; #6448 is the `ExpectedTrueFactors`\\npiece.\\n\\n## Deliverables\\n\\nIn `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean` (and, if needed, small\\nsupporting lemmas in `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean` for the\\nmod-preservation of `normalizeCandidateFactor` / `normalizeFactorSign`):\\n\\n1. **Per-candidate certificate extraction.** Prove\\n   ```lean\\n   theorem Hex.bhksIndicatorCandidate?_representsIntegerFactorAtLift\\n     {core : Hex.ZPoly} {d : Hex.LiftData} {indicator : Array Int}\\n     {candidate quotient : Hex.ZPoly} {members : List Nat}\\n     (h : Hex.bhksIndicatorCandidate? core d indicator = some (candidate, quotient))\\n     (hselected :\\n        Hex.bhksIndicatorSelectedFactors d.liftedFactors indicator =\\n          some (selectedFactorsOfMembers d.liftedFactors members)) :\\n       RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift core d candidate\\n         (liftedFactorSubsetOfMembers d members)\\n   ```\\n   The proof unfolds `bhksIndicatorCandidate?` against `hselected`, uses\\n   `selectedFactorsOfMembers_polyProduct` to identify the executable product\\n   with `liftedFactorProduct d (liftedFactorSubsetOfMembers d members)`, and\\n   then discharges the `reduceModPow` equality by composing\\n   `Hex.ZPoly.reduceModPow_centeredLiftPoly_reduceModPow_eq`-style identities\\n   (i.e., that the centred-lift + reduce round-trip is a `reduceModPow`\\n   identity) with the existing mod-preservation of `normalizeCandidateFactor`\\n   and `normalizeFactorSign`. Add the small mod-preservation lemmas (private\\n   in `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`) if they are not already present.\\n\\n2. **Canonical per-class certificate family.** Prove\\n   ```lean\\n   theorem ForwardRecoveryInputs.bhksIndicatorCandidates?_canonicalRepresentations\\n     {f : Hex.ZPoly} {d : Hex.LiftData}\\n     (h : ForwardRecoveryInputs f d)\\n     (trueSupports :\\n        Set (Set (Fin (projectedRowsOfLiftData f d h.rows_pos).factorCount)))\\n     (hindicators :\\n        h.expectedIndicators = expectedIndicatorArrayOfSupports trueSupports) :\\n       ∀ i, i < (expectedIndicatorArrayOfSupports trueSupports).size →\\n         RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift f d (h.expectedFactors.getD i 0)\\n           ((liftedFactorSubsetsOfSupports d trueSupports).getD i ∅)\\n   ```\\n   The proof routes through (1) and the canonical-indicator selection\\n   identifications listed above. The `hindicators` premise lets the caller\\n   align the `ForwardRecoveryInputs` indicators with the canonical\\n   `expectedIndicatorArrayOfSupports` shape; for the actual cap path it is\\n   discharged by `equivalenceClassIndicatorsOfLiftData_eq_expectedIndicatorArrayOfSupports`\\n   composed with `h.indicators_match`.\\n\\n3. **Wrapper instantiated at the actual cap.** Optionally expose a thin\\n   wrapper specialised to `core = (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore`,\\n   `d = factorFastCapLiftData f primeData`, matching the exact hypothesis\\n   shape of\\n   `CanonicalRecoveryTailInputs.ofCanonicalSupportRepresentations`\\n   ([Recovery.lean:3794](../HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean#L3794)).\\n   If trivial, just call (2) inline at the use site instead.\\n\\n4. Keep executable definitions unchanged. Do not introduce `sorry`,\\n   `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, or `FIXME`.\\n\\n## Context\\n\\n- Parent BHKS D1 residual umbrella: #6412.\\n- Sibling (other recovery-tail input): #6448 (`ExpectedTrueFactors`\\n  constructor).\\n- Downstream consumer:\\n  `CanonicalRecoveryTailInputs.ofCanonicalSupportRepresentations`\\n  from #6442 / [Recovery.lean:3794](../HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean#L3794).\\n- Worker note carrying the (a)/(b) split is in\\n  [`progress/20260603T031618Z_9a09327e.md`](../progress/20260603T031618Z_9a09327e.md).\\n- The converse direction (certificate ⇒ candidate equality) is\\n  `Hex.bhksIndicatorCandidate?_eq_some_of_mignottePrecision`\\n  ([Basic.lean:5032](../HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean#L5032)). The\\n  proof of (1) uses the centred-lift + reduce-mod chain in the forward\\n  direction.\\n- Key existing identifications:\\n  `Hex.bhksIndicatorSelectedFactors_expectedIndicatorArrayOfSupports`,\\n  `selectedFactorsOfMembers_polyProduct`,\\n  `liftedFactorSubsetsOfSupports_getD`,\\n  `centeredLiftPoly_reduceModPow_eq`\\n  ([HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean:4408](../HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean#L4408)).\\n\\n## Out of scope\\n\\n- The `ExpectedTrueFactors` constructor (sibling issue #6448).\\n- The end-to-end\\n  `CanonicalRecoveryTailInputs.ofForwardRecoveryInputs` producer, which\\n  composes this issue + #6448 + the existing\\n  `ofCanonicalSupportRepresentations` constructor. A follow-up issue\\n  covers it after both siblings land.\\n- The cap-side strict-arithmetic comparison (#6445).\\n- Any change to the executable\\n  `bhksIndicatorCandidate?` / `bhksIndicatorCandidates?` definitions.\\n- Editing `SPEC/`, top-level `PLAN.md`, `AGENTS.md`, or workflow files.\\n\\n## Verification\\n\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus`\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.Recovery`\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`\\n- `lake build`\\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\\n- `git diff --check`\\n- Added-line forbidden-token grep over touched Lean files finds no `sorry`,\\n  `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, or `FIXME`.\\n\",\"closed\":false,\"closedAt\":null,\"comments\":[{\"id\":\"IC_kwDOSKC1W88AAAABEriH_g\",\"author\":{\"login\":\"kim-em\"},\"authorAssociation\":\"OWNER\",\"body\":\"Session: `7cc0f088-e1ab-41fd-916f-9dadc261697e` Branch: `agent/7cc0f088`\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-06-03T04:24:32Z\",\"includesCreatedEdit\":false,\"isMinimized\":false,\"minimizedReason\":\"\",\"reactionGroups\":[],\"url\":\"https://github.com/kim-em/hex/issues/6450#issuecomment-4609050622\",\"viewerDidAuthor\":true},{\"id\":\"IC_kwDOSKC1W88AAAABErqMqQ\",\"author\":{\"login\":\"kim-em\"},\"authorAssociation\":\"OWNER\",\"body\":\"Claimed by session `a60e31d5-6ea7-4220-afd9-c74a1c1e1f8b` on branch `agent/a60e31d5`\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-06-03T04:51:41Z\",\"includesCreatedEdit\":false,\"isMinimized\":false,\"minimizedReason\":\"\",\"reactionGroups\":[],\"url\":\"https://github.com/kim-em/hex/issues/6450#issuecomment-4609182889\",\"viewerDidAuthor\":true},{\"id\":\"IC_kwDOSKC1W88AAAABErup-g\",\"author\":{\"login\":\"kim-em\"},\"authorAssociation\":\"OWNER\",\"body\":\"Decomposed into #6455, #6456\\n\\n(reason: deliverable (1) as stated is not provable without additional monic hypotheses on `core` and the selected lifted factors. transform := final.transform\n    pivotCols := ⟨final.pivots.toArray, by simp⟩ }\n\nprivate theorem rref_rank_le_n (M : Matrix R n m) : (rref M).rank ≤ n := by\n  unfold rref\n  change (rrefLoop 0 m { row := 0, echelon := M, transform := 1, pivots := [] }).pivots.length ≤ n\n  rw [← (rref_final_shape M).row_eq_length]\n  exact (rref_final_shape M).row_le_n\n\nprivate theorem rref_rank_le_m (M : hshape : RrefShapeInvariant (R := R) (n := n) (m := m) m final := by\n    simpa [final] using rref_final_shape M\n  change (⟨final.pivots.toArray, by simp⟩ : Vector (Fin m) final.pivots.length).get i satisfies the `IsRREF` contract. -/\ntheorem rref_isRREF (M : Matrix R n m) : IsRREF M (rref M) := by\n  refine\n    { toIsEchelonForm :=\n        { transform_mul := rref_transform_mul M\n          transform_inv := ?_\n          transform_right_inv := ?_\n          rank_le_n := rref_rank_le_n M\n          rank_le_m := rref_rank_le_m M\n          pivotCols_sorted := rref_pivotCols_sorted M\n          below_pivot_zero := ?_\n          zero_row := ?_ }\n      pivot_one := ?_\n      above_pivot_zero := ?_ }\n  all_goals sorry\n\nend FieldAlgorithms\n\nnamespace IsEchelonForm\n\nprivate theorem rowCombination_transform_transpose [Lean.Grind.CommRing R]\n    {M : Matrix R n m} {D : RowEchelonData R n m}\n    (E : IsEchelonForm M D) (e : Vector R n) :\n    rowCombination M (Matrix.transpose D.transform * e) =\n      rowCombination D.echelon e := by\n  unfold rowCombination\n  calc\n    Matrix.transpose M * (Matrix.transpose D.transform * e) =\n        (Matrix.transpose M * Matrix.transpose D.transform) * e := by\n          exact (Matrix.mul_assoc_vec (A := Matrix.transpose M)\n            (B := Matrix.transpose D.transform) (v := e)).symm\n    _ = Matrix.transpose (D.transform * M) * e := by\n          rw [← Matrix.transpose_mul_of_mul_comm Lean.Grind.CommSemiring.mul_comm]\n    _ = Matrix.transpose D.echelon * e := by\n          rw [E.transform_mul]\n\n/-- Converse row-combination transport: an `M`-row-combination witness `c`\nyields a `D.echelon`-row-combination witness `Matrix.transpose Tinv * c`,\nwhere `Tinv` is any left inverse of `D.transform`. The proof reuses the\nforward transport at the candidate witness. -/\nprivate theorem rowCombination_transformInv_transpose [Lean.Grind.CommRing R]\n    {M : Matrix R n m} {D : RowEchelonData R n m}\n    (E : IsEchelonForm M D) {Tinv : Matrix R n n}\n    (hTinv : Tinv * D.transform = 1) (c : Vector R n) :\n    rowCombination D.echelon (Matrix.transpose Tinv * c) = rowCombination M c := by\n  have hcompose :\n      Matrix.transpose D.transform * (Matrix.transpose Tinv * c) = c := by\n    calc\n      Matrix.transpose D.transform * (Matrix.transpose Tinv * c) =\n          (Matrix.transpose D.transform * Matrix.transpose Tinv) * c := by\n            exact (Matrix.mul_assoc_vec (A := Matrix.transpose D.transform)\n              (B := Matrix.transpose Tinv) (v := c)).symm\n      _ = Matrix.transpose (Tinv * D.transform) * c := by\n            rw [← Matrix.transpose_mul_of_mul_comm Lean.Grind.CommSemiring.mul_comm]\n      _ = Matrix.transpose (1 : Matrix R n n) * c := by\n            rw [hTinv]\n      _ = (1 : Matrix R n n) * c := by\n            rw [Matrix.transpose_one]\n      _ = c := Matrix.one_mulVec c\n  have hforward := E.rowCombination_transform_transpose (e := Matrix.transpose Tinv * c)\n  rw [hcompose] at hforward\n  exact hforward.symm\n\n/-- Existential converse transport: any `v` in the row span of `M` is also in\nthe row span of `D.echelon`, with an explicit witness produced from a left\ninverse of `D.transform`. -/\nprivate theorem exists_rowCombination_echelon_of_M [Lean.Grind.CommRing R]\n    {M : Matrix R n m} {D : RowEchelonData R n m}\n    (E : IsEchelonForm M D) {v : Vector R m}\n    (h : ∃ c : Vector R n, rowCombination M c = v) :\n    ∃ d : Vector R n, rowCombination D.echelon d = v := by\n  rcases h with ⟨c, hc⟩\n  rcases E.transform_inv with ⟨Tinv, hTinv⟩\n  refine ⟨Matrix.transpose Tinv * c, ?_⟩\n  rw [E.rowCombination_transformInv_transpose hTinv c, hc]\n\nvariable [Mul R] [Add R] [OfNat R 0] [OfNat R 1]\nvariable {M : Matrix R n m} {D : RowEchelonData R n m}\n\n/-- The echelon-side coefficients selected by pivot coordinates. -/\nprivate def echelonCoeffs [Lean.Grind.Field R] (E : IsEchelonForm M D)\n    (v : Vector R m) : Vector R n :=\n  Vector.ofFn fun i =>\n    if h : i.val < D.rank then\n      let pi : Fin D.rank := ⟨i.val, h⟩\n      v[D.pivotCols.get pi] /\n        D.echelon[(IsEchelonForm.pivotRow E pi)][D.pivotCols.get pi]\n    else\n      0\n\n/-- Coefficients for expressing `v` in the row span, if the echelon rows solve it. -/\ndef spanCoeffs [Lean.Grind.Field R] [DecidableEq R] (E : IsEchelonForm M D)\n    (v : Vector R m) : Option (Vector R n) :=\n  let coeffs := Matrix.transpose D.transform * E.echelonCoeffs v\n  if rowCombination M coeffs = v then\n    some coeffs\n  else\n    none\n\n/-- Decidable row-span membership test derived from `spanCoeffs`. -/\ndef spanContains [Lean.Grind.Field R] [DecidableEq R] (E : IsEchelonForm M D)\n    (v : Vector R m) : Bool :=\n  (E.spanCoeffs v).isSome\n\n/-- `spanCoeffs` returns coefficients whose row combination equals `v`. -/\ntheorem spanCoeffs_sound [Lean.Grind.Field R] [DecidableEq R]\n    (E : IsEchelonForm M D) (_hpiv : E.HasNonzeroPivots) (v : Vector R m)\n    (c : Vector R n) :\n    E.spanCoeffs v = some c → rowCombination M c = v := by\n  intro h\n  unfold spanCoeffs at h\n  dsimp only at h\n  split at h\n  · rename_i hspan\n    injection h with hc\n    subst c\n    exact hspan\n  · contradiction\n\n/-- If `spanContains` succeeds, the vector is in the row span. -/\ntheorem spanContains_sound [Lean.Grind.Field R] [DecidableEq R]\n    (E : IsEchelonForm M D) (hpiv : E.HasNonzeroPivots) (v : Vector R m) :\n    E.spanContains v = true → ∃ c : Vector R n, rowCombination M c = v := by\n  intro h\n  unfold spanContains at h\n  cases hCoeffs : E.spanCoeffs v with\n  | none =>\n      simp [hCoeffs] at h\n  | some c =>\n      exact ⟨c, E.spanCoeffs_sound hpiv v c hCoeffs⟩\n\nend IsEchelonForm\n\nnamespace IsRREF\n\n/-- RREF data has nonzero pivots because every pivot is normalized to one. -/\ntheorem hasNonzeroPivots [Lean.Grind.Field R]\n    {M : Matrix R n m} {D : RowEchelonData R n m} (E : IsRREF M D) :\n    E.toIsEchelonForm.HasNonzeroPivots := by\n  intro i\n  have hpivot :\n      D.echelon[E.toIsEchelonForm.pivotRow i][D.pivotCols.get i] = 1 := by\n    simpa [IsEchelonForm.pivotRow] using E.pivot_one i\n  intro hzero\n  exact (show (0 : R) ≠ 1 from Lean.Grind.Field.zero_ne_one) (hzero.symm.trans hpivot)\n\nvariable {M : Matrix R n m} {D : RowEchelonData R n m}\n\nprivate theorem foldl_add_eq_acc_ring {R : Type u} [Lean.Grind.Ring R]\n    {α : Type v} (xs : List α) (f : α → R) (acc : R)\n    (hf : ∀ x ∈ xs, f x = 0) :\n    xs.foldl (fun acc x => acc + f x) acc = acc := by\n  induction xs generalizing acc with\n  | nil =>\n      simp only [List.foldl_nil]\n  | cons x xs ih =>\n      simp only [List.foldl_cons]\n      have hx : f x = 0 := hf x (by simp)\n      have hxs : ∀ y ∈ xs, f y = 0 := fun y hy => hf y (List.mem_cons_of_mem _ hy)\n      rw [hx]\n      have hac : acc + (0 : R) = acc := by grind\n      rw [hac]\n      exact ih acc hxs\n\nprivate theorem foldl_sum_congr {R : Type u} [Add R]\n    {α : Type v} (xs : List α) (f g : α → R) (acc : R)\n    (h : ∀ x ∈ xs, f x = g x) :\n    xs.foldl (fun acc x => acc + f x) acc =\n      xs.foldl (fun acc x => acc + g x) acc := by\n  induction xs generalizing acc with\n  | nil =>\n      rfl\n  | cons x xs ih =>\n      simp only [List.foldl_cons]\n      have hx : f x = g x := h x (by simp)\n      have hxs : ∀ y ∈ xs, f y = g y := fun y hy => h y (List.mem_cons_of_mem _ hy)\n      rw [hx]\n      exact ih (acc + g x) hxs\n\nprivate theorem foldl_indicator_mul_unique {R : Type u} [Lean.Grind.Ring R]\n    {n : Nat} (xs : List (Fin n)) (i : Fin n) (f : Fin n → R)\n    (hi : i ∈ xs) (hnodup : xs.Nodup) (acc : R) :\n    xs.foldl (fun acc l => acc + (if i = l then (1 : R) else 0) * f l) acc =\n      acc + f i := by\n  induction xs generalizing acc with\n  | nil =>\n      exact absurd hi List.not_mem_nil\n  | cons x xs ih =>\n      simp only [List.foldl_cons]\n      rcases List.mem_cons.mp hi with hieq | hitail\n      · subst i\n        have hxs_zero :\n            ∀ y ∈ xs, (if x = y then (1 : R) else 0) * f y = 0 := by\n          intro y hy\n          have hxy : x ≠ y := fun heq => (List.nodup_cons.mp hnodup).1 (heq ▸ hy)\n          rw [if_neg hxy]\n          grind\n        rw [if_pos rfl]\n        rw [foldl_add_eq_acc_ring xs _ _ hxs_zero]\n        grind\n      · have hxi : i ≠ x := by\n          intro heq\n          rw [← heq] at hnodup\n          exact (List.nodup_cons.mp hnodup).1 hitail\n        rw [if_neg hxi]\n        have hzero : (0 : R) * f x = 0 := by grind\n        rw [hzero]\n        have hacc : acc + (0 : R) = acc := by grind\n        rw [hacc]\n        rw [ih hitail (List.nodup_cons.mp hnodup).2 acc]\n\nprivate theorem pivot_column_entry [Lean.Grind.Field R] (E : IsRREF M D)\n    (p : Fin D.rank) (i : Fin n) :\n    D.echelon[i][D.pivotCols.get p] =\n      if E.toIsEchelonForm.pivotRow p = i then 1 else 0 := by\n  by_cases hi : i.val < D.rank\n  · let q : Fin D.rank := ⟨i.val, hi⟩\n    by_cases hpq : p = q\n    · subst q\n      have hip : E.toIsEchelonForm.pivotRow p = i := by\n        apply Fin.ext\n        simpa [IsEchelonForm.pivotRow] using congrArg Fin.val hpq\n      rw [if_pos hip]\n      subst p\n      simpa [IsEchelonForm.pivotRow] using E.pivot_one ⟨i.val, hi⟩\n    · have hrow_ne : E.toIsEchelonForm.pivotRow p ≠ i := by\n        intro hrow\n        apply hpq\n        apply Fin.ext\n        simpa [IsEchelonForm.pivotRow] using congrArg Fin.val hrow\n      rw [if_neg hrow_ne]\n      have hne : i.val ≠ p.val := by\n        intro hval\n        apply hpq\n        apply Fin.ext\n        exact hval.symm\n      cases Nat.lt_or_gt_of_ne hne with\n      | inl hip =>\n          exact E.above_pivot_zero p i hip\n      | inr hpi =>\n          exact E.toIsEchelonForm.below_pivot_zero p i hpi\n  · have hrow_ne : E.toIsEchelonForm.pivotRow p ≠ i := by\n      intro hrow\n      apply hi\n      rw [← Fin.ext_iff.mp hrow]\n      exact p.isLt\n    rw [if_neg hrow_ne]\n    have hzero := E.toIsEchelonForm.zero_row i (by omega)\n    simpa using congrArg (fun row => row[D.pivotCols.get p]) hzero\n\nprivate theorem rowCombination_pivotCoeff [Lean.Grind.Field R] (E : IsRREF M D)\n    (c : Vector R n) (p : Fin D.rank) :\n    (rowCombination D.echelon c)[D.pivotCols.get p] =\n      c[E.toIsEchelonForm.pivotRow p] := by\n  unfold rowCombination\n  simp [HMul.hMul, Matrix.mulVec, Matrix.dot, Matrix.row, Hex.Vector.dotProduct,\n    Matrix.transpose, Matrix.col]\n  change (List.finRange n).foldl\n      (fun acc i => acc + D.echelon[i][D.pivotCols.get p] * c[i]) 0 =\n    c[E.toIsEchelonForm.pivotRow p]\n  calc\n    (List.finRange n).foldl\n        (fun acc i => acc + D.echelon[i][D.pivotCols.get p] * c[i]) 0 =\n        (List.finRange n).foldl\n          (fun acc i =>\n            acc + (if E.toIsEchelonForm.pivotRow p = i then (1 : R) else 0) * c[i]) 0 := by\n (List.finRange n) (E.toIsEchelonForm.pivotRow p)\n              (fun i => c[i]) (List.mem_finRange _) (List.nodup_finRange n) 0\n          have hzero : (0 : R) + c[E.toIsEchelonForm.pivotRow p] =\n {c d : Vector R n}\n    (hcoeff : ∀ i : Fin D.rank,\n      c[E.toIsEchelonForm.pivotRow i] = d[E.toIsEchelonForm.pivotRow i]) :\n    rowCombination D.echelon c = rowCombination D.echelon d := by\n After #3374 supplies positivity of the identity selected minor, the remaining proof layer is to show the Cauchy-Binet fold is strictly positive because it contains that positive square and every integer square term is nonnegative.\n\ndepends-on: #3374\nParent: #3184\n\n## Deliverables\n\n1. Prove that `firstColumns k n hk` is in `selectedColumnTuples k n`, and that the corresponding `columnTupleMatrix` for `leadingRows M k hk` is `Matrix.leadingPrefix M k hk`.\n3. Prove a small finite-list lemma showing a fold of integer squares is strictly positive when one listed term has positive square and all other terms are squares.\n4. Combine these with `det_gramMatrix_eq_sum_minors_sq` and #3374 to prove a reusable leading-prefix Gram positivity theorem for upper-triangular positive-diagonal matrices. Suggested shape:\n\n```lean\ntheorem det_gramMatrix_leadingRows_pos_of_upperTriangular_pos_diag\n    (M : Matrix Int n n)\n    (hzero : forall i j, j.val < i.val -> M[i][j] = 0)\n    (hdiag : forall i, 0 < M[i][i])\n    (k : Nat) (hk : k <= n) :\n    0 < Matrix.det (Matrix.gramMatrix (leadingRows M k hk))\n```\n\nEquivalent statement is fine if the final #3184 wrapper can use it directly.\n5. No new `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, `FIXME`, or theorem-level `sorry`.\n\n## Context\n\nRead #3184, #3374, #3238, `HexMatrix/Basic.lean` around `gramMatrix`/`leadingPrefix`/`submatrix`, and `HexMatrix/Determinant.lean` around `selectedColumnTuples`, `columnTupleMatrix`, and `det_gramMatrix_eq_sum_minors_sq`.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexMatrix`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- `git diff --check`\n- Banned-marker scan of touched files shows no new markers.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Proving upper-triangular determinant positivity (#3374).\n- The final `Matrix.independent` wrapper.\n- BHKS-specific wiring.\n\n\n### #3374 HexMatrix: prove determinant positivity for upper-triangular Int matrices\n## Current state\n\nParent #3184 needs `independent_of_upperTriangular_pos_diag` for square integer matrices. Worker investigation showed the final Gram/Cauchy-Binet proof needs a separate determinant fact for the selected identity minor: every leading upper-triangular prefix with positive diagonal has positive determinant.\n\nParent: #3184\n\n## Deliverables\n\n1. Prove any needed determinant recursion helper, such as a last-row expansion for matrices whose last row is zero before the diagonal.\n3. Keep executable matrix/determinant definitions unchanged.\n4. HO-3 remains separate.\n- Proving factorization correctness theorems or BHKS termination.\n- Changing executable factorization behavior.\n- Editing `SPEC/`, top-level `PLAN.md`, or top-level `AGENTS.md`.\n\n\n### #3369 HexPolyFp: discharge derivative-active Yun state provider\n## Current state\n\nOriginal issue #3307 was partially completed by PR #3365. Use the provider to remove the explicit `hstate` parameter and expose a derivative-active specialization with the original #3307 shape:\n\n```lean\nprivate theorem yunFactorsLevelCompletes_of_derivative_active\n    (hp : Hex.Nat.Prime p) (f : FpPoly p) (multiplicity fuel : Nat)\n    (hmultiplicity : 0 < multiplicity) (hfuel : f.size < fuel + 1)\n    (hzero : f.isZero = false)\n    (hdf : (DensePoly.derivative f).isZero = false) :\n    let g := DensePoly.gcd f (DensePoly.derivative f)\n    let c := f / g\n    yunFactorsLevelCompletes c g multiplicity 1 fuel\n```\n\n3. Do not add `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, `FIXME`, or theorem-level `sorry`.\n\n## Context\n\nRead #3316, #3345, #3350, #3351, and `HexPoly/Euclid.lean` around:\n\n- `exists_last_not_natCast_dvd_coeff`\n- the finite-grid McCoy descent helper from #3351\n- `nat_dvd_of_scalar_mul_primitive_coeff_dvd`\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexPoly.Euclid`\n- `lake build HexPoly`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- `git diff --check`\n- `rg -n '^axiom\\\\b|native_decide|TODO|FIXME|sorry' HexPoly/Euclid.lean`\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Proving the abstract finite-grid descent itself; that is #3350.\n- Instantiating the finite-grid descent for polynomial coefficient products; that is #3351.\n- The primitive-product coefficient divisibility wrapper (#3252), which should remain blocked until this theorem lands.\n- Changing executable polynomial definitions.\ndepends-on: #3351\n\n\n### #3328 HexBZ: enforce p >= 3 in good-prime selection\n## Current state\n\nThe current `HexBerlekampZassenhaus` SPEC says admissible modular primes must\navoid `p = 2`:\n\n> `isGoodPrime` expresses the mathematical admissibility condition for the\n> modular reduction prime: at minimum `p ∤ lc(f)`, `p ≥ 3`, and `f mod p` is\n> square-free.\n\nand the BHKS pitfalls section repeats:\n\n> Avoid `p = 2`. KP Lemma 2.6 needs a separate parity argument. Pick the\n> smallest admissible prime ≥ 3.\n\nThe implementation still admits and selects `2`:\n\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean` defines `isGoodPrime` with `p > 1`.\n- `smallPrimeCandidates` starts with `{ p := 2, ... }`.\n- `choosePrime` and `fallbackPrimeChoiceData` fall back to `2`.\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Conformance.lean` has guards expecting\n  `choosePrime squareFreeTypical = 2`, `isGoodPrime squareFreeTypical 2`,\n  and `choosePrimeData repeatedRootPoly` to use `p = 2`.\n\nThis is a narrow support issue for HO-1 (#2564): it aligns prime selection\nwith the post-HO-1 BHKS/CLD contract without changing the recombination\nalgorithm itself.\n\n## Deliverables\n\n1. Update the proof-facing API around good-prime selection as needed, for\n   example by exposing a small theorem that `isGoodPrime f p = true` implies\n   `3 ≤ p`, alongside the existing leading-coefficient and square-free\n   projections.\n3. Update `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Conformance.lean` guards to expect the new\n   smallest admissible prime behavior, and add a direct guard that `p = 2` is\n   rejected for a polynomial where the old implementation accepted it.\n\n## Library placement\n\n- Primary file: `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`.\n- Conformance file: `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Conformance.lean`.\n- SPEC quote from `SPEC/Libraries/hex-berlekamp-zassenhaus.md`: \"`isGoodPrime`\n  expresses the mathematical admissibility condition for the modular reduction\n  prime: at minimum `p ∤ lc(f)`, `p ≥ 3` (avoid `p = 2`; see Pitfall 6), and\n  `f mod p` is square-free.\"\n- Q1: this file owns the prime-selection sub-API named by the\n  `HexBerlekampZassenhaus` SPEC.\n- Q2: no Mathlib; this is executable BZ pipeline and Lean-only conformance.\n- Q3: upstream Mathlib does not contain this project-specific `ZPoly`\n  prime-selection API.\n- Q4: no missing infrastructure is expected; this is a local predicate,\n  candidate-list, fallback, and guard update.\n\n## Context\n\nRead:\n\n- Human-oversight issue #2564, especially the BHKS/CLD pitfalls.\n- `SPEC/Libraries/hex-berlekamp-zassenhaus.md`, prime selection and Pitfall 6.\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean` around `isGoodPrime`,\n  `smallPrimeCandidates`, `choosePrimeScore?`, `choosePrime`,\n  `choosePrimeData?`, and `fallbackPrimeChoiceData`.\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Conformance.lean` around the good-prime and\n  `choosePrimeData` guards.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus.Conformance`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- `git diff --check`\n- `rg -n '^axiom\\\\b|native_decide|TODO|FIXME|sorry' HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Conformance.lean`\n  shows no new banned markers beyond pre-existing tracked proof holes.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Rewriting the BHKS lattice, recovery, or precision-doubling algorithm.\n- Proving HO-1 bridge obligations or HO-4 termination.\n- Adding new adversarial JSONL fixtures; HO-2 fixture completion is already\n  tracked separately.\n- Benchmark work for HO-3.\n- Editing `SPEC/`, top-level `PLAN.md`, or top-level `AGENTS.md`.\n\n\n### #3308 HexPolyFp: prove reachable Yun quotient residual derivative invariant\n## Current state\n\nIssue #3279 was partially advanced by open PR #3309, which adds two compiling private step-transfer lemmas in `HexPolyFp/SquareFree.lean`:\n\n```lean\nprivate theorem yunStep_tail_derivative_isZero_of_derivative_isZero_of_common_dvd_one\n    [ZMod64.PrimeModulus p]\n    (c w : FpPoly p)\n    (hder : (DensePoly.derivative w).isZero = true)\n    (hcommon :\n      ∀ d : FpPoly p,\n        d ∣ DensePoly.gcd c w →\n          d ∣ w / DensePoly.gcd c w →\n            d ∣ (1 : FpPoly p)) :\n    (DensePoly.derivative (w / DensePoly.gcd c w)).isZero = true\n\nprivate theorem yunStep_tail_derivative_isZero_of_source_common_dvd_one\n    [ZMod64.PrimeModulus p]\n    (c w : FpPoly p)\n    (hder : (DensePoly.derivative w).isZero = true)\n    (hcommon :\n      ∀ d : FpPoly p, d ∣ c → d ∣ w → d ∣ (1 : FpPoly p)) :\n    (DensePoly.derivative (w / DensePoly.gcd c w)).isZero = true\n```\n\nThese package the #3280 cancellation helper and `yunStep_tail_common_dvd_one_of_common_dvd_one` for the Yun transition quotient. Workers should start this issue after #3309 is merged or otherwise available on their base branch.\n\nParent: #3279\ndepends-on: #3309\n\n## Deliverables\n\n1. In `HexPolyFp/SquareFree.lean`, prove the remaining Frobenius-stability invariant for reachable Yun states. A target shape is still:\n\n```lean\nprivate theorem yunFactorsPairwiseReachable_coprime_residual_derivative_zero\n    [ZMod64.PrimeModulus p]\n    (c w : FpPoly p) (fuel : Nat)\n    (hreachable : yunFactorsPairwiseReachable c w fuel)\n    (hcoprime : ∀ d : FpPoly p, d ∣ c → d ∣ w → d ∣ (1 : FpPoly p)) :\n    isOne w = false → (DensePoly.derivative w).isZero = true\n```\n\nEquivalent strengthened invariant shapes are fine if they directly feed the derivative-active provider for `yunFactorsContributionResidualComplete`.\n2. Use `derivativeSplit_residual_derivative_zero_of_coprime` for the base derivative split.\n3. Use the `yunStep_tail_derivative_isZero_*` lemmas from #3309 for the transition once the source residual derivative-zero fact is available.\n4. No executable changes and no new `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, `FIXME`, or theorem-level `sorry`.\n\n## Context\n\nRead `HexPolyFp/SquareFree.lean` around:\n\n- `right_factor_derivative_isZero_of_mul_derivative_isZero_of_common_dvd_one`\n- `derivativeSplit_residual_derivative_zero_of_coprime`\n- `yunStep_tail_common_dvd_one_of_common_dvd_one`\n- `yunStep_tail_derivative_isZero_of_derivative_isZero_of_common_dvd_one`\n- `yunStep_tail_derivative_isZero_of_source_common_dvd_one`\n- `yunFactorsPairwiseReachable_current_squarefree`\n- `yunFactorsPairwiseReachable_common_dvd_one`\n- `yunFactorsContributionResidualComplete`\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexPolyFp.SquareFree`\n- `lake build HexPolyFp`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- `git diff --check`\n- `rg -n '^axiom\\\\b|native_decide|TODO|FIXME|sorry' HexPolyFp/SquareFree.lean` shows no new banned markers beyond the pre-existing tracked `squareFreeAuxRevContribution_derivative_active_pow_obligation` hole.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Reworking executable Yun/square-free functions.\n- Scaled-loop composition.\n- Editing `SPEC/`, top-level `PLAN.md`, or top-level `AGENTS.md`.\n\n\n### #3304 HexBZ core: prove Mathlib-free ZPoly irreducibility checker iff\n## Current state\n\nHuman-oversight issue #2637 introduced the corrected\n`Hex.Factorization` public API and explicitly requires the\nMathlib-free `Hex.ZPoly.Irreducible` class, boolean checker, and\ndecidable instance to be backed by Lean proofs.\n\nThe executable class and checker are present in\n`HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`:\n\n```lean\nclass ZPoly.Irreducible (f : ZPoly) : Prop where ...\ndef ZPoly.isIrreducible (f : ZPoly) : Bool := ...\ntheorem ZPoly.isIrreducible_iff (f : ZPoly) :\n    isIrreducible f = true ↔ Irreducible f := by\n  sorry\n```\n\nThe proof currently depends on the factorization product and factor-entry\ninvariants being available; it should not be attempted by unfolding the\nfactorization pipeline from scratch.\n\ndepends-on: #3087\ndepends-on: #2803\nSupports: #2637\n\n## Deliverables\n\n1. In `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`, prove\n   `Hex.ZPoly.isIrreducible_iff` without changing the public\n   `Irreducible`, `isIrreducible`, `factor`, or `Factorization` APIs.\n2. Use the landed product theorem from #3087 and the executable\n   factor-entry irreducibility invariant from #2803; do not duplicate\n   those proofs locally.\n3. Keep the constant-polynomial branch local and Mathlib-free, using\n   the existing `isNatPrime`/unit facts already in the file or minimal\n   nearby helper lemmas if needed.\n\n## Library placement\n\n- Primary file: `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`.\n- SPEC quote from `SPEC/Libraries/hex-berlekamp-zassenhaus.md`:\n  \"The polynomial-case predicate uses the `Factorization` projections\n  directly: scalar must be a unit (i.e., `f` is primitive up to sign),\n  `factors` has exactly one entry, and that entry has multiplicity 1.\"\n- Q1: this is the Mathlib-free checker theorem for the public\n  `Hex.ZPoly.Irreducible` class required by the BZ factorization API.\n- Q2: no Mathlib; this theorem lives in the executable BZ library.\n- Q3: no upstream Mathlib theorem states this project-specific checker\n  over `Hex.ZPoly` and `Hex.Factorization`.\n- Q4: depends on #3087 for product preservation and #2803 for the\n  factor-entry irreducibility invariant.\n\n## Context\n\nRead:\n\n- Human-oversight issue #2637.\n- `SPEC/Libraries/hex-berlekamp-zassenhaus.md`, especially the\n  `Factorization` record and `Hex.ZPoly.Irreducible` sections.\n- `PLAN/Phase5.md` and `PLAN/Conventions.md`.\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean` around `Factorization.product`,\n  `factor_product_of_bound`, `ZPoly.Irreducible`, `ZPoly.isIrreducible`,\n  and `instDecidableIrreducible`.\n- The executable invariants delivered by #3087 and #2803.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus.Basic`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- `git diff --check`\n- `rg -n '^axiom\\b|native_decide|TODO|FIXME|sorry' HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`\n  shows no new banned markers and the `isIrreducible_iff` hole is gone.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Proving `factor_product_of_bound`; that is #3087.\n- Proving Mathlib-facing `irreducibleByFactorization_iff` or\n  `factor_unique`; those remain in #2833.\n- Changing executable factorization behavior or the public API.\n- Editing `SPEC/`, top-level `PLAN.md`, or top-level `AGENTS.md`.\n\n\n### #3284 HexPolyFp: assemble unconditional scaled Yun pow invariant\n## Current state\n\nParent #3220 decomposes into local termination lemmas (#3282), the derivative-active completion theorem, and this final assembly layer.\n\nThe conditional algebraic theorem already exists:\n\n```lean\nprivate theorem yunFactorsContributionWithLevel_pow_invariant_of_completes\n    [ZMod64.PrimeModulus p] (c w : FpPoly p) (base level fuel : Nat)\n    (hcompletes : yunFactorsLevelCompletes c w base level fuel) :\n    let contribution := yunFactorsContributionWithLevel c w base level fuel\n    contribution.1 * pow contribution.2 base =\n      pow c (base * level) * pow w base\n```\n\nIssue #3307 was partially completed by open PR #3365 and the remaining derivative-active completion work is tracked by #3369. This final assembly should run after #3365 lands and #3369 provides `yunFactorsLevelCompletes_of_derivative_active` without the explicit state-provider hypothesis.\n\ndepends-on: #3369\nParent: #3220\n\n## Deliverables\n\n1. In `HexPolyFp/SquareFree.lean`, prove the unconditional derivative-active scaled Yun invariant:\n\n```lean\nprivate theorem yunFactorsContributionWithLevel_pow_invariant\n    [ZMod64.PrimeModulus p]\n    (hp : Hex.Nat.Prime p) (f : FpPoly p) (multiplicity fuel : Nat)\n    (hmultiplicity : 0 < multiplicity) (hfuel : f.size < fuel + 1)\n    (hzero : f.isZero = false)\n    (hdf : (DensePoly.derivative f).isZero = false) :\n    let g := DensePoly.gcd f (DensePoly.derivative f)\n    let c := f / g\n    let contribution := yunFactorsContributionWithLevel c g multiplicity 1 fuel\n    contribution.1 * pow contribution.2 multiplicity = pow f multiplicity\n```\n\n2. Compose `yunFactorsLevelCompletes_of_derivative_active` from #3369 with `yunFactorsContributionWithLevel_pow_invariant_of_completes`, then use `div_gcd_mul_reconstruct` and `pow_mul_base`/existing pow lemmas to rewrite `pow (f / gcd f f') multiplicity * pow (gcd f f') multiplicity` to `pow f multiplicity`.\n3. Keep this issue focused on the theorem above and any tiny algebraic rewrite helper needed for the final `pow` step.\n4. No executable definition changes. No new `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, `FIXME`, or theorem-level `sorry`.\n\n## Context\n\nRead:\n\n- #3220 for parent scope.\n- #3369 for the remaining derivative-active completion theorem.\n- PR #3365 for the partial state-provider specialization currently not on `main`.\n- `HexPolyFp/SquareFree.lean` around `yunFactorsContributionWithLevel_pow_invariant_of_completes`, `div_gcd_mul_reconstruct`, `pow_mul_base`, and the existing derivative-active product-reconstruction cluster.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexPolyFp.SquareFree`\n- `lake build HexPolyFp`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- `git diff --check`\n- `rg -n '^axiom\\b|native_decide|TODO|FIXME|sorry' HexPolyFp/SquareFree.lean` shows no new banned markers beyond the pre-existing tracked `squareFreeAuxRevContribution_derivative_active_pow_obligation` hole.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Proving loop termination/completion; that is #3369 and its prerequisites.\n- Closing the outer `squareFreeAuxRevContribution_derivative_active_pow_obligation` directly.\n- Scaled residual derivative-zero or pairwise-coprime work.\n- Editing `SPEC/`, top-level `PLAN.md`, or top-level `AGENTS.md`.\n\n\n### #3252 HexPoly: assemble primitive product coefficient divisibility from McCoy helper\n## Current state\n\nParent #3169 asks for the reusable theorem:\n\n```lean\ntheorem coeff_dvd_of_primitive_mul_coeff_dvd\n    (p q : DensePoly Int) (d : Nat)\n    (hp : content p = 1)\n    (hprod : forall n, (d : Int) ∣ (p * q).coeff n) :\n    forall n, (d : Int) ∣ q.coeff n\n```\n\nThe scalar annihilator prerequisite #3168 is closed and available as `nat_dvd_of_scalar_mul_primitive_coeff_dvd`.\n\nThe McCoy annihilator prerequisite was decomposed several times. #3316 is closed in favor of #3345 and #3346; #3345 was then decomposed into #3350 and #3351 for the finite-grid descent. This issue remains blocked until #3346 lands the public theorem `exists_scalar_annihilator_of_mul_coeff_dvd_of_exists_not_dvd_coeff`.\n\nParent: #3169\ndepends-on: #3346\n\n## Deliverables\n\n1. In `HexPoly/Euclid.lean`, prove the public coefficient-divisibility theorem with a shape equivalent to:\n\n```lean\ntheorem coeff_dvd_of_primitive_mul_coeff_dvd\n    (p q : DensePoly Int) (d : Nat)\n    (hp : content p = 1)\n    (hprod : forall n, (d : Int) ∣ (p * q).coeff n) :\n    forall n, (d : Int) ∣ q.coeff n\n```\n\n2. Prove it by contradiction using the McCoy annihilator helper from #3346 and the existing scalar annihilator `nat_dvd_of_scalar_mul_primitive_coeff_dvd`.\n3. Do not change executable polynomial definitions and do not add `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, `FIXME`, or theorem-level `sorry`.\n\n## Context\n\nRead #3169, #3346, #3351, #3350, #3168, #3133, #3126, PR #3134, `SPEC/Libraries/hex-poly-z.md`, `PLAN/Phase5.md`, and `PLAN/Conventions.md`.\n\nRelevant APIs in `HexPoly/Euclid.lean`:\n\n- `nat_dvd_of_scalar_mul_primitive_coeff_dvd`\n- the McCoy annihilator helper from #3346\n- the finite-grid McCoy descent helper from #3351\n- `content`, `content_dvd_coeff`, and `nat_eq_one_of_content_eq_one_of_nat_dvd_coeff`\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexPoly`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- `git diff --check`\n- `rg -n '^axiom\\\\b|native_decide|TODO|FIXME|sorry' HexPoly/Euclid.lean` shows no new banned markers or proof holes.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Proving the McCoy annihilator helper itself; that is #3346.\n- Adding `ZPoly.primitive_mul`; that remains #3127.\n- Full Gauss theorem `content (p * q) = content p * content q`.\n- Changing executable polynomial definitions.\n\n### #3239 HexPolyFp: wire derivative-active Yun residual provider from reachable invariant\n## Current state\n\nParent #3200 asks for the full derivative-active Yun residual derivative-zero chain. sessions/1822438d-c697-47e2-b453-ebe16da351f4.stdout:66:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_33","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '170,250p' HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/TerminationBound.lean\"","aggregated_output":"\nnamespace BHKS\n\n/--\nProof-facing hypotheses for the executable-cap BHKS separation step.\n\nThe `bad_setup_of_projected_not_indicator` field is the remaining bridge from\na projected lattice vector outside the true-factor indicator lattice to the\nbad-vector setup consumed by the resultant contradiction.  This structure lets\nthe cap/resultant layer expose the final `L' = W` theorem without depending on\nthe later failed-recovery construction.\n-/\nstructure ExecutableCapSeparationHypotheses\n    (W : ExecutableBadVectorWitness)\n    (trueSupports : Set (Set (Fin W.projectedRows.factorCount))) where\n  cut : CutProjectionHypotheses W.projectedRows trueSupports\n  bad_setup_of_projected_not_indicator :\n    ∀ v : Fin W.projectedRows.factorCount → ℤ,\n      v ∈ projectedRowSpanInt W.projectedRows →\n        v ∉ trueFactorIndicatorLattice trueSupports →\n          ExecutableBadVectorWitness.IsBhksBadVectorSetup W\n  hp : 0 < W.liftData.p\n  l2norm_upper_lt_divisor :\n    (HexPolyZMathlib.l2norm W.inputPolynomial) ^\n        W.auxiliaryPolynomial.natDegree *\n      (HexPolyZMathlib.l2norm W.auxiliaryPolynomial) ^\n        W.inputPolynomial.natDegree <\n    (W.liftData.p ^ (W.liftData.k * W.localFactorDegree) : ℝ)\n\n/--\nAt any executable fast-path precision cap, the cap-level bad-vector\ncontradiction excludes every vector in `L' \\ W`.\n-/\ntheorem no_projected_not_indicator_of_factorFastPrecisionCap_le\n    (W : ExecutableBadVectorWitness)\n    (trueSupports : Set (Set (Fin W.projectedRows.factorCount)))\n    {a : Nat} (ha : Hex.factorFastPrecisionCap W.input ≤ a)\n    (C : ℝ) (hC_nonneg : 0 ≤ C) (hC : C ≤ 2)\n    (hcap : ExecutableCapSeparationHypotheses W trueSupports) :\n    ∀ v : Fin W.projectedRows.factorCount → ℤ,\n      v ∈ projectedRowSpanInt W.projectedRows →\n        v ∉ trueFactorIndicatorLattice trueSupports →\n          False := by\n  intro v hv hnot\n  let h_bad := hcap.bad_setup_of_projected_not_indicator v hv hnot\n  exact\n    ExecutableBadVectorWitness.no_bhks_bad_setup_of_factorFastPrecisionCap_le\n      W ha C hC_nonneg hC h_bad hcap.hp\n      hcap.l2norm_upper_lt_divisor\n\n/--\nExecutable-cap BHKS separation: at any precision meeting\n`factorFastPrecisionCap`, the projected row span equals the true-factor\nindicator lattice, assuming the remaining failed-recovery-to-bad-vector bridge.\n-/\ntheorem projectedRowSpan_eq_trueFactorIndicatorLattice_of_cap\n    (W : ExecutableBadVectorWitness)\n    (trueSupports : Set (Set (Fin W.projectedRows.factorCount)))\n    {a : Nat} (ha : Hex.factorFastPrecisionCap W.input ≤ a)\n    (C : ℝ) (hC_nonneg : 0 ≤ C) (hC : C ≤ 2)\n    (hcap : ExecutableCapSeparationHypotheses W trueSupports) :\n    projectedRowSpanInt W.projectedRows =\n      trueFactorIndicatorLattice trueSupports := by\n  exact projectedRowSpan_eq_trueFactorIndicatorLattice\n    W.projectedRows trueSupports\n    { cut := hcap.cut\n      no_projected_not_indicator :=\n        no_projected_not_indicator_of_factorFastPrecisionCap_le\n          W trueSupports ha C hC_nonneg hC hcap }\n\nend BHKS\n\nend HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/1822438d-c697-47e2-b453-ebe16da351f4.stdout:84:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_42","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '68,230p' HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean\"","aggregated_output":"Proof-facing hypotheses for the BHKS Lemma 3.3 / B7 step at fixed precision.\n\n`SeparationHypotheses` (or the cap-specialised\n`ExecutableCapSeparationHypotheses` from `TerminationBound.lean`) provides\n`L' = W`.  The `indicators_match` field is the abstract conclusion of BHKS\nLemma 3.3 connecting the executable rref + signature partition to a target\nindicator list; this is the residual obligation B7 in the SPEC, which\ndepends on still-open executable RREF correctness and is intentionally left\nabstract here.\n-/\nstructure EquivalenceClassRecoveryHypotheses\n    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (d : Hex.LiftData) where\n  /-- Positive lattice dimension so the projected rows are well-defined. -/\n  rows_pos : HasPositiveDimension f d\n  /-- True-factor supports backing the indicator lattice `W`. -/\n  trueSupports :\n    Set (Set (Fin (projectedRowsOfLiftData f d rows_pos).factorCount))\n  /-- The BHKS `L' = W` conclusion specialised to this lift data. -/\n  lattice_eq_indicators :\n    BHKS.projectedRowSpanInt (projectedRowsOfLiftData f d rows_pos) =\n      BHKS.trueFactorIndicatorLattice trueSupports\n  /-- BHKS Lemma 3.3 conclusion: the executable equivalence-class indicators\n      match a target list determined by the true-factor supports. -/\n  expectedIndicators : Array (Array Int)\n  indicators_match :\n    equivalenceClassIndicatorsOfLiftData f d rows_pos = expectedIndicators\n\n/-- Under the equivalence-class recovery hypotheses, the executable\n`bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators` output is exactly the expected indicator\nlist. This is the BHKS Lemma 3.3 / B7 bridge from `L' = W` to the executable\nindicator surface. -/\ntheorem bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators_eq_of_recovery\n    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (d : Hex.LiftData)\n    (h : EquivalenceClassRecoveryHypotheses f d) :\n    equivalenceClassIndicatorsOfLiftData f d h.rows_pos = h.expectedIndicators :=\n  h.indicators_match\n\n/-- BHKS Lemma 3.3 conclusion exposed at the raw projected rows, for callers\nthat already have the projected-rows value in hand. -/\ntheorem bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators_projectedRows_eq_of_recovery\n    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (d : Hex.LiftData)\n    (h : EquivalenceClassRecoveryHypotheses f d) :\n    Hex.bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators\n        (projectedRowsOfLiftData f d h.rows_pos) = h.expectedIndicators :=\n  h.indicators_match\n\n/--\nProof-facing hypotheses for forward BHKS recovery at fixed precision.\n\nThe fields encode the four guards inside the executable `Hex.bhksRecover?`:\npositive lattice dimension, non-degenerate equivalence-class partition,\nsuccessful per-indicator integer reconstruction, and exact product check.\nBy the SPEC Group D forward-verification argument, these guards hold\nsimultaneously when the precision dominates Mignotte and `L' = W` holds.\nThe discharge of each individual field from the abstract precision /\nseparation hypotheses is left to later bridge work; this record packages\nexactly what the executable function needs to return `some`.\n-/\nstructure RecoveryHypotheses (f : Hex.ZPoly) (d : Hex.LiftData) where\n  /-- Positive lattice dimension so the recovery enters the recovery branch. -/\n  rows_pos : HasPositiveDimension f d\n  /-- The equivalence-class partition is non-degenerate, so the executable\n      function does not bail out via `bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition`. -/\n  nondegenerate :\n    Hex.bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition\n        (projectedRowsOfLiftData f d rows_pos)\n        (equivalenceClassIndicatorsOfLiftData f d rows_pos) = false\n  /-- The verified factor list returned by the executable recovery. -/\n  expectedFactors : Array Hex.ZPoly\n  /-- Per-indicator centred-residue reconstruction succeeds and divides `f`. -/\n  candidates_eq :\n    Hex.bhksIndicatorCandidates? f d\n        (equivalenceClassIndicatorsOfLiftData f d rows_pos) =\n      some expectedFactors\n  /-- The reconstructed factors multiply back to `f`, so the final product\n      check inside `Hex.bhksRecover?` succeeds. -/\n  product_eq : Array.polyProduct expectedFactors = f\n\n/--\nForward-verification theorem (SPEC Group D, scoped to one precision/recovery\ncall): under the executable-cap recovery hypotheses, `Hex.bhksRecover? f d`\nreturns `some <expected factors>`.\n\nThis composes with `BHKS.projectedRowSpan_eq_trueFactorIndicatorLattice_of_cap`\nfrom `TerminationBound.lean` (the executable-cap `L' = W` theorem closed in\nissue #3034): when the recovery hypotheses are constructed at any precision\nthat meets `Hex.factorFastPrecisionCap f`, the executable `bhksRecover?` call\ninside `Hex.factorFastCoreWithBound` returns `some _`, so that loop iteration\nexits via the success branch rather than the precision-doubling fallback.\n-/\ntheorem bhksRecover_eq_some_of_recovery\n    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (d : Hex.LiftData) (h : RecoveryHypotheses f d) :\n    Hex.bhksRecover? f d = some h.expectedFactors := by\n  have hrows : 1 ≤ (Hex.bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors).factorCount +\n      (Hex.bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors).coeffWidth := h.rows_pos\n  have hnondeg :\n      Hex.bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition\n          (Hex.bhksProjectedRows (Hex.bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors)\n            hrows (Hex.bhksLatticeBasis_independent _))\n          (Hex.bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators\n            (Hex.bhksProjectedRows\n              (Hex.bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors)\n              hrows (Hex.bhksLatticeBasis_independent _))) = false :=\n    h.nondegenerate\n  have hcand :\n      Hex.bhksIndicatorCandidates? f d\n          (Hex.bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators\n            (Hex.bhksProjectedRows\n              (Hex.bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors)\n              hrows (Hex.bhksLatticeBasis_independent _))) =\n        some h.expectedFactors :=\n    h.candidates_eq\n  have hprod := h.product_eq\n  unfold Hex.bhksRecover?\n  simp only [dif_pos hrows, hnondeg, hcand, Bool.false_eq_true, if_false, hprod,\n    BEq.refl, if_true]\n\n/-- Corollary form of `bhksRecover_eq_some_of_recovery` that drops the\nexpected-factors witness from the conclusion: under the recovery hypotheses,\n`Hex.bhksRecover? f d` is in the `some _` branch. -/\ntheorem bhksRecover_isSome_of_recovery\n    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (d : Hex.LiftData) (h : RecoveryHypotheses f d) :\n    (Hex.bhksRecover? f d).isSome := by\n  rw [bhksRecover_eq_some_of_recovery f d h]\n  rfl\n\n/--\nProof-facing inputs for the SPEC Group D forward-verification clause at one\nprecision/recovery call: `L' = W` (deliverable 1, supplied by issue #3034 at\nthe executable cap) plus the residual abstract obligations B7 (BHKS\nLemma 3.3 indicator identification) and A2 (Mignotte centred-residue\nreconstruction) that this layer treats as hypotheses.\n\nThe Mignotte precision side condition is the SPEC's\n`p^k > 2 · defaultFactorCoeffBound f`; the Group D obligation establishes\nthis whenever `Hex.factorFastPrecisionCap f ≤ k`, since the cap dominates\nthe Mignotte coefficient bound.  The two abstract obligations\n(`indicators_match`, `candidates_eq`) wrap the per-step BHKS Lemma 3.3 / A2\ncontent and are the natural follow-up tasks for later bridge work.\n-/\nstructure ForwardRecoveryInputs (f : Hex.ZPoly) (d : Hex.LiftData) where\n  /-- Positive lattice dimension so the projected rows / recovery branch make\n      sense. -/\n  rows_pos : HasPositiveDimension f d\n  /-- True-factor supports backing the indicator lattice `W`. -/\n  trueSupports :\n    Set (Set (Fin (projectedRowsOfLiftData f d rows_pos).factorCount))\n  /-- BHKS `L' = W` at this lift data, supplied by #3034's\n      `BHKS.projectedRowSpan_eq_trueFactorIndicatorLattice_of_cap`. -/\n  lattice_eq_indicators :\n    BHKS.projectedRowSpanInt (projectedRowsOfLiftData f d rows_pos) =\n      BHKS.trueFactorIndicatorLattice trueSupports\n  /-- Mignotte-precision side condition: the modular precision exceeds twice\n      the executable Mignotte coefficient bound, so the centred-residue lift\n      is unique. -/\n  mignotte_precision :\n    2 * Hex.ZPoly.defaultFactorCoeffBound f < d.p ^ d.k\n  /-- BHKS Lemma 3.3 / B7 conclusion, left abstract: the executable\n      equivalence-class indicators agree with a target indicator list\n      `expectedIndicators`. -/\n  expectedIndicators : Array (Array Int)\n  indicators_match :\n    equivalenceClassIndicatorsOfLiftData f d rows_pos = expectedIndicators\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/1822438d-c697-47e2-b453-ebe16da351f4.stdout:88:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_44","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '244,315p' HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean\"","aggregated_output":"namespace ForwardRecoveryInputs\n\n/-- Promote a SPEC-input bundle to the immediate recovery hypotheses\nconsumed by `bhksRecover_eq_some_of_recovery`.  The promotion is a\nfield-by-field repackaging that uses `indicators_match` to substitute\n`expectedIndicators` for the executable equivalence-class output in the\nnon-degenerate and candidate fields. -/\ndef toRecoveryHypotheses {f : Hex.ZPoly} {d : Hex.LiftData}\n    (h : ForwardRecoveryInputs f d) : RecoveryHypotheses f d where\n  rows_pos := h.rows_pos\n  nondegenerate := by\n    have hindicators := h.indicators_match\n    show\n      Hex.bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition\n          (projectedRowsOfLiftData f d h.rows_pos)\n          (equivalenceClassIndicatorsOfLiftData f d h.rows_pos) = false\n    rw [hindicators]\n    exact h.nondegenerate\n  expectedFactors := h.expectedFactors\n  candidates_eq := by\n    have hindicators := h.indicators_match\n    show\n      Hex.bhksIndicatorCandidates? f d\n        (equivalenceClassIndicatorsOfLiftData f d h.rows_pos) =\n        some h.expectedFactors\n    rw [hindicators]\n    exact h.candidates_eq\n  product_eq := h.product_eq\n\nend ForwardRecoveryInputs\n\n/--\nSPEC Group D forward-verification statement at one precision/recovery\ncall: under `L' = W` (deliverable 1) and Mignotte-precision plus the\nresidual abstract obligations B7 / A2, the executable\n`Hex.bhksRecover? f d` returns `some <expected factors>`.\n\nThis is the headline theorem the issue asks for: the bridge from the\ncap-specialised `L' = W` of #3034 (`mignotte_precision` is automatic at any\nprecision meeting `Hex.factorFastPrecisionCap f`) to the success branch of\nthe executable BHKS recovery pipeline.\n-/\ntheorem bhksRecover_eq_some_of_forwardInputs\n    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (d : Hex.LiftData) (h : ForwardRecoveryInputs f d) :\n    Hex.bhksRecover? f d = some h.expectedFactors :=\n  bhksRecover_eq_some_of_recovery f d h.toRecoveryHypotheses\n\n/-- Corollary form: under the SPEC forward-verification inputs,\n`Hex.bhksRecover? f d` is `some _`. -/\ntheorem bhksRecover_isSome_of_forwardInputs\n    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (d : Hex.LiftData) (h : ForwardRecoveryInputs f d) :\n    (Hex.bhksRecover? f d).isSome :=\n  bhksRecover_isSome_of_recovery f d h.toRecoveryHypotheses\n\nend BHKS\n\nend HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/9c6df96f-15c1-426e-aa3c-6f6c5a54aae5.stdout:12:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_5","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' progress/20260617_aad44673.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Progress - 2026-06-17 (session aad44673, one-shot #7592)\n\n## Accomplished\n\nClaimed #7592 (feat: add BHKS cut-separation prefix certificate, critical-path)\nvia `pod once`. Split into its two staged deliverables: the executable prefix\ncut (\"lands first\") and the `CutRetention` proof. **Landed the executable\nprefix cut; rejected the `CutRetention` proof half as an unsound target** (same\ndefect #7575 already diagnosed, invariant under the prefix change), with a\nmachine-checked comment on the issue.\n\n### Executable prefix cut (landed)\n\n`HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`: added `bhksCutPrefixCount` (computes\n`t = (max passing GS index) + 1` by an increasing-order fold) and rewired\n`bhksCutProjectReducedRows` / `bhksRetainedRowIndices` to retain the contiguous\nprefix `b_0 … b_t` in original order, including earlier rows whose own GS norm\nexceeds the radius. This is the genuine BHKS Lemma 5.7 prefix and fixes the\nnon-prefix defect diagnosed on #7578 (per-row survivor set was not a GS-norm\nprefix under LLL).\n\nVerification:\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus` — clean.\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.Lattice` — clean (proof layer intact;\n  nothing constructs `CutRetention`, so the runtime change breaks no proof).\n- Regenerated `conformance-fixtures/HexBerlekampZassenhaus/bz.jsonl` via\n  `hexbz_emit_fixtures` — **byte-identical** to the committed flint-verified\n  ground truth. Factorization output is unchanged (survivor set only grows;\n  final factor multisets match flint).\n- Added-line forbidden-token scan: no sorry/axiom/native_decide/TODO/FIXME.\n\n### `CutRetention` proof half (rejected as unsound — premise defect)\n\n`CutRetention.mem_projected_of_norm_le` (`Lattice.lean:424`) quantifies over\n**arbitrary** `v : Fin r → ℤ` with only a norm bound and concludes membership in\nthe **projected** span `projectedRowSpanInt`. This is unsatisfiable — the same\ndefect #7575 posted (kernel-checked `IsEmpty (CutRetention P)`) — and the prefix\nchange does **not** repair it. Machine-checked under the new prefix code:\n`factorCount=1, coeffWidth=0, reduced=[[2]]` gives `cutRadiusSq4=4`,\n`bhksCutPrefixCount=0`, `bhksCutProjectReducedRows=#[]`, so\n`projectedRowSpanInt=⊥`, yet `v=![1]` has `‖v‖²=1 ≤ 4`. Membership in a\nsublattice never follows from a norm bound alone.\n\nThe issue's *Proof section* states the SOUND fact correctly (\"any\n**original-lattice** vector v with `‖v‖²≤radius` lies in the integer span of the\nretained prefix rows\"). The defect is only the packaging: the sound statement is\nabout lattice vectors of the `(r+n)`-dim reduced basis, not arbitrary `ℤ^r`\nvectors, and not the projected span. `CutRetention` drops the lattice hypothesis\nand projects — that is where soundness is lost.\n\n## Current frontier\n\nExecutable prefix cut is the substrate any sound survivor-span proof needs.\nLanded via `create-pr --partial` (issue → `replan`). Comment on #7592 gives the\ncorrected target.\n\n## Next step (for the replan)\n\nProve the full-lattice survivor-span lemma directly into `CutProjectionHypotheses`\n(NOT `CutRetention`, which should be retired along with\n`cutProjectionHypotheses_of_retention` / `..._of_retention`):\n- For `v` with `memLattice reducedMatrix v` and `‖v‖² ≤ R`, `v ∈` ℤ-span of the\n  prefix rows `b_0 … b_t`, via `basis_normSq` (gramDetVec ↔ `‖b*_i‖²`,\n  `HexGramSchmidtMathlib/Int.lean:1070`) and the integer-top-coefficient /\n  orthogonality lemmas (`normSq_latticeVec_ge_min_basis_normSq`,\n  `rowCombination_normSq_ge_of_orthogonal_coeff_sq_ge_one`,\n  `HexGramSchmidt/Int.lean`).\n- Then the factorization→lattice bridge #7578 listed as absent: true-support\n  indicators are projections of genuine short lattice vectors (CLD-vanishing for\n  true factors), so `CutProjectionHypotheses` holds at exactly the indicators it\n  is consumed at (`PartitionRefinement.lean:484`).\n\n## Blockers\n\nNone for the landed change. The proof half is blocked on a corrected,\nnon-`CutRetention` target (premise defect), routed to replan.\nNote: the worktree carries pre-existing unrelated `.claude/*` modifications from\nbefore this session; not touched or staged.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

The defect is only the packaging: the sound statement is\nabout lattice vectors of the `(r+n)`-dim reduced basis, not arbitrary `ℤ^r`\nvectors, and not the projected span. `CutRetention` drops the lattice hypothesis\nand projects — that is where soundness is lost.\n\n## Current frontier\n\nExecutable prefix cut is the substrate any sound survivor-span proof needs.\nLanded via `create-pr --partial` (issue → `replan`). sessions/04e82479-e4b3-45c9-b5d1-5a3dfa6b5b17.stdout:22:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_9","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc 'coordination list-unclaimed'","aggregated_output":"#7667 feat: scheduled fast-core determinism for raw fast disjunct (2026-06-17T02:57)\n#7651 feat: prove tight psiCut aggregation bound (2026-06-16T22:07)\n#7653 Summarize fifty-ninth Phase 4/5/6 checkpoint (2026-06-16T22:11)\n#7654 review: audit true-factor lift interface for BHKS CLD consumers (2026-06-16T22:11)\n#7655 review: audit exhaustiveLiftBound slow-exhaustive switch (2026-06-16T22:15)\n#7656 review: audit CutProjectionHypotheses projection producer (2026-06-16T22:19)\n#7660 feat: prove TrueFactorLift product CLD aggregation identity (2026-06-16T23:57)\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/04e82479-e4b3-45c9-b5d1-5a3dfa6b5b17.stdout:29:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_14","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"coordination read-issue 7654 --json title,labels,body --jq '{title:.title, labels:[.labels[].name], body:.body}'\"","aggregated_output":"{\"body\":\"depends-on: #7649\\nlibrary: HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib\\n\\n## Current state\\n\\n#7649 adds the first proof-facing interface that distinguishes genuine true-factor lift data from arbitrary `BhksLatticeBasis.cldRows`. That interface is the premise layer for the planned analytic chain:\\n\\n- #7650 will prove the product CLD identity for true-factor supports.\\n- #7651 will prove the tight `psiCut` aggregation bound.\\n- #7640 will consume the tight norm certificate to build `CutProjectionHypotheses`.\\n\\nExisting review #7645 audits the earlier CLD norm-bound and cut-radius reduction chain (`TrueFactorCLDNormBound`, `cldTailSq_le_of_colBound`, `normBound_of_colBound`, coordinate identities, and cut-radius reductions). It does not specifically audit the new #7649 true-factor lift-data interface for non-vacuity, canonicity, or consumer fit.\\n\\n## Deliverables\\n\\nPost a review verdict on this issue after #7649 lands. Assess the landed `BHKS.TrueFactorLift` / `BHKS.TightColumnBound` interface along three axes:\\n\\n1. **Non-vacuity and sound typing:** the interface should rule out arbitrary raw `cldRows` counterexamples without becoming impossible to construct from real `Hex.bhksLatticeBasis` data.\\n2. **Canonicity:** the fields should pin the intended lifted factors/support/product data closely enough that #7650 cannot prove a product identity for a non-canonical witness.\\n3. **Consumer fit:** the exposed theorem/field shapes should line up with #7650, #7651, and `BHKS.tightNormBound_of_lift` without forcing those workers to restate the interface or weaken the tight `2 * natAbs \\u003c= factorCount` column target.\\n\\nDo not modify code in this review issue. If a defect is found, give a concrete counterexample shape or missing-field shape and identify which follow-up feature issue should own the fix.\\n\\n## Context\\n\\nRelevant files:\\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Lattice.lean`\\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/CLDColumnBound.lean`\\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`\\n\\nRelated issues:\\n- #7649 true-factor lift-data interface\\n- #7650 product CLD identity\\n- #7651 tight psiCut aggregation bound\\n- #7645 older norm-bound/cut-radius review\\n- #2564 HO-1 directive\\n\\nThe review should read #7647's skip/counterexample discussion if needed: the old target was false for arbitrary block-form bases because `LiftedFactorSupport` was only a bare subset and `cldRows` were unconstrained.\\n\\n## Verification\\n\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.Lattice`\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.CLDColumnBound`\\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\\n- Verdict comment posted on the issue covering all three axes above.\\n- No code changes in the review PR/branch, unless the reviewer correctly splits off a separate feature issue instead of modifying this one.\\n\",\"labels\":[\"agent-plan\",\"review\"],\"title\":\"review: audit true-factor lift interface for BHKS CLD consumers\"}\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/04e82479-e4b3-45c9-b5d1-5a3dfa6b5b17.stdout:31:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_13","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"coordination read-issue 7667 --json title,labels,body --jq '{title:.title, labels:[.labels[].name], body:.body}'\"","aggregated_output":"{\"body\":\"library: HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib\\n\\nResidual of https://github.com/kim-em/hex/issues/7664 (feat: bridge scheduled fast-core loop to raw fast disjunct) after partial PR https://github.com/kim-em/hex/pull/7666 (feat: decouple scheduled fast-core loop start from recovery precision).\\n\\n## What already landed\\n\\nPR #7666 lands the sound core of #7664 deliverables 1-3: two scheduled-precision-decoupled fast-core irreducibility wrappers in `PartitionRefinement.lean`,\\n`factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs_on_schedule` and its default-cap specialization `factorFastCoreDefault_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs_on_schedule`. These decouple the BHKS core loop's start precision (`initialHenselPrecision a`) from the `ForwardRecoveryInputs` recovery precision `target`, routing the count argument through `factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_count_eq_of_cut`. They take the loop-output equality at the actual `start` as a **hypothesis**. #7664 deliverable 5 (`factorFast_terminates`) was already proven independently.\\n\\nThis issue is the remaining work: discharge that loop-output hypothesis, then lift to the raw fast disjunct (#7664 deliverable 4). It depends on #7666 merging first; rebase onto merged main before starting.\\n\\n## Blocker: scheduled-loop determinism substrate\\n\\nThe wrappers cannot be made unconditional from a cap package alone. `factorFastCoreWithBound` returns the array of the **first** schedule precision where `bhksRecoverClassified` returns `.success` (`HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean:6941`), while the cap `target` is the **last** schedule element. Existing infrastructure proves only existence (`factorFastCoreWithBound_isSome_of_recovery_on_schedule` / `..._ne_none_...`, `Basic.lean:7105/7196`) and per-success product/divisibility/sign/record (`bhksRecoverClassified_success_*`, `Basic.lean:11238-11322`) — never factor identity, never equality of the first-success array to the cap recovery. An earlier schedule precision that exits `.success` returns a **different** array.\\n\\n### Deliverable 1 (the substrate)\\n\\nProve the determinism lemma equating the executable loop output to the cap recovery:\\n\\n```\\nfactorFastCoreWithBound core B primeData (initialHenselPrecision a) fuel\\n  = some hinputs.expectedFactors\\n```\\n\\nfrom the cap `ForwardRecoveryInputs` package. This reduces to a BHKS precision-soundness fact: **recovery fails below the Mignotte precision**, so the first schedule success cannot occur before the cap, pinning the first-success array to the cap recovery. Do not weaken to existence-only and do not assume determinism — establish it from precision soundness.\\n\\n### Deliverable 2 (the lift, #7664 deliverable 4)\\n\\nWith deliverable 1 discharging the loop-output hypothesis of the #7666 wrappers, lift through `reassemblePolynomialFactors_factor_irreducible_of_complete_and_core_irreducible` (`Basic.lean:9781`) using `reassemblyExpansionComplete_of_irreducible_squarefree_cover_of_pos_lc` (`IntReductionMod.lean:2295`) for the fast core factors, and expose the raw `h_raw` fast-disjunct shape consumed by #6672. Mirror the slow-path capstone `reassemblyComplete_of_slowSubstrate_bound` (#7665, `slowModularRaw_irreducible_of_fast_none`); the asymmetry is that the slow path runs a single fixed-precision `exhaustiveCoreFactorsWithBound` with no schedule loop, which is why it was unconditional and the fast path needs deliverable 1 first.\\n\\n## Context\\n\\nRelevant files:\\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/PartitionRefinement.lean`\\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean`\\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean`\\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`\\n\\nKeep the successful array pinned to the same target precision as the `ForwardRecoveryInputs` package; do not bypass the schedule by applying the direct-core theorem at an arbitrary `k`.\\n\\n## Verification\\n\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.PartitionRefinement`\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus`\\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\\n- `git diff --check`\\n- Added-line forbidden-token scan over touched Lean files finds no new `sorry`, `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, or `FIXME`.\\n\",\"labels\":[\"agent-plan\",\"critical-path\",\"feature\"],\"title\":\"feat: scheduled fast-core determinism for raw fast disjunct\"}\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/04e82479-e4b3-45c9-b5d1-5a3dfa6b5b17.stdout:47:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_23","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc 'coordination read-issue 7667 --json body --jq .body'","aggregated_output":"library: HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib\n\nResidual of https://github.com/kim-em/hex/issues/7664 (feat: bridge scheduled fast-core loop to raw fast disjunct) after partial PR https://github.com/kim-em/hex/pull/7666 (feat: decouple scheduled fast-core loop start from recovery precision).\n\n## What already landed\n\nPR #7666 lands the sound core of #7664 deliverables 1-3: two scheduled-precision-decoupled fast-core irreducibility wrappers in `PartitionRefinement.lean`,\n`factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs_on_schedule` and its default-cap specialization `factorFastCoreDefault_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs_on_schedule`. These decouple the BHKS core loop's start precision (`initialHenselPrecision a`) from the `ForwardRecoveryInputs` recovery precision `target`, routing the count argument through `factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_count_eq_of_cut`. They take the loop-output equality at the actual `start` as a **hypothesis**. #7664 deliverable 5 (`factorFast_terminates`) was already proven independently.\n\nThis issue is the remaining work: discharge that loop-output hypothesis, then lift to the raw fast disjunct (#7664 deliverable 4). It depends on #7666 merging first; rebase onto merged main before starting.\n\n## Blocker: scheduled-loop determinism substrate\n\nThe wrappers cannot be made unconditional from a cap package alone. `factorFastCoreWithBound` returns the array of the **first** schedule precision where `bhksRecoverClassified` returns `.success` (`HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean:6941`), while the cap `target` is the **last** schedule element. Existing infrastructure proves only existence (`factorFastCoreWithBound_isSome_of_recovery_on_schedule` / `..._ne_none_...`, `Basic.lean:7105/7196`) and per-success product/divisibility/sign/record (`bhksRecoverClassified_success_*`, `Basic.lean:11238-11322`) — never factor identity, never equality of the first-success array to the cap recovery. An earlier schedule precision that exits `.success` returns a **different** array.\n\n### Deliverable 1 (the substrate)\n\nProve the determinism lemma equating the executable loop output to the cap recovery:\n\n```\nfactorFastCoreWithBound core B primeData (initialHenselPrecision a) fuel\n  = some hinputs.expectedFactors\n```\n\nfrom the cap `ForwardRecoveryInputs` package. This reduces to a BHKS precision-soundness fact: **recovery fails below the Mignotte precision**, so the first schedule success cannot occur before the cap, pinning the first-success array to the cap recovery. Do not weaken to existence-only and do not assume determinism — establish it from precision soundness.\n\n### Deliverable 2 (the lift, #7664 deliverable 4)\n\nWith deliverable 1 discharging the loop-output hypothesis of the #7666 wrappers, lift through `reassemblePolynomialFactors_factor_irreducible_of_complete_and_core_irreducible` (`Basic.lean:9781`) using `reassemblyExpansionComplete_of_irreducible_squarefree_cover_of_pos_lc` (`IntReductionMod.lean:2295`) for the fast core factors, and expose the raw `h_raw` fast-disjunct shape consumed by #6672. Mirror the slow-path capstone `reassemblyComplete_of_slowSubstrate_bound` (#7665, `slowModularRaw_irreducible_of_fast_none`); the asymmetry is that the slow path runs a single fixed-precision `exhaustiveCoreFactorsWithBound` with no schedule loop, which is why it was unconditional and the fast path needs deliverable 1 first.\n\n## Context\n\nRelevant files:\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/PartitionRefinement.lean`\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean`\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean`\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`\n\nKeep the successful array pinned to the same target precision as the `ForwardRecoveryInputs` package; do not bypass the schedule by applying the direct-core theorem at an arbitrary `k`.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.PartitionRefinement`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- `git diff --check`\n- Added-line forbidden-token scan over touched Lean files finds no new `sorry`, `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, or `FIXME`.\n\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

sessions/04e82479-e4b3-45c9-b5d1-5a3dfa6b5b17.stdout:107:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_60","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"coordination read-issue 7656 --json title,labels,body --jq '{title:.title, labels:[.labels[].name], body:.body}'\"","aggregated_output":"{\"body\":\"depends-on: #7640\\nlibrary: HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib\\n\\n## Current state\\n\\n#7640 is the direct producer that should turn the tight BHKS prefix\\nsurvivor-span lemma into `BHKS.CutProjectionHypotheses` for true-factor\\nsupports. It is downstream of the tight norm reduction (#7641) and the\\nprojection/prefix-row infrastructure from #7639, and it is consumed by #7594 on\\nthe path to the fast-BHKS assembly capstone.\\n\\nTwo nearby review issues already cover adjacent but different surfaces:\\n\\n- #7645 audits the older CLD norm-bound / cut-radius reduction chain and the\\n  loose-vs-tight bound distinction.\\n- #7654 audits the true-factor lift-data interface added by #7649.\\n\\nNeither specifically audits the final #7640 projection and packaging step:\\ntransporting full-row prefix-span membership into `projectedRowSpanInt`, proving\\nthe retained-row projection/fold bridge, and packaging the result as\\n`CutProjectionHypotheses` without falling back to the refuted universal\\n`CutRetention` route.\\n\\n## Deliverables\\n\\nPost a review verdict on this issue after #7640 lands. Assess:\\n\\n1. **Projection soundness:** the full `(factorCount + coeffWidth)`-row\\n   prefix-span membership is transported to exactly the first `factorCount`\\n   coordinates, with no off-by-one, row-order, or `Array`/`Matrix.row` mismatch\\n   between `bhksProjectedRows`, `bhksCutProjectReducedRows`, and\\n   `projectedRowsIntMatrix`.\\n2. **Hypothesis discipline:** the final producer carries the required\\n   true-factor lattice and tight norm hypotheses, and does not reintroduce the\\n   false arbitrary-vector `CutRetention.mem_projected_of_norm_le` shape or a\\n   `CutSeparation` assumption as a hidden shortcut.\\n3. **Consumer fit:** the resulting `BHKS.CutProjectionHypotheses` theorem has\\n   the shape #7594 and #6771 need for true supports, without weakening the tight\\n   `4 * normSq \\u003c= bhksCutRadiusSq4` requirement or forcing downstream workers to\\n   restate the projection bridge.\\n\\nDo not modify code in this review issue. If a defect is found, report the exact\\nstatement, row/projection mismatch, or missing hypothesis shape, and identify\\nwhether #7640 should be repaired or a new narrow feature issue should own the\\nfix.\\n\\n## Context\\n\\nRelevant files:\\n\\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Lattice.lean`\\n- `HexGramSchmidtMathlib/Int.lean`\\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`\\n\\nRelated issues:\\n\\n- #7640 direct `CutProjectionHypotheses` producer\\n- #7645 CLD norm-bound / cut-radius review\\n- #7654 true-factor lift-interface review\\n- #7594 downstream BHKS cut projection assembly\\n- #2564 HO-1 directive\\n\\n## Verification\\n\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.Lattice`\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`\\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\\n- Verdict comment posted on this issue covering all three axes above.\\n- No implementation files are modified.\\n\",\"labels\":[\"agent-plan\",\"review\"],\"title\":\"review: audit CutProjectionHypotheses projection producer\"}\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
