sessions/1e9743c5-17e5-4aaa-8581-4758314478e7.stdout:50:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_27","type":"agent_message","text":"The repo already has the #6790 `CutSurvival` bridge, so #6791 can probably be a focused norm-bound/API slice rather than the full `CutProjectionHypotheses` construction. I’m reading the local definitions around that bridge and the executable cut radius."}}
sessions/1e9743c5-17e5-4aaa-8581-4758314478e7.stdout:56:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_30","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '430,470p' HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Lattice.lean\"","aggregated_output":"  exact Submodule.subset_span ⟨i, rfl⟩\n\n/--\nProof-facing certificate that the executable Gram-Schmidt cut retained one row\nfor each true-factor indicator.\n\nThe executable `BhksProjectedRows` stores only post-cut projected rows.  This\nrecord is the minimal bridge from later cut-survival proofs to the existing\n`CutProjectionHypotheses` surface: a caller identifies the retained projected\nrow whose first `r` coordinates are the requested true-support indicator.\n-/\nstructure CutSurvival\n    (L : Hex.BhksProjectedRows) (trueSupports : Set (Set (Fin L.factorCount))) where\n  rowOfIndicator : trueSupports → Fin L.projectedRows.size\n  rowOfIndicator_eq :\n    ∀ S : trueSupports,\n      Matrix.row (projectedRowsIntMatrix L) (rowOfIndicator S) =\n        indicatorVector S.1\n\nnamespace CutSurvival\n\n/-- A retained indicator row is in the projected integer row span. -/\ntheorem mem_projected\n    (L : Hex.BhksProjectedRows) (trueSupports : Set (Set (Fin L.factorCount)))\n    (hcut : CutSurvival L trueSupports) (S : trueSupports) :\n    indicatorVector S.1 ∈ projectedRowSpanInt L := by\n  have hrow := projectedRow_mem_projectedRowSpanInt L (hcut.rowOfIndicator S)\n  simpa [hcut.rowOfIndicator_eq S] using hrow\n\n/-- Package retained-row evidence as the standard cut hypothesis record. -/\ndef toCut\n    (L : Hex.BhksProjectedRows) (trueSupports : Set (Set (Fin L.factorCount)))\n    (hcut : CutSurvival L trueSupports) :\n    CutProjectionHypotheses L trueSupports where\n  indicator_mem_projected := mem_projected L trueSupports hcut\n\nend CutSurvival\n\n/-- Each projected rational row is one of the generators of the rational row space. -/\ntheorem projectedRow_mem_projectedRowSpaceRat\n    (L : Hex.BhksProjectedRows) (i : Fin L.projectedRows.size) :\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/41e75524-cc2d-45ba-9d38-765441a4ad6f.stdout:25:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_11","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"coordination read-issue 3533 --json title,labels,body --jq '{title, labels: [.labels[].name], body}'\"","aggregated_output":"{\"body\":\"## Current state\\n\\nHuman-oversight issue #2567 asks for the BHKS Theorem 5.2 leaf theorem\\n`factorFast f != none`. The current support chain has split the\\nfixed-precision recovery proof in `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean`\\ninto explicit fields on `ForwardRecoveryInputs`.\\n\\nOpen issue #3530 owns the B7 indicator-identification field\\n`indicators_match`. Open issue #3529 owns the A2 candidate reconstruction field\\n`candidates_eq`. The remaining executable guard immediately adjacent to B7 is\\nthe non-degenerate partition check:\\n\\n```lean\\nHex.bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition\\n    (projectedRowsOfLiftData f d rows_pos) expectedIndicators = false\\n```\\n\\nThis issue should discharge that guard from the true-support indicator data,\\nwithout also proving A2 reconstruction, the final product check, or the public\\n`factorFast_terminates` theorem.\\n\\nSupports: #2567\\n\\ndepends-on: #3530\\n\\n## Deliverables\\n\\n1. In `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean`, add a theorem or\\n   constructor, with a name like\\n   `ForwardRecoveryInputs.nondegenerateOfTrueSupportIndicators`, proving the\\n   `nondegenerate` field from the B7-matched expected indicator partition and\\n   the true-factor support hypotheses.\\n2. Keep the proof scoped to the executable\\n   `bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition` guard used by `bhksRecover?`; do not\\n   change the executable recovery code.\\n3. If the needed fact is really a small array/partition helper, add it locally\\n   in `Recovery.lean` or the existing BZ Mathlib recovery support module rather\\n   than broadening into RREF correctness.\\n\\n## Library placement\\n\\nTarget file:\\n\\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean`\\n\\nGoverning SPEC quote from `SPEC/Libraries/hex-berlekamp-zassenhaus.md`:\\n\\\"With `L' = W` from step 3 and precision \\u003e= Mignotte: B7 produces exactly the\\nirreducible-factor indicators (Lemma 3.3), A2 gives exact integer-coefficient\\nlifts of each `g_{w_C}`, and exact division of `f` succeeds for every\\ncandidate. So the algorithm exits via `some _`, not `none`.\\\"\\n\\nQ1: `Recovery.lean` owns the forward-verification bridge from B7/A2 facts to\\nthe executable `bhksRecover?` success branch.\\n\\nQ2: yes. This is Mathlib-facing proof infrastructure for the BHKS recovery\\nbridge.\\n\\nQ3: Mathlib has generic list/array and linear algebra facts, but no theorem\\nabout Hex's `bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition` or BHKS true-factor support\\nindicators.\\n\\nQ4: this depends on #3530 for the final expected-indicator shape. If #3530\\nalso proves this exact non-degeneracy guard, close this issue as duplicate\\nrather than repeating the proof.\\n\\n## Context\\n\\nRead:\\n\\n- Human-oversight issue #2567.\\n- Issue #3530 and its PR if already open.\\n- `SPEC/Libraries/hex-berlekamp-zassenhaus.md`, especially Group B/B7 and the\\n  Group D forward-verification paragraph.\\n- `PLAN/Phase5.md`\\n- `PLAN/Conventions.md`\\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean`\\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean` around\\n  `bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition`, `bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators`, and\\n  `bhksRecover?`.\\n\\n## Verification\\n\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.Recovery`\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`\\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\\n- `git diff --check`\\n- `rg -n '^axiom\\\\b|native_decide|TODO|FIXME|sorry' HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean`\\n\\n## Out of scope\\n\\n- Proving B7 indicator identification itself; that is #3530.\\n- Proving A2 candidate reconstruction; that is #3529.\\n- Proving the final product check or public `factorFast_terminates`.\\n- Changing executable BZ recovery behavior.\\n- Editing `SPEC/`, top-level `PLAN.md`, or top-level `AGENTS.md`.\\n\",\"labels\":[\"agent-plan\",\"feature\"],\"title\":\"HexBZ Mathlib: prove recovery indicator partition nondegeneracy\"}\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/41e75524-cc2d-45ba-9d38-765441a4ad6f.stdout:32:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_16","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"coordination read-issue 3757 --json title,labels,body --jq '{title, labels: [.labels[].name], body}'\"","aggregated_output":"{\"body\":\"## Current state\\n\\nParent #3530 asks for the BHKS B7 bridge from `projectedRowSpanInt L = trueFactorIndicatorLattice trueSupports` to the executable `bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators` output. Inspection showed this is broader than a local constructor: the project first needs the generic executable RREF row-span bridge in #3756, then a BZ-specific theorem about column signatures of the RREF of the projected-row matrix.\\n\\nParent: #3530\\n\\ndepends-on: #3756\\n\\n## Deliverables\\n\\n1. In `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean` and/or `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Lattice.lean`, add a proof-facing definition of the expected B7 indicator array determined by `trueSupports` that matches the executable array-of-0/1 shape used by `Hex.bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators`.\\n2. Prove a BZ-specific bridge: if `projectedRowSpanInt L = trueFactorIndicatorLattice trueSupports`, then the column-signature grouping performed by `Hex.bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators L` returns exactly that expected indicator array.\\n3. Use #3756's completed row-span bridge to reason about the executable `Matrix.rref` row space. Add only narrow local lemmas for integer-to-rational row-space transport or signature/class membership if they are missing.\\n4. Add `EquivalenceClassRecoveryHypotheses.ofIndicatorLattice` (or an equivalently named constructor) that fills `indicators_match` from `lattice_eq_indicators` using the B7 bridge.\\n5. Do not add `axiom`, use `native_decide`, or introduce new theorem-level `sorry`.\\n\\n## Library placement\\n\\nTarget files:\\n\\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean`\\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Lattice.lean` if helper lattice statements are needed\\n- `HexMatrixMathlib/RankSpanNullspace.lean` only for tiny follow-on bridge lemmas after #3756\\n\\nGoverning SPEC quote from `SPEC/Libraries/hex-berlekamp-zassenhaus.md`:\\n\\\"B7. Equivalence-class identification given `L' = W` (BHKS Lemma 3.3). When `L' = W`, the rref + equivalence-class procedure produces exactly the indicator vectors of irreducible-factor subsets.\\\"\\n\\n## Context\\n\\nRead:\\n\\n- Parent issue #3530.\\n- Prerequisite #3756.\\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean` around `bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators`, `bhksColumnSignature`, and `bhksClassIndicator`.\\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean` around `EquivalenceClassRecoveryHypotheses`.\\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Lattice.lean` around `projectedRowSpanInt` and `trueFactorIndicatorLattice`.\\n- `HexMatrix/RREF.lean` and `HexMatrixMathlib/RankSpanNullspace.lean` after #3756 lands.\\n- `PLAN/Phase5.md` and `PLAN/Conventions.md`.\\n\\n## Verification\\n\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.Recovery`\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`\\n- `lake build HexMatrixMathlib.RankSpanNullspace` if touched\\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\\n- `git diff --check`\\n- `rg -n '^axiom\\\\\\\\b|native_decide|TODO|FIXME|sorry' HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Lattice.lean HexMatrixMathlib/RankSpanNullspace.lean`\\n\\n## Out of scope\\n\\n- A2 centred-residue reconstruction.\\n- Final `factorFast` termination/non-none leaf.\\n- Changing executable BZ recovery or RREF algorithms.\\n- Editing `SPEC/`, top-level `PLAN.md`, or top-level `AGENTS.md`.\\n\",\"labels\":[\"agent-plan\",\"feature\"],\"title\":\"HexBZ Mathlib: prove B7 indicator signatures from true-factor lattice\"}\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/3f38e3c8-1918-4708-9f8b-e7496011ec4b.stdout:26:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_11","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc 'coordination read-issue 4055 --json body --jq .body'","aggregated_output":"## Current state\n\nPartial work on #4030 has landed the upper-bound half of the fast-core count equality:\n\n```lean\nHexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.UFDPartition.length_le_normalizedFactors_card\nHexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_count_le\n```\n\nThe remaining obligation is the BHKS/B8 lower bound showing that a successful fast-core recovery cannot emit fewer candidates than the normalized irreducible factor count of the input core. Once this lower bound is available, #4030's requested equality can be assembled by antisymmetry.\n\ndepends-on: #4030\n\n## Deliverables\n\n1. In `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean` or a focused sibling bridge module, prove a theorem equivalent to:\n\n   ```lean\n   theorem factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_count_ge\n       {core : Hex.ZPoly} {B : Nat} {primeData : Hex.PrimeChoiceData}\n       {k fuel : Nat} {coreFactors : Array Hex.ZPoly}\n       -- include the square-free / primitive / admissible-prime / BHKS recovery hypotheses needed by the bridge\n       (h : Hex.factorFastCoreWithBound core B primeData k fuel = some coreFactors) :\n       (UniqueFactorizationMonoid.normalizedFactors\n         (HexPolyZMathlib.toPolynomial core)).card ≤\n           (coreFactors.toList.map HexPolyZMathlib.toPolynomial).length\n   ```\n\n   The exact hypothesis list should follow the existing recovery bridge records rather than inventing new assumptions.\n2. Route the proof through the BHKS partition-refinement argument:\n   B6 gives true-factor indicator support inclusion in the projected row span, the executable equivalence-class partition therefore refines true supports, and candidate reconstruction plus the final product check rule out strict refinement.\n3. Compose with `factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_count_le` to produce the equality theorem originally requested by #4030.\n4. Do not introduce `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, `FIXME`, or new theorem-level `sorry`.\n\n## Context\n\nRead #4030, `SPEC/Libraries/hex-berlekamp-zassenhaus.md` Group B6/B7/B8, `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Lattice.lean`, `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean`, `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/UFDPartition.lean`, and `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean` around `bhksRecoverClassified`, `bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators`, `bhksIndicatorCandidates?`, and `factorFastCoreWithBound`.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.Basic`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- `git diff --check`\n- No new `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, `FIXME`, or theorem-level `sorry` in affected files.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Exhaustive branch certification.\n- Small-mod singleton branch certification.\n- Final `factorWithBound` output assembly.\n- Editing `SPEC/`, top-level `PLAN.md`, or top-level `AGENTS.md`.\n\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/cbeada37-38db-4e99-9e65-2f960b01c68a.stdout:20:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_9","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"gh issue view 3063 --json labels,title,body --jq '{title:.title, labels:[.labels[].name], body:.body}'\"","aggregated_output":"{\"body\":\"## Current state\\n\\nHuman-oversight issue #2564 forbids the old additive-coefficient recombination\\nlattice: true integer factors are subset products of lifted local factors, not\\nshort-vector sums of their coefficient arrays.\\n\\nCurrent `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean` now has the newer BHKS CLD\\nexecutable surface (`bhksLatticeBasis`, `bhksProjectedRows`,\\n`bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators`, `bhksRecover?`, `factorFast`,\\n`factorSlow`, and the `factor`/`factorWithBound` combinator). However the old\\nadditive path is still present as `RecombinationLattice`,\\n`recombinationLattice?`, `decodeShortVector`, `shortVectorCandidates`,\\n`recombineLLL?`, `recombine`, `LiftedFactorsRecombineTo`,\\n`adaptiveCoreFactors`, and `recombine_product_of_lifted_factors`.\\n\\nThat stale surface is still referenced by `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/CrossCheck.lean`\\nand `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Conformance.lean`, so tests can continue to exercise\\nthe inadmissible lattice even though the public fast path should be BHKS CLD.\\n\\nSupports: #2564\\n\\n## Deliverables\\n\\n1. In `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`, remove or make private the obsolete\\n   additive recombination API so production-facing names no longer expose or\\n   depend on `recombineLLL?` / `recombine` / `LiftedFactorsRecombineTo`.\\n2. Retarget `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/CrossCheck.lean` and the affected\\n   conformance guards to compare the supported surfaces instead: `bhksRecover?`\\n   or `factorFast` when it returns `some`, and the exhaustive `factorSlow`\\n   backstop.\\n3. Preserve executable behavior of `factorFast`, `factorSlow`, `factor`,\\n   and `factorWithBound`; this issue is a surface quarantine/retargeting pass,\\n   not a rewrite of BHKS CLD recovery.\\n\\n## Library placement\\n\\nFiles: `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`,\\n`HexBerlekampZassenhaus/CrossCheck.lean`, and\\n`HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Conformance.lean`.\\n\\nSPEC quote from `SPEC/Libraries/hex-berlekamp-zassenhaus.md`: \\\"An\\nadditive-coefficient lattice that decodes short vectors as `Σ λ_i g_i (mod\\np^a)` candidate polynomials is not van Hoeij and is not admissible.\\\"\\n\\nQ1: This is the executable recombination surface cleanup required by the\\nSPEC-pinned BHKS CLD architecture.\\nQ2: No Mathlib bridge is needed; the work is in the Mathlib-free executable and\\nconformance modules.\\nQ3: Reuse the already-present BHKS CLD helpers and exhaustive slow path rather\\nthan introducing a new recombination algorithm.\\nQ4: No prerequisite beyond current `origin/main`; blocked proof holes in the\\nBHKS correctness story are tracked separately by #2953, #3018, #3019, #2764,\\n#2803, and #2833.\\n\\n## Context\\n\\nRead:\\n\\n- #2564, especially the owner explanation of why the additive lattice is wrong.\\n- `SPEC/Libraries/hex-berlekamp-zassenhaus.md` around \\\"Fast path: van Hoeij CLD\\n  lattice\\\" and \\\"Proof-obligation pitfalls\\\".\\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean` around the BHKS CLD helpers and the old\\n  additive `RecombinationLattice` / `recombineLLL?` block.\\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/CrossCheck.lean`.\\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Conformance.lean`.\\n\\n## Verification\\n\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus.Basic`\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus.CrossCheck`\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus.Conformance`\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus`\\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\\n- `git diff --check`\\n- `rg -n 'recombineLLL\\\\\\\\?|RecombinationLattice|decodeShortVector|shortVectorCandidates|LiftedFactorsRecombineTo|adaptiveCoreFactors' HexBerlekampZassenhaus` shows no production-facing stale additive path remains, except comments explicitly documenting its removal if needed.\\n- `rg -n '^axiom\\\\\\\\b|native_decide|TODO|FIXME|sorry' HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean HexBerlekampZassenhaus/CrossCheck.lean HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Conformance.lean` shows no new banned markers beyond unrelated pre-existing proof holes.\\n\\n## Out of scope\\n\\n- Proving `bhksLatticeBasis_independent`; that is #2953.\\n- Proving normalization/reassembly product theorems; those are #3018 and #3019.\\n- Proving the bounded Factorization and Mathlib capstones; those are #2764,\\n  #2803, and #2833.\\n- Closing #2564 or editing the human-oversight issue.\\n- Editing `SPEC/`, top-level `PLAN.md`, or top-level `AGENTS.md`.\",\"labels\":[\"agent-plan\",\"feature\"],\"title\":\"HexBZ: quarantine stale additive recombination surface\"}\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/cbeada37-38db-4e99-9e65-2f960b01c68a.stdout:49:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_27","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1540,2025p' HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean\"","aggregated_output":"        indices.any (fun i => indicator.getD i 0 == 1) then\n      some <| indices.foldl\n        (fun selected i =>\n          if indicator.getD i 0 == 1 then\n            selected.push (liftedFactors.getD i 0)\n          else\n            selected)\n        #[]\n    else\n      none\n\n/--\nReconstruct and verify one BHKS equivalence-class indicator.\n\nThe indicator row is supplied by the later RREF recovery stage. This helper\nonly checks that the row is a nonempty `0/1` vector over the lifted factors,\nforms `lc(f) * product selected g_i` modulo the Hensel modulus, applies the\ncentred integer lift, normalizes content and sign, and accepts the candidate\nonly when exact division of `f` succeeds.\n-/\ndef bhksIndicatorCandidate?\n    (f : ZPoly) (d : LiftData) (indicator : Array Int) : Option (ZPoly × ZPoly) :=\n  match bhksIndicatorSelectedFactors d.liftedFactors indicator with\n  | none => none\n  | some selected =>\n      let modulus := liftModulus d\n      let raw := DensePoly.scale (DensePoly.leadingCoeff f) (Array.polyProduct selected)\n      let candidate := normalizeCandidateFactor <|\n        centeredLiftPoly (ZPoly.reduceModPow raw d.p d.k) modulus\n      match exactQuotient? f candidate with\n      | some quotient => some (candidate, quotient)\n      | none => none\n\nprivate def bhksIndicatorOneCount (r : Nat) (indicator : Array Int) : Nat :=\n  (List.range r).foldl\n    (fun count i => if indicator.getD i 0 == 1 then count + 1 else count)\n    0\n\nprivate def bhksIndicatorAllOnes (r : Nat) (indicator : Array Int) : Bool :=\n  indicator.size == r && bhksIndicatorOneCount r indicator == r\n\nprivate def bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition\n    (L : BhksProjectedRows) (indicators : Array (Array Int)) : Bool :=\n  indicators.isEmpty ||\n    L.projectedRows.isEmpty ||\n    (indicators.size == 1 &&\n      bhksIndicatorAllOnes L.factorCount (indicators.getD 0 #[]))\n\nprivate def bhksIndicatorCandidates?\n    (f : ZPoly) (d : LiftData) (indicators : Array (Array Int)) :\n    Option (Array ZPoly) :=\n  indicators.foldl\n    (fun acc indicator =>\n      match acc with\n      | none => none\n      | some candidates =>\n          match bhksIndicatorCandidate? f d indicator with\n          | some candidate => some (candidates.push candidate.1)\n          | none => none)\n    (some #[])\n\n/--\nRun the fixed-precision BHKS recovery pipeline.\n\nThis executable glue builds the CLD lattice for the lifted factors, runs LLL\nplus the Gram-Schmidt cut, extracts BHKS Lemma 3.3 equivalence-class\nindicators by RREF, reconstructs every indicated candidate by centred lifting,\nand accepts only when the verified candidates multiply back to `f`.\n-/\ndef bhksRecover? (f : ZPoly) (d : LiftData) : Option (Array ZPoly) :=\n  let L := bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors\n  if hrows : 1 ≤ L.factorCount + L.coeffWidth then\n    let projected := bhksProjectedRows L hrows (bhksLatticeBasis_independent L)\n    let indicators := bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators projected\n    if bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition projected indicators then\n      none\n    else\n      match bhksIndicatorCandidates? f d indicators with\n      | none => none\n      | some candidates =>\n          if Array.polyProduct candidates == f then\n            some candidates\n          else\n            none\n  else\n    none\n\nprivate def bhksIndicatorGuardLift : LiftData :=\n  { p := 5\n    k := 2\n    liftedFactors := bhksGuardFactors }\n\n#guard bhksIndicatorCandidate? cldGuardF bhksIndicatorGuardLift #[1, 0] =\n  some (DensePoly.ofCoeffs #[-2, 1], DensePoly.ofCoeffs #[-3, 1])\n#guard bhksIndicatorCandidate? cldGuardF bhksIndicatorGuardLift #[0, 0] = none\n#guard bhksIndicatorCandidate? cldGuardF bhksIndicatorGuardLift #[2, 0] = none\n#guard (bhksIndicatorCandidate? cldGuardF bhksIndicatorGuardLift #[0, 1]).map Prod.snd =\n  some (DensePoly.ofCoeffs #[-2, 1])\n\n#guard bhksRecover? cldGuardF bhksIndicatorGuardLift =\n  some bhksGuardFactors\n\nprivate def bhksDegenerateRecoverLift : LiftData :=\n  { p := 5\n    k := 2\n    liftedFactors := #[DensePoly.ofCoeffs #[1]] }\n\n#guard bhksRecover? cldGuardF bhksDegenerateRecoverLift = none\n\nprivate def bhksFailedDivisionRecoverLift : LiftData :=\n  { p := 5\n    k := 2\n    liftedFactors := #[DensePoly.ofCoeffs #[-2, 1], DensePoly.ofCoeffs #[-4, 1]] }\n\n#guard bhksIndicatorCandidate? cldGuardF bhksFailedDivisionRecoverLift #[0, 1] = none\n#guard bhksRecover? cldGuardF bhksFailedDivisionRecoverLift = none\n\nprivate structure RecombinationLattice where\n  rows : Nat\n  cols : Nat\n  coeffWidth : Nat\n  rows_pos : 1 ≤ rows\n  coeffWidth_le_cols : coeffWidth ≤ cols\n  basis : Matrix Int rows cols\n\nprivate def recombinationLattice? (d : LiftData) (coeffWidth : Nat) :\n    Option RecombinationLattice :=\n  if hrows : 0 < d.liftedFactors.size then\n    let rows := d.liftedFactors.size\n    let cols := coeffWidth + rows\n    let modulusNat := liftModulus d\n    let modulus := Int.ofNat modulusNat\n    let basis : Matrix Int rows cols :=\n      Matrix.ofFn fun i j =>\n        if hcoeff : j.val < coeffWidth then\n          centeredModNat (d.liftedFactors[i].coeff j.val) modulusNat\n        else if j.val - coeffWidth = i.val then\n          modulus\n        else\n          0\n    some\n      { rows\n        cols\n        coeffWidth\n        rows_pos := hrows\n        coeffWidth_le_cols := Nat.le_add_right coeffWidth rows\n        basis }\n  else\n    none\n\nprivate theorem recombinationLattice_independent (L : RecombinationLattice) :\n    L.basis.independent := by\n  sorry\n\nprivate def decodeShortVector (coeffWidth cols : Nat) (v : Vector Int cols) : ZPoly :=\n  DensePoly.ofCoeffs <|\n    (List.range coeffWidth).map\n      (fun i =>\n        if h : i < cols then\n          v.get ⟨i, h⟩\n        else\n          0)\n    |>.toArray\n\nprivate def shortVectorCandidates (L : RecombinationLattice) : Array ZPoly :=\n  (lll.shortVectors L.basis (3 / 4) lll_delta_lower lll_delta_upper L.rows_pos\n      (recombinationLattice_independent L)).map\n    (decodeShortVector L.coeffWidth L.cols)\n\nprivate structure RecombinationState where\n  target : ZPoly\n  factors : Array ZPoly\n\nprivate def acceptRecombinationCandidate\n    (state : RecombinationState) (candidate : ZPoly) : RecombinationState :=\n  match exactQuotient? state.target candidate with\n  | some quotient => { target := quotient, factors := state.factors.push candidate }\n  | none => state\n\nprivate def verifyShortVectorCandidates (target : ZPoly) (candidates : Array ZPoly) :\n    Option (Array ZPoly) :=\n  let final :=\n    candidates.foldl acceptRecombinationCandidate { target, factors := #[] }\n  if final.target = 1 then\n    some final.factors\n  else\n    none\n\n/--\nRecombine lifted local factors via LLL short-vector enumeration.\n\nThe production recombination path; returns `none` when the LLL pass produces\nno candidates that exactly partition `f`. Exposed (rather than `private`) so\nthe SPEC-sanctioned LLL-vs-exhaustive cross-check in `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/\nCrossCheck.lean` can compare its output against `recombinationSearch` on the\nsame lifted-factor set.\n-/\ndef recombineLLL? (f : ZPoly) (d : LiftData) : Option (Array ZPoly) :=\n  let coeffWidth := f.degree?.getD 0 + 1\n  match recombinationLattice? d coeffWidth with\n  | none => none\n  | some L => verifyShortVectorCandidates f (shortVectorCandidates L)\n\nprivate def recombinationSearchAux\n    (target : ZPoly) (localFactors : List ZPoly) : Nat → Option (List ZPoly)\n  | 0 => none\n  | fuel + 1 =>\n      if target = 1 then\n        some []\n      else\n        firstSome (subsetSplitsWithFirst localFactors) fun split =>\n          let candidate := Array.polyProduct split.1.toArray\n          match exactQuotient? target candidate with\n          | none => none\n          | some quotient =>\n              match recombinationSearchAux quotient split.2 fuel with\n              | none => none\n              | some rest => some (candidate :: rest)\n\n/--\nSearch for an integer-factor recombination of the lifted local factors.\n\nThe search enumerates subsets containing the first remaining local factor,\naccepts a subset only when its product exactly divides the current target, and\nthen recurses on the quotient and unused local factors.\n-/\ndef recombinationSearch (f : ZPoly) (localFactors : List ZPoly) : Option (List ZPoly) :=\n  recombinationSearchAux f localFactors (localFactors.length + 1)\n\nprivate def recombinationSearchModAux\n    (target : ZPoly) (modulus : Nat) (localFactors : List ZPoly) :\n    Nat → Option (List ZPoly)\n  | 0 => none\n  | fuel + 1 =>\n      if target = 1 then\n        some []\n      else\n        firstSome (subsetSplitsWithFirst localFactors) fun split =>\n          let candidate :=\n            ZPoly.primitivePart <|\n              centeredLiftPoly (Array.polyProduct split.1.toArray) modulus\n          match exactQuotient? target candidate with\n          | none => none\n          | some quotient =>\n              match recombinationSearchModAux quotient modulus split.2 fuel with\n              | none => none\n              | some rest => some (candidate :: rest)\n\nprivate def recombinationSearchMod\n    (f : ZPoly) (modulus : Nat) (localFactors : List ZPoly) : Option (List ZPoly) :=\n  recombinationSearchModAux f modulus localFactors (localFactors.length + 1)\n\nprivate def recombineExhaustive (f : ZPoly) (d : LiftData) : Array ZPoly :=\n  match recombinationSearchMod f (liftModulus d) d.liftedFactors.toList with\n  | some factors => factors.toArray\n  | none => #[]\n\nprivate def exhaustiveRecombinationLocalFactorLimit : Nat := 4\n\nprivate def canUseExhaustiveRecombination (d : LiftData) : Bool :=\n  d.liftedFactors.size ≤ exhaustiveRecombinationLocalFactorLimit\n\n/--\nThe lifted factors contain enough information for the executable exhaustive\nrecombination search to recover factors of `f`.\n-/\ndef LiftedFactorsRecombineTo (f : ZPoly) (d : LiftData) : Prop :=\n  (∃ factors, recombineLLL? f d = some factors ∧ Array.polyProduct factors = f) ∨\n    (recombineLLL? f d = none ∧ canUseExhaustiveRecombination d ∧\n      ∃ factors,\n        recombinationSearchMod f (liftModulus d) d.liftedFactors.toList = some factors)\n\n/--\nRecombine lifted local factors into integer factors.\n\nThe production path is LLL-based. Exhaustive subset recombination is retained\nonly as a small-input fallback; for larger inputs, an LLL miss is reported as\nan explicit recombination failure.\n-/\ndef recombine (f : ZPoly) (d : LiftData) : Array ZPoly :=\n  match recombineLLL? f d with\n  | some factors => factors\n  | none =>\n      if canUseExhaustiveRecombination d then\n        recombineExhaustive f d\n      else\n        #[]\n\nprivate def recombineLLLBranchGuardFactors : Array ZPoly :=\n  #[DensePoly.ofCoeffs #[-1, 1],\n    DensePoly.ofCoeffs #[-2, 1],\n    DensePoly.ofCoeffs #[-3, 1],\n    DensePoly.ofCoeffs #[-4, 1],\n    DensePoly.ofCoeffs #[-5, 1]]\n\nprivate def recombineLLLBranchGuardInput : ZPoly :=\n  Array.polyProduct recombineLLLBranchGuardFactors\n\nprivate def recombineLLLBranchGuardLift : LiftData :=\n  { p := 2\n    k := 8\n    liftedFactors := recombineLLLBranchGuardFactors }\n\n#guard !canUseExhaustiveRecombination recombineLLLBranchGuardLift\n\n#guard Array.polyProduct (recombine recombineLLLBranchGuardInput recombineLLLBranchGuardLift) =\n  recombineLLLBranchGuardInput\n\nprivate def initialHenselPrecision (B : Nat) : Nat :=\n  if B ≤ 4 then B else 4\n\nprivate def nextHenselPrecision (k B : Nat) : Nat :=\n  if 2 * k < B then\n    2 * k\n  else\n    B\n\nprivate def factorFastCoreWithBound\n    (core : ZPoly) (B : Nat) (primeData : PrimeChoiceData) : Nat → Nat → Option (Array ZPoly)\n  | _k, 0 => none\n  | k, fuel + 1 =>\n      let liftData := henselLiftData core k primeData\n      match bhksRecover? core liftData with\n      | some factors => some factors\n      | none =>\n        if k ≥ B then\n          none\n        else\n          factorFastCoreWithBound core B primeData (nextHenselPrecision k B) fuel\n\nprivate def factorFastCoreGuardPrimeData : PrimeChoiceData :=\n  choosePrimeData cldGuardF\n\n#guard factorFastCoreWithBound cldGuardF 1 factorFastCoreGuardPrimeData\n    (initialHenselPrecision 1) (ZPoly.quadraticDoublingSteps 1 + 2) =\n  none\n\n#guard factorFastCoreWithBound cldGuardF 4 factorFastCoreGuardPrimeData\n    (initialHenselPrecision 4) (ZPoly.quadraticDoublingSteps 4 + 2) =\n  some bhksGuardFactors\n\n/--\nAdaptively lift and retry LLL recombination, using the explicit bound as the\nceiling. If LLL recombination still fails at the bound, report the core as\nirreducible under that bound.\n-/\nprivate def adaptiveCoreFactors\n    (core : ZPoly) (B : Nat) (primeData : PrimeChoiceData) : Nat → Nat → Array ZPoly\n  | _k, 0 => #[core]\n  | k, fuel + 1 =>\n      let liftData := henselLiftData core k primeData\n      match recombineLLL? core liftData with\n      | some factors => factors\n      | none =>\n          if k ≥ B then\n            #[core]\n          else\n            adaptiveCoreFactors core B primeData\n              (nextHenselPrecision k B) fuel\n\nprivate def exhaustiveCoreFactorsWithBound\n    (core : ZPoly) (B : Nat) (primeData : PrimeChoiceData) : Array ZPoly :=\n  if B = 0 then\n    #[core]\n  else\n    let liftData := henselLiftData core B primeData\n    let factors := recombineExhaustive core liftData\n    if factors.isEmpty then\n      #[core]\n    else\n      factors\n\nprivate def factorSlowFactorsWithBound (f : ZPoly) (B : Nat) : Array ZPoly :=\n  let normalized := normalizeForFactor f\n  if normalized.squareFreeCore.degree?.getD 0 = 0 then\n    reassemblePolynomialFactors normalized #[]\n  else\n    match quadraticIntegerRootFactors? normalized.squareFreeCore with\n    | some coreFactors => reassemblePolynomialFactors normalized coreFactors\n    | none =>\n        let primeData := choosePrimeData normalized.squareFreeCore\n        let coreFactors :=\n          exhaustiveCoreFactorsWithBound normalized.squareFreeCore B primeData\n        reassemblePolynomialFactors normalized coreFactors\n\nprivate def factorSlowWithBound (f : ZPoly) (B : Nat) : Factorization :=\n  factorizationOfFactors f (factorSlowFactorsWithBound f B)\n\n/--\nFactor using the exhaustive recombination path at the default Mignotte\ncoefficient bound. This is the public slow-path backstop for the two-tier BZ\nAPI.\n-/\ndef factorSlow (f : ZPoly) : Factorization :=\n  factorSlowWithBound f (ZPoly.defaultFactorCoeffBound f)\n\nprivate def factorFastFactorsWithBound (f : ZPoly) (B : Nat) : Option (Array ZPoly) :=\n  let normalized := normalizeForFactor f\n  if normalized.squareFreeCore.degree?.getD 0 = 0 then\n    some (reassemblePolynomialFactors normalized #[])\n  else if B = 0 then\n    none\n  else\n    if B = 1 then\n      let primeData := choosePrimeData normalized.squareFreeCore\n      if primeData.factorsModP.size ≤ 1 then\n        some (reassemblePolynomialFactors normalized #[normalized.squareFreeCore])\n      else\n        match factorFastCoreWithBound normalized.squareFreeCore B primeData\n            (initialHenselPrecision B) (ZPoly.quadraticDoublingSteps B + 2) with\n        | some coreFactors => some (reassemblePolynomialFactors normalized coreFactors)\n        | none => none\n    else\n      match quadraticIntegerRootFactors? normalized.squareFreeCore with\n      | some coreFactors => some (reassemblePolynomialFactors normalized coreFactors)\n      | none =>\n        let primeData := choosePrimeData normalized.squareFreeCore\n        if primeData.factorsModP.size ≤ 1 then\n          some (reassemblePolynomialFactors normalized #[normalized.squareFreeCore])\n        else\n          match factorFastCoreWithBound normalized.squareFreeCore B primeData\n              (initialHenselPrecision B) (ZPoly.quadraticDoublingSteps B + 2) with\n          | some coreFactors => some (reassemblePolynomialFactors normalized coreFactors)\n          | none => none\n\n#guard factorFastFactorsWithBound cldGuardF 1 = none\n\n#guard factorFastFactorsWithBound cldGuardF 4 =\n  some bhksGuardFactors\n\n/--\nPrecision cap used by the public fast path.\n\nThe cap is the larger of the BHKS separation threshold bound and the\nMignotte coefficient bound, so later termination proofs can use the same\nprecision for both lattice separation and exact integer reconstruction.\n-/\ndef factorFastPrecisionCap (f : ZPoly) : Nat :=\n  max (bhksBound f) (ZPoly.defaultFactorCoeffBound f)\n\nprivate def factorFastWithBound (f : ZPoly) (B : Nat) : Option Factorization :=\n  (factorFastFactorsWithBound f B).map (factorizationOfFactors f)\n\n/--\nPublic van Hoeij CLD fast path with a combined BHKS/Mignotte precision cap.\n\nThe bounded core loop only accepts candidates certified by the fixed-precision\nBHKS recovery pipeline; if every precision up to the cap misses, this reports\n`none` so the public `factor` combinator can use the slow backstop.\n-/\ndef factorFast (f : ZPoly) : Option Factorization :=\n  factorFastWithBound f (factorFastPrecisionCap f)\n\n#guard (factorFast (DensePoly.ofCoeffs #[1, 1, 1, 1, 1])).map Factorization.product =\n  some (DensePoly.ofCoeffs #[1, 1, 1, 1, 1])\n\n#guard factorFastWithBound (DensePoly.ofCoeffs #[1, 0, 0, 0, 1]) 4 = none\n\n/-- Factor with an explicit coefficient bound for the recombination stage. -/\ndef factorWithBound (f : ZPoly) (B : Nat) : Factorization :=\n  (factorFastWithBound f B).getD (factorSlowWithBound f B)\n\n#guard Factorization.product (factorWithBound cldGuardF 1) = cldGuardF\n\n/--\nFactor using the Mignotte-bounded fast attempt with exhaustive slow fallback.\n\nThe standalone `factorFast` entry point exposes the proof-facing combined\nBHKS/Mignotte cap. The default total factorization combinator keeps the\nruntime-oriented coefficient bound before falling back to exhaustive\nrecombination, so irreducible inputs that split modulo the chosen prime do not\nforce the full BHKS threshold search.\n-/\ndef factor (f : ZPoly) : Factorization :=\n  factorWithBound f (ZPoly.defaultFactorCoeffBound f)\n\nnamespace ZPoly\n\n/-- Mathlib-free irreducibility predicate for integer polynomials. -/\nclass Irreducible (f : ZPoly) : Prop where\n  not_zero : f ≠ 0\n  not_unit : ¬ ZPoly.IsUnit f\n  no_factors :\n    ∀ a b : ZPoly, f = a * b → ZPoly.IsUnit a ∨ ZPoly.IsUnit b\n\nprivate def isNatPrime (n : Nat) : Bool :=\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/9ff63b88-275c-44d1-a920-12002e2f74f3.stdout:200:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_112","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc 'coordination read-issue 3757 --json body --jq .body'","aggregated_output":"## Current state\n\nParent #3530 asks for the BHKS B7 bridge from `projectedRowSpanInt L = trueFactorIndicatorLattice trueSupports` to the executable `bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators` output. Inspection showed this is broader than a local constructor: the project first needs the generic executable RREF row-span bridge in #3756, then a BZ-specific theorem about column signatures of the RREF of the projected-row matrix.\n\nParent: #3530\n\ndepends-on: #3756\n\n## Deliverables\n\n1. In `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean` and/or `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Lattice.lean`, add a proof-facing definition of the expected B7 indicator array determined by `trueSupports` that matches the executable array-of-0/1 shape used by `Hex.bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators`.\n2. Prove a BZ-specific bridge: if `projectedRowSpanInt L = trueFactorIndicatorLattice trueSupports`, then the column-signature grouping performed by `Hex.bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators L` returns exactly that expected indicator array.\n3. Use #3756's completed row-span bridge to reason about the executable `Matrix.rref` row space. Add only narrow local lemmas for integer-to-rational row-space transport or signature/class membership if they are missing.\n4. Add `EquivalenceClassRecoveryHypotheses.ofIndicatorLattice` (or an equivalently named constructor) that fills `indicators_match` from `lattice_eq_indicators` using the B7 bridge.\n5. Do not add `axiom`, use `native_decide`, or introduce new theorem-level `sorry`.\n\n## Library placement\n\nTarget files:\n\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean`\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Lattice.lean` if helper lattice statements are needed\n- `HexMatrixMathlib/RankSpanNullspace.lean` only for tiny follow-on bridge lemmas after #3756\n\nGoverning SPEC quote from `SPEC/Libraries/hex-berlekamp-zassenhaus.md`:\n\"B7. Equivalence-class identification given `L' = W` (BHKS Lemma 3.3). When `L' = W`, the rref + equivalence-class procedure produces exactly the indicator vectors of irreducible-factor subsets.\"\n\n## Context\n\nRead:\n\n- Parent issue #3530.\n- Prerequisite #3756.\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean` around `bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators`, `bhksColumnSignature`, and `bhksClassIndicator`.\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean` around `EquivalenceClassRecoveryHypotheses`.\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Lattice.lean` around `projectedRowSpanInt` and `trueFactorIndicatorLattice`.\n- `HexMatrix/RREF.lean` and `HexMatrixMathlib/RankSpanNullspace.lean` after #3756 lands.\n- `PLAN/Phase5.md` and `PLAN/Conventions.md`.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.Recovery`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`\n- `lake build HexMatrixMathlib.RankSpanNullspace` if touched\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- `git diff --check`\n- `rg -n '^axiom\\\\b|native_decide|TODO|FIXME|sorry' HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Lattice.lean HexMatrixMathlib/RankSpanNullspace.lean`\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- A2 centred-residue reconstruction.\n- Final `factorFast` termination/non-none leaf.\n- Changing executable BZ recovery or RREF algorithms.\n- Editing `SPEC/`, top-level `PLAN.md`, or top-level `AGENTS.md`.\n\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/fcea758e-ba06-450a-91cd-b3e6865a5a16.stdout:14:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_6","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc 'for f in $(ls -1 progress | sort | tail -5); do printf '\"'\\\\n### progress/%s\\\\n' \\\"\"'$f\"; sed -n '\"'1,220p' \\\"progress/\"'$f\"; done'","aggregated_output":"\n### progress/20260618_172129_17ba12cd.md\n# Progress — 2026-06-18 (session 17ba12cd, /work → /feature #7957)\n\n## Accomplished\n\nCompleted both deliverables of #7957 (HexLLL Phase 6 `@[grind]` automation\ncoverage for `Basic.lean`), confined to `HexLLL/Basic.lean`.\n\n### `@[grind =]` annotation pass\n\nAdded `@[grind =]` to the public characterising lemmas whose statements are\nuseful rewrites for automated reasoning:\n\n- Lattice-preservation iff family (LLLState): `swapStep_memLattice_iff`,\n  `sizeReduceColumn_memLattice_iff`, `sizeReduce_memLattice_iff`,\n  `lllLoop_memLattice_iff`.\n- Lattice-preservation iff family (SteeredState): `reduceColumn_memLattice_iff`,\n  `sizeReduce_memLattice_iff`, `swap_memLattice_iff`, `prep_memLattice_iff`,\n  `loop_memLattice_iff`, `finalSweep_memLattice_iff`, `steeredReduce_memLattice_iff`.\n- Structural equations: `sizeReduce_d` (`(s.sizeReduce k).d = s.d`),\n  `sizeReduce_basis`.\n- Upgraded the two existing `@[simp]` projection lemmas `init_b` and\n  `refreshRow_b` to `@[simp, grind =]`.\n\nAll of these are strictly term-reducing rewrites (`memLattice (op s).b v ↔\nmemLattice s.b v`, `(op s).d = s.d`), so the rule set is terminating — no\n`grind`-loop risk. Skipped `swapStep_ν_eq` / `swapStep_d_eq`: both carry a\n`(s.swapStep k).Valid` side hypothesis that `grind` would have to discharge,\nmaking them conditional rewrites with no clear payoff and a loop/cost risk.\n\n### Payoff demonstration\n\nWith the component iff lemmas tagged, two chained proofs collapse to `grind`:\n\n- `prep_memLattice_iff`: `unfold prep; rw [...]; split <;> simp [...]` →\n  `unfold prep; grind`.\n- `steeredReduce_memLattice_iff`: `unfold steeredReduce; rw [finalSweep_…,\n  loop_…, init_b]` → `unfold steeredReduce; grind`.\n\nThe remaining iff proofs (`loop_memLattice_iff`, `finalSweep_memLattice_iff`)\nare inductions, so they keep their explicit structure; the annotations still pay\noff as downstream `grind` availability for consuming layers and for these two\nin-file simplifications.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexLLL` → 399 jobs, `Build completed successfully`. `HexLLL.Basic`\n  builds in 2.6s (was 2.7s pre-change) — no annotation slowdown.\n- No `warning`/`error`/`sorry` in the build log.\n- `git diff --check` clean; no added `sorry`/`axiom`/`native_decide`/`TODO`/\n  `FIXME`.\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py` → exit 0.\n- Confined to `HexLLL/Basic.lean`: no `libraries.yml` `done_through` bump, no\n  `*Mathlib` bridge touched, no new imports.\n\n## Current frontier\n\nHexLLL Phase 6 `grind`-annotation surface for `Basic.lean` is covered. The\nlattice-preservation rewrites are now visible to `grind` in consuming layers.\n\n## Next step\n\nSibling Phase 6 `@[grind]` passes remain unclaimed: #7960 (HexGramSchmidt),\n#7961 (HexGFq).\n\n## Blockers\n\nNone.\n\nNote: worktree carries a pre-existing unrelated `.claude/skills/agent-worker-flow/SKILL.md`\nmodification from before this session; not touched or staged.\n\n### progress/20260618_656d42c5.md\n# Progress — 2026-06-18 (session 656d42c5, /work)\n\n## Accomplished\n\nPicked up via `/work`; exercised judgment across the unclaimed feature queue.\n\n### #7783 (executable per-member Berlekamp irreducibility) — diagnosed & skipped\n\nPremise-checked the issue's proof route and found it **impossible as stated**.\n`berlekampFactor` (`Factor.lean:114-118`) fixes the witness set to `f`'s kernel\n`(fixedSpaceKernel f hmonic).toList` and applies it to *every* factor; loop\ntermination gives, per factor `g`, `∀ w ∈ f-kernel, kernelWitnessSplit? g w =\nnone`. But the only completeness producer\n`irreducible_of_no_kernelWitnessSplit_squareFree` (`RabinSoundness.lean:2994`)\nrequires the factor's **own** kernel `(fixedSpaceKernel g _).toList`. No bridge\nbetween `f`'s kernel and a divisor's kernel exists in `HexBerlekamp/`. The\ntheorem is *true* (Berlekamp completeness) but needs a new generalized\ncompleteness theorem (CRT-lift of a g-kernel splitter into f's kernel +\nspan-mod-g linearity), not the thin per-factor composition. Posted evidence-rich\ncomment, `coordination skip 7783` (→ replan).\n\n### #7786 (BHKS fast-core forward-recovery via _of_lift) — deliverable 1 landed\n\n- **Deliverable 1 (landed):** added\n  `factorFastFactorsWithBound_raw_zpolyIrreducible_of_cut`\n  (`PartitionRefinement.lean`), the forward `factorFastFactorsWithBound`-level\n  raw-irreducibility wrapper with **no** `ForwardRecoveryInputs` param, routed\n  through `..._of_cut` (`:530`). Avoids the dead reverse `lattice_eq_indicators`\n  field (route-blocked behind #6779/#2564). Thin re-parameterization of the\n  `_of_forwardInputs` sibling (`:1010`).\n- **Deliverable 2 (blocked):** the producer feeding `_of_lift` needs the\n  per-support `∀ S, TrueFactorLift` family at first-success precision. Only the\n  single-subset constructors `trueFactorLiftOfSubset` /\n  `trueFactorLiftSemanticsOfToMonicSubset` (`LiftBridge.lean:78,104`) exist, and\n  they demand an exact `liftedFactorProduct d T = factor` witness per support —\n  the unproduced recovery/coverage direction. Every `∀ S, TrueFactorLift` is a\n  hypothesis, never a conclusion. Diagnosed on the issue with the missing-lemma\n  shape; partial PR.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.PartitionRefinement` → built the full\n  CI-gated library (3777 jobs), `Build completed successfully`, 0 `error:`.\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py` → exit 0.\n- `git diff --check` clean; no `sorry`/`axiom`/`native_decide`/`TODO`/`FIXME` on\n  added lines.\n\n## Current frontier\n\nDeliverable 1's `_of_cut` wrapper is the furthest-forward fast-route piece; it\ncomposes the instant a deliverable-2 coverage producer exists.\n\n## Next step\n\nReplan deliverable 2 as its own issue gated on a coverage substrate: a producer\nconcluding `∀ S : trueSupports, BHKS.TrueFactorLift L S` (+ semantics +\nseparation) from the first-success recovery (the per-support exact\n`liftedFactorProduct d T = factor`). An *unconditional* form (for #6672) also\nneeds the scheduled-loop determinism `hcore` (BHKS precision-soundness, not in\ntree). For #7783: replan as a generalized completeness theorem\n`irreducible_of_no_kernelWitnessSplit_squareFree_of_dvd` + thin loop assembly.\n\n## Blockers\n\n- #7783: missing generalized completeness theorem (witness-set bridge).\n- #7786 deliverable 2: missing per-support coverage producer; unconditional form\n  additionally blocked on scheduled-loop determinism.\n\nNote: worktree carries pre-existing unrelated `.claude/*` modifications from\nbefore this session; not touched or staged.\n\n### progress/20260618_6b2951a8.md\n# Progress — 2026-06-18 (session 6b2951a8, /work → /feature #7924)\n\n## Accomplished\n\nDelivered the reusable recovered-lift Hensel-semantics producers for the monic\n`toMonicLiftData` coordinate (#7924), in `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/LiftBridge.lean`.\n\nThree additive theorems, all derived from the `toMonicPrimeData?` selection\nwitness rather than re-bundled as caller hypotheses:\n\n- `toMonicLiftData_two_le_p`: `2 ≤ (toMonicLiftData core B primeData).p` — the\n  `hp` side condition. From `toMonicPrimeData?_prime`.\n- `toMonicLiftData_one_lt_modulus`: `1 < p ^ k` — the `hk` side condition. From\n  the prime lower bound plus `1 ≤ precisionForCoeffBound`.\n- `toMonicLiftData_liftedFactor_hensel_semantics` (deliverable 2): for every\n  lifted-factor index `i`, `∃ g, Monic (liftedFactor d i) ∧ 0 < degree ∧\n  congr (toMonic core).monic (liftedFactor d i * g) (p ^ k)`. This is exactly\n  the per-index `hfac` body of\n  `factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_recoveredLift`. The\n  complement `g` is `liftedFactorProduct d (univ \\ {i})`; the congruence is the\n  per-index specialisation of\n  `henselLiftData_liftedFactorProduct_subset_complement_congr_core`, factored\n  out of the `selected_congr` field of `trueFactorLiftSemanticsOfToMonicSubset`.\n  Monicity / positive degree reuse the existing\n  `toMonicLiftData_liftedFactor_monic_of_monicPrimeData` /\n  `_natDegree_pos_of_monicPrimeData`.\n\nThe remaining two endpoint side conditions are already one-liners in tree and\nneed no new lemma: `hf_lc` (`leadingCoeff (lift S).f = 1`) is the monicity of\n`(toMonic core).monic` (`toMonic_monic_isMonic_of_pos_degree`, since `Monic` is\ndefeq `leadingCoeff = 1`); `hfactor_monic` (`(toPolynomial (lift S).factor).Monic`)\nis `HexHenselMathlib.toPolynomial_monic_of_dense_monic` applied to the recovered\nfactor's monicity, which the family producer\n`recoveredLift_family_of_indicatorCandidates` (#7913) already establishes\ninternally (`hcl_monic`). These are family-producer/`#7921`-wiring facts, not\n`toMonicPrimeData?`-derived, so they live at the integration site.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.LiftBridge` → green.\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib` → 3790 jobs, `Build completed\n  successfully`, 0 errors (includes the `PartitionRefinement` endpoint module).\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py` → DAG OK.\n- `git diff -U0 … | grep` → no added `sorry`/`axiom`/`native_decide`/`TODO`/`FIXME`.\n- `git diff --check` clean.\n\n## Current frontier\n\nThe lifted-factor and modulus side conditions of the fast `h_raw` disjunct\nendpoint are now producible from `toMonicPrimeData?` + core facts, independent of\nthe candidate fold.\n\n## Next step\n\n#7921 (parent): wire `recoveredLift_family_of_indicatorCandidates` into\n`factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_recoveredLift`, using\n`toMonicLiftData_liftedFactor_hensel_semantics` for `hfac`, the two modulus\nlemmas for `hp`/`hk`, and the in-tree monicity bridges for `hf_lc`/`hfactor_monic`.\nThe remaining substantive bridge is #7923 (emitted-indicator support family →\nsubtype-indexed `trueSupports`).\n\n## Blockers\n\nNone for this issue.\n\nNote: worktree carries a pre-existing unrelated `.claude/skills/agent-worker-flow/SKILL.md`\nmodification from before this session; not touched or staged.\n\n### progress/20260618_a19c7e12.md\n# Progress — 2026-06-18 (session a19c7e12, /work → /feature)\n\n## Accomplished\n\n### #7821 (HexGF2Mathlib proof-only Phase 4 completion) — landed\n\nPremise-checked then executed the proof-only Phase-4 bookkeeping bump for\n`HexGF2Mathlib` (`libraries.yml` `done_through` `3 → 4`).\n\n**Premise-check (deliverable 1):** confirmed the bridge files expose no\nseparable executable computational surface that the `HexGF2` bench fails to\nattribute. Every `def`/`instance` in `HexGF2Mathlib/Basic.lean` and\n`HexGF2Mathlib/Field.lean` is correspondence plumbing whose domain/codomain\nis a Mathlib-side type: `toFpPoly`/`ofFpPoly`/`equiv` bridge the executable\npacked `Hex.GF2Poly` to `Hex.FpPoly 2`; `toNat`/`ofNatBelowDegree` give a\nfinite index \"without changing the executable `HexGF2` representation\" (their\nown docstring); `toGeneric`/`ofGeneric`/`modulusFpPoly`/`reducedPackedRep*`/\n`finEquiv` bridge `Hex.GF2n`/`Hex.GF2nPoly` to the Mathlib `GenericFiniteField`\nconstruction and `Fin (2 ^ n)`. The GF(2) arithmetic kernel (packed-word\nadd/mul, field ops) lives in `HexGF2` (`done_through: 5`) and is benched by\n`hexgf2_bench` (`lakefile.lean`, root `HexGF2Bench`). `lakefile.lean` carries\nonly `lean_lib HexGF2Mathlib` (no bench exe) with the explicit comment that\nMathlib-side libraries are proof-only with no computational kernel to bench.\nPremise holds — same shape as the merged precedents #5397, #7808, #7809.\n\n**Deliverable 2:** single-line bump, no other library entry touched.\n\nThis also unblocks `HexGFqMathlib` Phase 4 (was waiting on\n`HexGF2Mathlib.done_through >= 4`); it now appears in the `status.py` Ready\nlist.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `python3 scripts/status.py HexGF2Mathlib` → `done_through: 4`, advanced to\n  Phase 5, `ready: yes`.\n- `python3 scripts/status.py` → exit 0; `HexGFqMathlib` now Ready (Phase 4),\n  no new blocked regressions.\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py` → exit 0.\n- `git diff --check` clean; diff touches only the single `HexGF2Mathlib`\n  `done_through` line (plus this progress file).\n\n## Current frontier\n\n`HexGFqMathlib` Phase 4 is the natural next bookkeeping completion (now\nunblocked); it is the last sibling in the GF cluster awaiting this bump.\n\n## Next step\n\nClaim `HexGFqMathlib` Phase 4 (no open issue yet — would need planning) or\nthe remaining proof-only bumps #7819 (HexPolyZMathlib), #7820 (HexLLLMathlib).\n\n## Blockers\n\nNone for this item.\n\n### progress/20260618_ff4697df.md\n# Progress — 2026-06-18 (session ff4697df, /work → /feature #7802)\n\n## Accomplished\n\nCompleted both deliverables of #7802 (Berlekamp factor irreducibility for\nnon-monic outputs), executable side only (`HexBerlekamp/`).\n\n### No-split transport across a unit scaling (`HexBerlekamp/Factor.lean`)\n\n`kernelWitnessSplit?_none_scale`: a failed Berlekamp split search is preserved\nwhen the factor being split is replaced by a unit scaling `scale c g` (`c ≠ 0`).\nSupporting machinery: mutually-dividing candidates share size/degree\n(`size_eq_of_dvd_dvd`), so `isNontrivialSplitFactor` agrees on associates\n(`isNontrivialSplitFactor_eq_of_associate`), and the split candidates of `g` and\n`scale c g` are associates for every witness/constant\n(`splitFactorAt_dvd_dvd_scale`). The aux-level transport is by induction on\nfuel.\n\n### Non-monic substrate (`HexBerlekamp/RabinSoundness.lean`)\n\n`irreducible_of_no_kernelWitnessSplit_squareFree_of_dvd_nonmonic`: drops the\nmonicity hypothesis on the divisor of #7788 to bare `g ≠ 0`. Positive-degree\ndivisors normalize to their monic associate `scale (leadingCoeff g)⁻¹ g`, to\nwhich #7788 applies after transporting the no-split fact; a nonzero constant is\nirreducible by definition.\n\n### Loop fixpoint + capstone\n\nIn `Factor.lean`: `berlekampFactorLoop_terminates_or_full` (the loop either\nreaches a `splitFirstFactor? = none` fixpoint or grows by exactly `fuel`),\n`factorProduct_length_le_degree` (positive-degree factors ⇒ length ≤ degree of\nproduct), and `kernelWitnessSplit?_none_of_berlekampFactor_factors` (the\n`f.size + 1` fuel always suffices, since each split adds a positive-degree\nfactor and the product is preserved as `f`, capping the count at `deg f`). Plus\n`berlekampFactor_factors_dvd`. In `RabinSoundness.lean`:\n`berlekampFactor_factors_irreducible` assembles nonzero + divides-`f` + no-split\nthrough the non-monic substrate.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekamp` → 478 jobs, `Build completed successfully`, no\n  `error`/`warning`.\n- `grep -c sorry HexBerlekamp/RabinSoundness.lean` → 0 (unchanged).\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py` → exit 0.\n- `git diff --check` clean; no added-line `sorry`/`axiom`/`native_decide`/\n  `TODO`/`FIXME`.\n\n## Current frontier\n\n`berlekampFactor_factors_irreducible` is the executable per-factor headline.\nThis unblocks #7799 (the Mathlib Berlekamp factor irreducibility bridge).\n\n## Next step\n\n#7799: discharge the `HexBerlekampMathlib` bridge consuming the executable\nresult.\n\n## Blockers\n\nNone for this issue.\n\nNote: worktree carries pre-existing unrelated `.claude/*` modifications from\nbefore this session; not touched or staged.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

sessions/fcea758e-ba06-450a-91cd-b3e6865a5a16.stdout:43:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_22","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"pod _filter-trusted-issues --label agent-plan --state open --limit 20 --json number,title,body --jq '.[] | \\\"### #\\\\(.number) \\\\(.title)\\\\n\\\\(.body)\\\\n\\\"'\"","aggregated_output":"### #7973 feat: HexConway Phase 6 grind automation coverage for Basic.lean\nlibrary: HexConway\n\n## Current state\n\n`HexConway` sits at `libraries.yml` `done_through: 5` and is in Phase 6\n(proof polishing). `PLAN/Phase6.md` calls out \"Appropriate automation\nannotations\" explicitly: *\"`@[grind]` for automated reasoning. Maximize\nwhat `simp` and `grind` can close automatically.\"*\n\nA scan confirms the gap is real and isolated to this library's main\nfile:\n\n- `HexConway/Basic.lean` (3826 lines, 116 top-level\n  `theorem`/`def`/`lemma`): **1** `@[grind]` annotation; ~30\n  `@[simp]` annotations on the lookup-table fixture lemmas\n  (`luebeckConwayPolynomial?_hit_*`, `_monic`, `_degree_pos` from\n  `Basic.lean:96` onward).\n- The characterising facts of this library are the per-`(p, n)`\n  Conway-table ground lemmas: the lookup-hit equalities\n  (`luebeckConwayPolynomial?_hit_2_1` at `:96`, `_hit_2_2` at `:319`,\n  …), the monic/degree-positive projections (`_monic`/`_degree_pos`),\n  and the irreducibility headlines (`luebeckConwayPolynomial_2_1_irreducible`\n  at `:291`, the `_p_n_irreducible` family through `:1985+`). These are\n  concrete rewrite/closing facts a downstream `grind` call would want.\n- `@[simp, grind =]` lemma annotations are standard in sibling\n  Mathlib-free executable layers (`HexMatrix/Basic.lean`,\n  `HexModArith/Basic.lean`, `HexGFqField/Basic.lean`), so the missing\n  piece is the lemma-level `@[grind]` annotations.\n\nThis is a sibling of the recently-landed `Basic.lean` `@[grind]`\npasses — #7952 (HexPolyZ), #7955 (HexPolyFp), #7957 (HexLLL),\n#7960 (HexGramSchmidt) — the same pass on a different library's\n`Basic.lean`; they are independent and run in parallel.\n\n## Deliverables\n\n1. **`@[grind]` audit of `HexConway/Basic.lean`.** For each public\n   characterising lemma whose statement is a useful rewrite or closing\n   fact for automated reasoning, add `@[grind]` (or `@[simp, grind =]`\n   where both fire) following the local conventions in\n   `HexModArith/Basic.lean` / `HexMatrix/Basic.lean` / `HexGFqField/Basic.lean`\n   — the lookup-hit and `_monic`/`_degree_pos`/`_irreducible` ground\n   facts are the obvious candidates as `@[grind =]`/default. Do **not**\n   blanket-annotate: only lemmas that genuinely help `grind` make\n   progress, with no `grind`-loop or performance regression.\n\n2. **Demonstrate the annotations pay off.** Where an existing proof in\n   `Basic.lean` can now be shortened to `by grind` / `by simp; grind`\n   or have a manual rewrite chain replaced, do so — but only when it\n   genuinely simplifies and stays green. If no proof shortens, that is\n   acceptable; record briefly in the progress file why the annotations\n   are still justified (downstream `grind` availability) rather than\n   forcing a contrived rewrite.\n\n3. **No scope creep.** This issue is the `grind` automation pass for\n   `Basic.lean` only. Do **not** bump `libraries.yml` `done_through`\n   (the library has other Phase 6 surface; the bump is a separate\n   exit-criteria audit), and do **not** touch any `*Mathlib` bridge\n   library.\n\n## Context\n\nRelevant files:\n\n- `HexConway/Basic.lean` — primary target (annotation pass).\n- `PLAN/Phase6.md` — \"Appropriate automation annotations\" criterion.\n- `SPEC/Libraries/hex-conway.md` — library spec.\n- Style references: `HexModArith/Basic.lean`, `HexMatrix/Basic.lean`,\n  `HexGFqField/Basic.lean` (existing `@[simp, grind =]` usage in the\n  Mathlib-free layer).\n\n`HexConway` is the Mathlib-free executable Conway-polynomial lookup-table\nlayer (`libraries.yml` `mathlib: false`, deps `HexBerlekamp`); `grind`/\n`simp` are available here but heavier Mathlib tactics are not (see the\n`hex-lean-mathlib-boundary` skill). Keep the file Mathlib-free: no new\nimports pulling in Mathlib.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexConway` green on the pinned toolchain.\n- No new `sorry`/`axiom`/`native_decide`/`TODO`/`FIXME`\n  (`git diff --check` clean).\n- The linter / `#lint`-equivalent stays clean on the library (no new\n  linter warnings introduced by the annotations).\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py` exits 0.\n- If any proof was shortened, confirm it still builds; if a `@[grind]`\n  annotation causes a loop or slowdown, drop it rather than keep it.\n\n\n### #7970 feat: HexModArith Phase 6 grind automation coverage for Basic.lean\nlibrary: HexModArith\n\n## Current state\n\n`HexModArith` sits at `libraries.yml` `done_through: 5` and is in\nPhase 6 (proof polishing). `PLAN/Phase6.md` calls out \"Appropriate\nautomation annotations\" explicitly: *\"`@[grind]` for automated\nreasoning. Maximize what `simp` and `grind` can close automatically.\"*\n\nA scan of `HexModArith/Basic.lean` (923 lines) shows the convention is\nalready live but unevenly applied:\n\n- The `toNat_*` arithmetic-characterising block already carries the\n  target form `@[simp, grind =]`: `toNat_zero` (`:521`), `toNat_one`\n  (`:526`), `toNat_add` (`:530`), `toNat_sub` (`:590`), `toNat_mul`\n  (`:640`), `toNat_pow` (`:845`). sessions/513bf41c-1dcd-4ff3-b55a-1e95a83fa3e1.stdout:88:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_46","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' plans/513bf41c-1.md; sed -n '1,220p' plans/513bf41c-2.md\"","aggregated_output":"## Current state\n\nHuman-oversight issue #2564 requires the Berlekamp-Zassenhaus recombination path to use the BHKS all-coefficients CLD lattice rather than the old additive-coefficient lattice. The current `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean` now contains the executable CLD surface (`centeredResiduePow`, `psiCut`, `cldCoeffs`, `bhksLatticeBasis`, `bhksProjectedRows`, equivalence-class indicators, and `bhksRecover?`), plus guards around several representative cases.\n\nOpen work already covers the constructor-specific lattice independence proof (#3185 / PR #3476), Factorization product and irreducibility tails (#3087, #3304, #2833), adversarial conformance (#3500), and benchmark follow-up (#3483). What is not separately covered is a focused HO-1 review of the executable CLD surface against the ten SPEC pitfalls and the BHKS shape it is meant to implement.\n\nSupports: #2564\n\n## Deliverables\n\n1. Audit `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean` against `SPEC/Libraries/hex-berlekamp-zassenhaus.md` \"Fast path: van Hoeij CLD lattice\", \"Recovery procedure\", \"Precision schedule\", and \"Pitfalls\" for the executable CLD/recovery path only.\n2. Add narrow, low-risk corrections found by the audit, such as missing `#guard`s, local docstring clarifications, or small executable checks that pin centred-residue `Psi`, all-coefficients lattice shape, rref/equivalence-class recovery, and fast-path no-fallback behavior.\n3. If the audit finds a substantive algorithmic mismatch rather than a narrow fix, do not rewrite the algorithm in this issue; file a follow-up `agent-plan` issue with the evidence and leave this issue's PR as an audit note or small guard-only change.\n\n## Library placement\n\nTarget file: `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`.\n\nGoverning SPEC quote from `SPEC/Libraries/hex-berlekamp-zassenhaus.md`: \"An additive-coefficient lattice that decodes short vectors as `Σ λ_i g_i (mod p^a)` candidate polynomials is not van Hoeij and is not admissible.\"\n\nQ1: `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean` owns the Mathlib-free executable CLD lattice, recovery helpers, and public fast/slow/combinator factorization path named by the BZ SPEC.\n\nQ2: no Mathlib strategy is needed for this audit slice; it is checking executable definitions and guards in the Mathlib-free core, not proving Group B bridge theorems.\n\nQ3: Mathlib has no implementation of Hex's project-local BHKS CLD executable surface.\n\nQ4: no missing infrastructure is expected for guard/doc/audit fixes; constructor-specific independence remains out of scope and is covered by #3185 / PR #3476.\n\n## Context\n\nRead:\n\n- Human-oversight issue #2564.\n- `SPEC/Libraries/hex-berlekamp-zassenhaus.md`, especially \"Fast path: van Hoeij CLD lattice\", \"Recovery procedure\", \"Precision schedule\", and \"Pitfalls\".\n- `PLAN/Conventions.md`.\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean` around `centeredResiduePow`, `psiCut`, `cldCoeffs`, `bhksLatticeBasis`, `bhksProjectedRows`, `bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators`, `bhksIndicatorCandidates?`, `bhksRecover?`, `factorFastWithBound`, `factorFast`, and `factor`.\n- PR #3476 only for awareness of the independence-proof branch; do not duplicate its deliverables.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus.Basic`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- `git diff --check`\n- `rg -n '^axiom\\b|native_decide|TODO|FIXME|sorry' HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean` shows no new banned markers and no new proof holes.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Proving or replacing `bhksLatticeBasis_independent`; that is #3185 / PR #3476.\n- Proving `factor_product_of_bound`, `ZPoly.isIrreducible_iff`, or Mathlib bridge factorization capstones.\n- Adding HO-2 adversarial conformance fixtures; #3500 is already open with a PR.\n- Benchmark compare/profile work; use the dedicated Phase 4 issues for that.\n- Editing `SPEC/`, top-level `PLAN.md`, or top-level `AGENTS.md`.\n## Current state\n\nHuman-oversight issue #2566 keeps the HexBerlekampZassenhaus Phase 4 benchmark evidence on the critical path. The repository now has `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Bench.lean`, `hexbz_bench`, `libraries.yml` Phase 4 input-family metadata, and `reports/hex-berlekamp-zassenhaus-performance.md`.\n\nThe current report still records a profiling gap: the sampled Firefox Profiler artefact retained address-only frames, so the report could not provide the required leaf-cost category split or inclusive `Hex.BerlekampZassenhaus.*` function ranking from `SPEC/profiling.md`. Open issue #3483 is already covering the separate internal fast/slow/public compare-domain gap; this issue should only handle the profile-symbolication/report concern.\n\nSupports: #2566\n\n## Deliverables\n\n1. Produce one representative symbolicated profile for at least the `degree-height-matrix` BZ input family, using the existing `hexbz_bench profile` path or a documented equivalent that yields demangled Lean function names.\n2. Update `reports/hex-berlekamp-zassenhaus-performance.md` with the required profile summary: reproducible command, commit/hardware, leaf-cost categories, inclusive-cost ranking of `Hex.BerlekampZassenhaus.*` functions, and a short dominant-cost narrative.\n3. Narrow or remove only the profile-specific concern in the report if the new data satisfies `SPEC/profiling.md`; leave the compare-domain and verdict/schedule concerns for #3483 or later dedicated issues.\n\n## Library placement\n\nTarget files:\n\n- `reports/hex-berlekamp-zassenhaus-performance.md`\n- optional small helper/script changes only if the existing profile command cannot produce symbolicated Lean names\n\nGoverning SPEC quote from `SPEC/profiling.md`: \"For each profiled case, the headline report's §Profile subsection records: Leaf cost categorisation ... Inclusive-cost ranking of Lean functions in the library's namespace ... Dominant-cost narrative.\"\n\nQ1: the headline report is the required Phase 4 evidence file for HexBerlekampZassenhaus profiling.\n\nQ2: no Mathlib strategy is involved; this is benchmark/profile reporting and optional tooling around compiled benchmark artefacts.\n\nQ3: not applicable to Mathlib; the deliverable is project-local profiling evidence.\n\nQ4: missing infrastructure is possible if the current `samply`/symbolication pipeline still cannot demangle Lean names on the worker host. If so, file a follow-up tooling issue with the exact blocker and keep this issue scoped to the BZ report update.\n\n## Context\n\nRead:\n\n- Human-oversight issue #2566.\n- `SPEC/profiling.md`.\n- `SPEC/benchmarking.md`, especially the headline report and attribution sections.\n- `PLAN/Phase4.md`.\n- `PLAN/Conventions.md`, especially audit-found issue handling.\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Bench.lean`.\n- `reports/hex-berlekamp-zassenhaus-performance.md`.\n- #3483 for the compare-domain scope that this issue must not duplicate.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake exe hexbz_bench list`\n- `lake exe hexbz_bench verify`\n- Re-run the documented profile command, or record why the host lacks the profiler and what artefact was used instead.\n- `git diff --check`\n- If any Lean or script files are touched: `python3 scripts/check_dag.py` and relevant `lake build`/`lake exe` checks.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Adding the internal compare group or shared scientific comparison domain; that is #3483.\n- Making every inconclusive benchmark verdict consistent.\n- Expanding all BZ scientific schedules.\n- Closing #2566; only the project owner closes human-oversight issues.\n- Editing `SPEC/`, top-level `PLAN.md`, or top-level `AGENTS.md`.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/74aad6dd-c491-4e7c-a048-5059e6f195c2.stdout:24:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_12","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' progress/20260510T180611Z_778bc6bc.md\"","aggregated_output":"# 2026-05-10 18:06 UTC — feature session #3035\n\n## Accomplished\n\n- Claimed issue #3035 (HexBZ Mathlib forward-verification bridge) and reset\n  the agent worktree onto `origin/main`.\n- Lifted `private` on `bhksIndicatorOneCount`, `bhksIndicatorAllOnes`,\n  `bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition`, `bhksIndicatorCandidates?`, and the\n  `bhksLatticeBasis_independent` proof in `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`\n  so the new bridge can name them. Added doc comments while exposing them;\n  executable definitions are unchanged.\n- Created `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean` with the SPEC\n  Group D forward-verification bridge:\n  * Helpers `latticeBasisOfLiftData`, `HasPositiveDimension`,\n    `projectedRowsOfLiftData`, `equivalenceClassIndicatorsOfLiftData` extract\n    the executable `Hex.bhksRecover?` building blocks at any\n    `(f, d : LiftData)`.\n  * `EquivalenceClassRecoveryHypotheses` and the matching theorems\n    `bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators_eq_of_recovery` /\n    `bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators_projectedRows_eq_of_recovery` package\n    BHKS Lemma 3.3 / B7 as the bridge from `L' = W` to the executable\n    indicator output (deliverable 1).\n  * `RecoveryHypotheses` packages the four guards inside `Hex.bhksRecover?`\n    (rows positivity, non-degenerate partition, per-indicator\n    reconstruction, exact product), and `bhksRecover_eq_some_of_recovery`\n    proves the executable returns `some <expected factors>` under those\n    guards. The proof unfolds `Hex.bhksRecover?` and reduces each branch\n    via `dif_pos` / `simp only`.\n  * `ForwardRecoveryInputs` is the SPEC-input layer: it explicitly carries\n    `lattice_eq_indicators` (the conclusion shape of #3034's cap separation\n    theorem), `mignotte_precision`\n    (`p^k > 2 · defaultFactorCoeffBound f`), the abstract B7 `indicators_match`,\n    and the abstract A2 `candidates_eq` and `product_eq` obligations.\n    `ForwardRecoveryInputs.toRecoveryHypotheses` promotes the SPEC bundle to\n    `RecoveryHypotheses`, and `bhksRecover_eq_some_of_forwardInputs` is the\n    headline theorem (deliverable 2).\n- Wired `Recovery` into `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.lean`.\n- Verified `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`,\n  `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus`, `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`, and\n  `git diff --check`. No new `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, `FIXME`, or\n  `sorry` introduced; the only sorries on the touched lines are pre-existing\n  in `bhksLatticeBasis_independent` and elsewhere.\n\n## Current frontier\n\nThe bridge is intentionally abstract over the residual SPEC obligations:\n\n- B7 (`indicators_match`): the executable equivalence-class indicators\n  match a target list under `L' = W`. Discharging this depends on\n  executable `Matrix.rref` correctness (HexMatrix Phase 5) plus a finite\n  case analysis of the BHKS Lemma 3.3 signature partition.\n- A2 (`candidates_eq`): the centred-residue lift recovers integer\n  coefficients exactly under `p^k > 2 · defaultFactorCoeffBound f`. This\n  is the Mignotte / Hensel reconstruction obligation; it consumes the\n  Mignotte bound already in `HexPolyZMathlib/Mignotte.lean`.\n- `product_eq` and `nondegenerate`: structural consequences once B7 + A2\n  land.\n\n## Next step\n\nOpen a sub-issue (or task in PLAN/) tracking the discharge of `B7`\n(`indicators_match`) once HexMatrix Phase 5 RREF correctness lands, and a\ncompanion sub-issue for A2 (`candidates_eq`) using the Mignotte coefficient\nbound from `HexPolyZMathlib/Mignotte.lean`. A subsequent issue can then\nchain `bhksRecover_eq_some_of_forwardInputs` into the outer\nprecision-doubling loop (`factorFastCoreWithBound`) toward the final\n`factorFast_terminates` theorem (#2567).\n\n## Blockers\n\nNone for this session — the issue's deliverables were narrowly scoped to a\nsingle precision/recovery call, and #3034 (cap separation) has already\nlanded. Future bridge work is tracked above.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/c5d768db-867a-437b-8358-9b054311e5b8.stdout:13:**What I found:** #7594's deliverable — compose true-factor norm bound ⟹ survivor-span ⟹ project to first `r` coords ⟹ `indicatorVector S ∈ projectedRowSpanInt = CutProjectionHypotheses` — is *exactly* `cutProjectionHypotheses_of_trueFactors` (`HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Lattice.lean:1443`), which landed via **#7640 / PR #7658** (commit `1b076e3c`), sorry/axiom/native_decide-free, and correctly avoids the unsound `CutRetention` route the two prior skips flagged. The forward-count consumer (`factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs`, `PartitionRefinement.lean:613`) takes `CutProjectionHypotheses` directly, so no narrow wrapper is owed by this issue.

sessions/162c0d33-7c45-462c-9c13-97eac8d960c1.stdout:12:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_5","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' progress/20260617_aad44673.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Progress - 2026-06-17 (session aad44673, one-shot #7592)\n\n## Accomplished\n\nClaimed #7592 (feat: add BHKS cut-separation prefix certificate, critical-path)\nvia `pod once`. Split into its two staged deliverables: the executable prefix\ncut (\"lands first\") and the `CutRetention` proof. **Landed the executable\nprefix cut; rejected the `CutRetention` proof half as an unsound target** (same\ndefect #7575 already diagnosed, invariant under the prefix change), with a\nmachine-checked comment on the issue.\n\n### Executable prefix cut (landed)\n\n`HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`: added `bhksCutPrefixCount` (computes\n`t = (max passing GS index) + 1` by an increasing-order fold) and rewired\n`bhksCutProjectReducedRows` / `bhksRetainedRowIndices` to retain the contiguous\nprefix `b_0 … b_t` in original order, including earlier rows whose own GS norm\nexceeds the radius. This is the genuine BHKS Lemma 5.7 prefix and fixes the\nnon-prefix defect diagnosed on #7578 (per-row survivor set was not a GS-norm\nprefix under LLL).\n\nVerification:\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus` — clean.\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.Lattice` — clean (proof layer intact;\n  nothing constructs `CutRetention`, so the runtime change breaks no proof).\n- Regenerated `conformance-fixtures/HexBerlekampZassenhaus/bz.jsonl` via\n  `hexbz_emit_fixtures` — **byte-identical** to the committed flint-verified\n  ground truth. Factorization output is unchanged (survivor set only grows;\n  final factor multisets match flint).\n- Added-line forbidden-token scan: no sorry/axiom/native_decide/TODO/FIXME.\n\n### `CutRetention` proof half (rejected as unsound — premise defect)\n\n`CutRetention.mem_projected_of_norm_le` (`Lattice.lean:424`) quantifies over\n**arbitrary** `v : Fin r → ℤ` with only a norm bound and concludes membership in\nthe **projected** span `projectedRowSpanInt`. This is unsatisfiable — the same\ndefect #7575 posted (kernel-checked `IsEmpty (CutRetention P)`) — and the prefix\nchange does **not** repair it. Machine-checked under the new prefix code:\n`factorCount=1, coeffWidth=0, reduced=[[2]]` gives `cutRadiusSq4=4`,\n`bhksCutPrefixCount=0`, `bhksCutProjectReducedRows=#[]`, so\n`projectedRowSpanInt=⊥`, yet `v=![1]` has `‖v‖²=1 ≤ 4`. Membership in a\nsublattice never follows from a norm bound alone.\n\nThe issue's *Proof section* states the SOUND fact correctly (\"any\n**original-lattice** vector v with `‖v‖²≤radius` lies in the integer span of the\nretained prefix rows\"). The defect is only the packaging: the sound statement is\nabout lattice vectors of the `(r+n)`-dim reduced basis, not arbitrary `ℤ^r`\nvectors, and not the projected span. `CutRetention` drops the lattice hypothesis\nand projects — that is where soundness is lost.\n\n## Current frontier\n\nExecutable prefix cut is the substrate any sound survivor-span proof needs.\nLanded via `create-pr --partial` (issue → `replan`). Comment on #7592 gives the\ncorrected target.\n\n## Next step (for the replan)\n\nProve the full-lattice survivor-span lemma directly into `CutProjectionHypotheses`\n(NOT `CutRetention`, which should be retired along with\n`cutProjectionHypotheses_of_retention` / `..._of_retention`):\n- For `v` with `memLattice reducedMatrix v` and `‖v‖² ≤ R`, `v ∈` ℤ-span of the\n  prefix rows `b_0 … b_t`, via `basis_normSq` (gramDetVec ↔ `‖b*_i‖²`,\n  `HexGramSchmidtMathlib/Int.lean:1070`) and the integer-top-coefficient /\n  orthogonality lemmas (`normSq_latticeVec_ge_min_basis_normSq`,\n  `rowCombination_normSq_ge_of_orthogonal_coeff_sq_ge_one`,\n  `HexGramSchmidt/Int.lean`).\n- Then the factorization→lattice bridge #7578 listed as absent: true-support\n  indicators are projections of genuine short lattice vectors (CLD-vanishing for\n  true factors), so `CutProjectionHypotheses` holds at exactly the indicators it\n  is consumed at (`PartitionRefinement.lean:484`).\n\n## Blockers\n\nNone for the landed change. The proof half is blocked on a corrected,\nnon-`CutRetention` target (premise defect), routed to replan.\nNote: the worktree carries pre-existing unrelated `.claude/*` modifications from\nbefore this session; not touched or staged.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
