The defect is only the packaging: the sound statement is\nabout lattice vectors of the `(r+n)`-dim reduced basis, not arbitrary `ℤ^r`\nvectors, and not the projected span. `CutRetention` drops the lattice hypothesis\nand projects — that is where soundness is lost.\n\n## Current frontier\n\nExecutable prefix cut is the substrate any sound survivor-span proof needs.\nLanded via `create-pr --partial` (issue → `replan`). Comment on #7592 gives the\ncorrected target.\n\n## Next step (for the replan)\n\nProve the full-lattice survivor-span lemma directly into `CutProjectionHypotheses`\n(NOT `CutRetention`, which should be retired along with\n`cutProjectionHypotheses_of_retention` / `..._of_retention`):\n- For `v` with `memLattice reducedMatrix v` and `‖v‖² ≤ R`, `v ∈` ℤ-span of the\n  prefix rows `b_0 … b_t`, via `basis_normSq` (gramDetVec ↔ `‖b*_i‖²`,\n  `HexGramSchmidtMathlib/Int.lean:1070`) and the integer-top-coefficient /\n  orthogonality lemmas (`normSq_latticeVec_ge_min_basis_normSq`,\n  `rowCombination_normSq_ge_of_orthogonal_coeff_sq_ge_one`,\n  `HexGramSchmidt/Int.lean`).\n- Then the factorization→lattice bridge #7578 listed as absent: true-support\n  indicators are projections of genuine short lattice vectors (CLD-vanishing for\n  true factors), so `CutProjectionHypotheses` holds at exactly the indicators it\n  is consumed at (`PartitionRefinement.lean:484`).\n\n## Blockers\n\nNone for the landed change. The proof half is blocked on a corrected,\nnon-`CutRetention` target (premise defect), routed to replan.\nNote: the worktree carries pre-existing unrelated `.claude/*` modifications from\nbefore this session; not touched or staged.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/a49eee87-8386-4a95-acca-9a78b9195249.stdout:36:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_20","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"coordination read-issue 7765 --json title,labels,body --jq '{title:.title, labels:[.labels[].name], body:.body}'\"","aggregated_output":"{\"body\":\"library: HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib\\n\\n## Current state\\n\\nReplacement for #7760. The original issue asked for an unconditional theorem\\nthat every raw factor returned by\\n`Hex.factorFastFactorsWithBound f (Hex.ZPoly.defaultFactorCoeffBound f)` is\\n`Hex.ZPoly.Irreducible`. That premise is false: for `f = 1`, the executable\\nfast wrapper returns `some #[(1 : Hex.ZPoly)]`, and `1` is not irreducible.\\nSee #7760 for the kernel-checked counterexample.\\n\\nDirective #2564's FactorSoundness capstone (#6672) is still blocked on the fast\\nbranch of the raw-factor irreducibility disjunction consumed by\\n`factor_entries_irreducible`, but the substrate must be scoped to the actual\\nBHKS fast-core branch rather than arbitrary wrapper success.\\n\\nExisting unconditional raw branches in\\n`HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/IntReductionMod.lean`:\\n\\n- slow-trial:\\n  `factorSlowTrialFactorsWithBound_factor_irreducible_of_fast_none`;\\n- slow-modular:\\n  `slowModularRaw_irreducible_of_fast_none`.\\n\\nExisting conditional fast theorem in\\n`HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/PartitionRefinement.lean`:\\n\\n```lean\\nfactorFastFactorsWithBound_raw_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs\\n```\\n\\nIt has the right conclusion for raw factors returned by the BHKS fast-core path,\\nbut still takes `BHKS.ForwardRecoveryInputs`, `BHKS.CutProjectionHypotheses`,\\nthe support-partition count equality, the loop-output equality `hcore`, and\\n`Hex.reassemblyExpansionComplete (Hex.normalizeForFactor f) rawFactors` as free\\nhypotheses.\\n\\n## Deliverables\\n\\n1. Add a fast-core-scoped substrate theorem with a short name, for example:\\n\\n   ```lean\\n   theorem factorFastCore_raw_irreducible_of_some\\n       (f : Hex.ZPoly) (B : Nat) {coreFactors : Array Hex.ZPoly}\\n       -- include the actual branch markers needed by the executable wrapper:\\n       -- degree/nonconstant guard, `0 \\u003c B`, non-singleton modular factorization,\\n       -- no quadratic shortcut when relevant, and the core success equality\\n       (hcore :\\n         Hex.factorFastCoreWithBound (Hex.normalizeForFactor f) B =\\n           some coreFactors) :\\n       ∀ raw ∈ coreFactors.toList, Hex.ZPoly.Irreducible raw\\n   ```\\n\\n   The exact signature should match the existing executable names and branch\\n   markers in `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`; avoid a theorem over\\n   arbitrary `factorFastFactorsWithBound = some` unless it first excludes or\\n   separately handles the constant/unit shortcuts.\\n\\n2. Discharge the hypotheses currently required by\\n   `factorFastFactorsWithBound_raw_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs` from the\\n   fast-core success package itself, including the fast-branch\\n   `reassemblyExpansionComplete` producer and the actual first-success\\n   `ForwardRecoveryInputs` / `CutProjectionHypotheses` / partition-count data.\\n   Reuse existing constructors in `Recovery.lean`, `TerminationBound.lean`,\\n   `Lattice.lean`, and `PartitionRefinement.lean` instead of restating the BHKS\\n   proof from scratch.\\n\\n3. If #6672 needs a wrapper-level theorem, assemble it by case-splitting the\\n   executable fast wrapper: prove irreducibility only for branches whose raw\\n   outputs are nonunit irreducibles, and route constant/unit shortcuts through\\n   the shape actually consumed by `factor_entries_irreducible` rather than\\n   claiming every raw factor is irreducible.\\n\\n4. Leave #6672's capstone theorem itself untouched except for any public helper\\n   exposure needed by this substrate. The output of this issue is the reusable\\n   fast substrate that #6672 can consume in a small follow-up assembly PR.\\n\\n## Library placement\\n\\nTarget files:\\n\\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/PartitionRefinement.lean` for the raw-factor\\n  irreducibility substrate, adjacent to\\n  `factorFastFactorsWithBound_raw_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs`;\\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean` or\\n  `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/TerminationBound.lean` only if a missing\\n  existing constructor needs to be exposed or composed.\\n\\n## Premise check\\n\\nBefore implementing, inspect the exact fast wrapper/core control flow. The\\nreplacement theorem must not imply `Hex.ZPoly.Irreducible (1 : Hex.ZPoly)` from\\n`factorFastFactorsWithBound (1 : Hex.ZPoly) default = some #[1]`.\\n\\nIf the fast-core theorem still cannot be stated without a larger missing BHKS\\nbridge, stop and comment with the concrete missing lemma shape rather than\\nadding assumptions, `sorry`, or a weakened theorem.\\n\\n## Context\\n\\n- Replaces stale issue: #7760.\\n- Parent directive: #2564.\\n- Direct blocker being unblocked: #6672.\\n- Recent substrate: #7739 / PR #7746 added\\n  `factorFastFactorsWithBound_raw_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs`, but it is\\n  conditional on caller-supplied recovery/cut/completeness hypotheses.\\n- Relevant files:\\n  - `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/PartitionRefinement.lean`\\n  - `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean`\\n  - `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/TerminationBound.lean`\\n  - `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Lattice.lean`\\n  - `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/IntReductionMod.lean`\\n  - `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`\\n\\n## Verification\\n\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.PartitionRefinement`\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus`\\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\\n- `git diff --check`\\n- Added-line forbidden-token scan over touched files shows no new `sorry`,\\n  `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, or `FIXME`.\\n- `rg -n \\\"factorFastCore_raw_irreducible_of_some|factorFastFactorsWithBound_raw_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs|factor_irreducible_of_nonUnit\\\" HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`\\n  confirms the new theorem is visible to #6672's assembly surface.\\n\\n## Out of scope\\n\\n- Proving `factor_irreducible_of_nonUnit` in #6672.\\n- Changing executable factorization behavior.\\n- Reworking slow-modular or slow-trial irreducibility proofs.\\n- Proving HO-4 termination / `factorFast` always succeeds at the BHKS cap.\\n\",\"labels\":[\"agent-plan\",\"feature\"],\"title\":\"feat: prove BHKS fast-core raw irreducibility substrate for BZ capstone\"}\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/a49eee87-8386-4a95-acca-9a78b9195249.stdout:38:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_18","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"coordination read-issue 6672 --json title,labels,body --jq '{title:.title, labels:[.labels[].name], body:.body}'\"","aggregated_output":"{\"body\":\"**FAST DISJUNCT NEEDS AN UNCONDITIONAL SUBSTRATE THAT DOES NOT YET EXIST.**\\nThe slow side is ready; the only remaining blocker is the executable\\nfast-BHKS raw irreducibility disjunct, and the closed dependency #7739\\ndid **not** unblock it (see below).\\n\\n---\\nlibrary: HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib\\n\\nParent: #6665.\\n\\n## Current state\\n\\nThe target theorem remains:\\n\\n```lean\\ntheorem factor_irreducible_of_nonUnit (f : Hex.ZPoly) :\\n    ∀ entry ∈ (Hex.factor f).factors, Hex.ZPoly.Irreducible entry.1\\n```\\n\\nIt lives in `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/FactorSoundness.lean` and is the\\nfinal assembly over the current `Hex.factor` dispatch. The assembly route is\\n`factor_entries_irreducible f h_raw` (`HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean:427`),\\nwhose `h_raw` is a three-way disjunction over the chosen raw-factor array.\\n\\n**Two of the three raw disjuncts are AVAILABLE, unconditionally:**\\n\\n- Slow-trial: `factorSlowTrialFactorsWithBound_factor_irreducible_of_fast_none`\\n  (`IntReductionMod.lean:6422`).\\n- Slow-modular: `slowModularRaw_irreducible_of_fast_none`\\n  (`IntReductionMod.lean:6225`).\\n\\n**The fast-BHKS disjunct is still gated.** It requires, for the bare\\nexecutable output `factorFastFactorsWithBound f (defaultFactorCoeffBound f)\\n= some rawFactors`, that `∀ raw ∈ rawFactors.toList,\\nHex.ZPoly.Irreducible raw`, with no further hypotheses. The deepest fast\\nlemmas are conditional:\\n\\n- `factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_lift`\\n  (`PartitionRefinement.lean:601`, #7723) threads the full CLD\\n  separation / true-factor-lift stack as free hypotheses.\\n- `factorFastFactorsWithBound_raw_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs`\\n  (`PartitionRefinement.lean:1042`, landed by #7739 / PR #7746) has the\\n  right conclusion shape but takes `BHKS.ForwardRecoveryInputs`,\\n  `BHKS.CutProjectionHypotheses`, the scheduled-loop determinism `hcore`,\\n  the support-partition count equality, and `reassemblyExpansionComplete`\\n  all as **free hypotheses**. None is produced from the executable run\\n  (machine-checked: `grep ': BHKS.ForwardRecoveryInputs'` /\\n  `': BHKS.CutProjectionHypotheses'` over\\n  `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/*.lean` returns only hypothesis binders;\\n  no fast-branch `reassemblyExpansionComplete` exists — only the\\n  constant/singleton/quadratic/exhaustive/exhaustiveIntegerTrial variants).\\n\\nSo #7739 delivered the **caller-threaded** (conditional) fast-raw\\nproducer, sibling to #7738's recorded-entry bridge — NOT the unconditional\\nsubstrate this capstone needs. The fast disjunct is not vacuous:\\n`#guard factorFastFactorsWithBound cldGuardF 4 = some …`\\n(`HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`) and `factor` runs at the default\\nbound, so the fast arm is genuinely reachable.\\n\\n## Blocked on a not-yet-planned fast substrate\\n\\nThis capstone becomes a three-way case split on `h_raw` through\\n`factor_entries_irreducible` once one new fast-side substrate lands,\\nsibling to the two available slow ones:\\n\\n\\u003e `factorFastRaw_irreducible_of_some` — given\\n\\u003e `factorFastFactorsWithBound f (defaultFactorCoeffBound f) = some rawFactors`,\\n\\u003e prove `∀ raw ∈ rawFactors.toList, Hex.ZPoly.Irreducible raw`,\\n\\u003e **unconditionally**.\\n\\nThat substrate needs (a) a fast-branch `reassemblyExpansionComplete`\\nproducer for the BHKS-core output, and (b) an unconditional discharge —\\nfrom the executable fast-core success at its actual first-success\\nprecision — of the package now threaded as hypotheses into\\n`factorFastFactorsWithBound_raw_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs`\\n(`ForwardRecoveryInputs` + `CutProjectionHypotheses` + the partition-count\\nequality + the loop-output `hcore`). This is the BHKS forward-recovery\\nsoundness content (the live successor to #7736/#7667), not capstone glue.\\n**No open issue currently tracks it** — a planner must create it before\\nthis capstone is claimable.\\n\\nDo not retry the closed dependencies #6771, #7561, #7664, #7675, #7736,\\n#7738, or #7739 as if they were still open prerequisites; their residual\\nfast-side scope is the unconditional `factorFastRaw_irreducible_of_some`\\nsubstrate above.\\n\\n## Deliverables (once the fast substrate lands)\\n\\n1. Replace the `sorry` in `factor_irreducible_of_nonUnit` with a real\\n   proof for all current `Hex.factor` arms.\\n2. Route through the current branch machinery for the fast-BHKS,\\n   slow-modular, and slow-trial raw sources.\\n3. Preserve downstream theorem names that consume\\n   `factor_irreducible_of_nonUnit`, including\\n   `factor_polynomialIrreducible_of_nonUnit` and uniqueness/checker\\n   consumers.\\n4. Do not change executable behavior.\\n5.   hinputs.trueSupports hcore_ne h hcut hsize hpartition\n\nset_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in\n/--\nDefault-bound fast-BHKS direct-core specialization via the forward-count route.\n\nThe BHKS core is hcore_lc_pos :\n      0 < Hex.DensePoly.leadingCoeff\n        (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore :=\n    Hex.squareFreeCore_leadingCoeff_pos_of_ne_zero f hf_ne\n  have hcore_ne : (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore ≠ 0 :=\n    zpoly_ne_zero_of_pos_lc hcore_lc_pos\n  exact\n    factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs\n      hcore_ne hinputs h hcut hpartition\n\n/-- Scheduled-loop form of\n`factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs`.\n\nThe executable fast dispatcher does not start the core loop at the recovery\nprecision: it starts at `Hex.initialHenselPrecision a` and walks the Hensel\nschedule, so the loop's `start` argument differs from the precision `target` at\nwhich the caller carries the `BHKS.ForwardRecoveryInputs` package.  This wrapper\ndecouples the two: the loop-success hypothesis `h` is stated at an arbitrary\n`start`/`fuel`, while the forward package, cut certificate, and partition count\nall sit at the scheduled `target`.\n\nThe decoupling is sound because the count argument routes through\n`factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_count_eq_of_cut`, whose loop-result\nparameters (`k`, `fuel`) and cut-precision parameters (`L`) are already\nindependent — they meet only at the size identity `hsize`, which is derived\npurely from the package fields (`candidates_eq` / `indicators_match`) at\n`target`.  The hypothesis `h` pins the loop output to the package's\n`expectedFactors`; supplying that equality for the actual executable start\n`Hex.initialHenselPrecision a` is the scheduled-loop determinism obligation left\nto the caller (it is *not* implied by the package at `target` alone, since the\nloop returns the first schedule success and an earlier precision could exit with\na different array). -/\ntheorem factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs_on_schedule\n    {core : Hex.ZPoly} {B : Nat} {primeData : Hex.PrimeChoiceData}\n    {start fuel target : Nat}\n    (hcore_ne : core ≠ 0)\n    (hinputs :\n      BHKS.ForwardRecoveryInputs core\n        (Hex.ZPoly.toMonicLiftData core target primeData))\n    (h :\n      Hex.factorFastCoreWithBound core B primeData start fuel =\n        some hinputs.expectedFactors)\n    (hcut :\n      BHKS.CutProjectionHypotheses\n        (BHKS.projectedRowsOfLiftData core\n          (Hex.ZPoly.toMonicLiftData core target primeData) hinputs.rows_pos)\n        hinputs.trueSupports)\n    (hpartition :\n      (BHKS.supportPartitionByMinColumn hinputs.trueSupports).length =\n hsize :\n      hinputs.expectedFactors.size =\n        (Hex.bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators\n          (BHKS.projectedRowsOfLiftData core\n            (Hex.ZPoly.toMonicLiftData core target primeData)\n            hinputs.rows_pos)).size := by\n    rw [Hex.bhksIndicatorCandidates?_size_eq hinputs.candidates_eq]\n    exact congrArg Array.size hinputs.indicators_match.symm\n  exact\n    factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_cut\n      hinputs.trueSupports hcore_ne h hcut hsize hpartition\n\nset_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in\n/--\nDefault-bound scheduled-loop fast-BHKS direct-core specialization.\n\nSame as `factorFastCoreDefault_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs`, but\nwith the loop-success hypothesis `h` decoupled from the recovery precision\n`target` so that it matches the executable schedule walk (which starts the core\nloop at `Hex.initialHenselPrecision a`, not at `target`).  The forward package,\ncut certificate, and partition count are all carried at the scheduled `target`;\n`h` is the loop-output equality at the actual `start`/`fuel`, supplied by the\nscheduled-loop determinism obligation.\n-/\ntheorem factorFastCoreDefault_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs_on_schedule\n    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (hf_ne : f ≠ 0)\n    (primeData : Hex.PrimeChoiceData) {start fuel target : Nat}\n    (hinputs :\n      BHKS.ForwardRecoveryInputs\n        (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore\n        (Hex.ZPoly.toMonicLiftData\n          (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore target primeData))\n    (h :\n      Hex.factorFastCoreWithBound\n          (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore\n          (Hex.ZPoly.defaultFactorCoeffBound f) primeData start fuel =\n        some hinputs.expectedFactors)\n    (hcut :\n      BHKS.CutProjectionHypotheses\n        (BHKS.projectedRowsOfLiftData\n          (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore\n f).squareFreeCore)).card) :\n    ∀ factor ∈ hinputs.expectedFactors.toList,\n      Hex.ZPoly.Irreducible factor := by\n  have hcore_lc_pos :\n      0 < Hex.DensePoly.leadingCoeff\n        (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore :=\n    Hex.squareFreeCore_leadingCoeff_pos_of_ne_zero f hf_ne\n  have hcore_ne : (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore ≠ 0 :=\n    zpoly_ne_zero_of_pos_lc hcore_lc_pos\n  exact\n    factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs_on_schedule\n      hcore_ne hinputs h hcut hpartition\n\n/--\nRecorded-entry irreducibility for the BHKS fast-core success arm.\n\nThe executable reassembly array is intentionally private to\n`HexBerlekampZassenhaus.Basic`, so this public theorem works through the\nexisting branch-shape lemma: each recorded `factorWithBound` entry is the\nsign-normalisation of some raw factor in the fast-core reassembly.  The\nforward-input/cut certificates prove each successful core output irreducible,\nand the supplied reassembly-completeness certificate lifts that proof across the\nnormalization reassembly.\n-/\ntheorem factorWithBound_fastCore_entry_irreducible_of_forwardInputs\n    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (hf_ne : f ≠ 0) (B : Nat)\n    (primeData : Hex.PrimeChoiceData)\n    (hB_pos : 1 ≤ B)\n    (hchoose :\n      Hex.choosePrimeData? (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore =\n        some primeData)\n    (hdeg :\n      (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore.degree?.getD 0 ≠ 0)\n    (hmulti : 1 < primeData.factorsModP.size)\n    (hquadratic :\n      B = 1 ∨\n        Hex.quadraticIntegerRootFactors?\n          (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore = none)\n    (hinputs :\n      BHKS.ForwardRecoveryInputs\n        (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore\n        (Hex.ZPoly.toMonicLiftData\n          (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore\n          (Hex.precisionForCoeffBound B primeData.p) primeData))\n    (hcore :\n      let a := Hex.precisionForCoeffBound B primeData.p\n      Hex.factorFastCoreWithBound (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore a\n        primeData (Hex.initialHenselPrecision a)\n        (Hex.ZPoly.quadraticDoublingSteps a + 2) =\n          some hinputs.expectedFactors)\n    (hcut :\n      BHKS.CutProjectionHypotheses\n        (BHKS.projectedRowsOfLiftData\n          (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore\n          (Hex.ZPoly.toMonicLiftData\n            (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore\n            (Hex.precisionForCoeffBound B primeData.p) primeData)\n          hinputs.rows_pos)\n        hinputs.trueSupports)\n    (hpartition :\n      (BHKS.supportPartitionByMinColumn hinputs.trueSupports).length =\n        (UniqueFactorizationMonoid.normalizedFactors\n          (HexPolyZMathlib.toPolynomial\n            (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore)).card)\n    (hcomplete :\n      Hex.reassemblyExpansionComplete (Hex.normalizeForFactor f)\n        hinputs.expectedFactors) :\n    ∀ entry ∈ (Hex.factorWithBound f B).factors.toList,\n      Hex.ZPoly.Irreducible entry.1 := by\n  have hcore_lc_pos :\n      0 < Hex.DensePoly.leadingCoeff\n        (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore :=\n    Hex.squareFreeCore_leadingCoeff_pos_of_ne_zero f hf_ne\n  have hcore_ne : (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore ≠ 0 :=\n    zpoly_ne_zero_of_pos_lc hcore_lc_pos\n  have hcore_irr :\n      ∀ factor ∈ hinputs.expectedFactors.toList,\n        Hex.ZPoly.Irreducible factor :=\n    factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs_on_schedule\n      hcore_ne hinputs hcore hcut hpartition\n  intro entry hentry\n  obtain ⟨raw, hraw_mem, hentry_eq⟩ :=\n    Hex.factorWithBound_entry_mem_fast_core_success_raw f B entry\n      primeData hB_pos hdeg hchoose hmulti hquadratic hcore hentry\n  have hraw_irr : Hex.ZPoly.Irreducible raw :=\n    Hex.reassemblePolynomialFactors_factor_irreducible_of_complete_and_core_irreducible\n      (Hex.normalizeForFactor f) hinputs.expectedFactors\n      hcomplete hcore_irr hraw_mem\n  rw [hentry_eq]\n  exact zpolyIrreducible_normalizeFactorSign_of_zpolyIrreducible hraw_irr\n\n/--\nFast `h_raw` disjunct producer for the BHKS fast-core success arm.\n\nThis is the fast half of the `h_raw` hypothesis consumed by\n`HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.factorWithBound_entries_irreducible` /\n`factor_entries_irreducible` (#3987 / #4170) and by the #6672 capstone:\nwhenever the public fast factor function `Hex.factorFastFactorsWithBound f B`\nreturns `some rawFactors`, every raw factor in that array is\n`Hex.ZPoly.Irreducible`.  Instantiating `B` at `Hex.ZPoly.defaultFactorCoeffBound f`\nyields exactly the first disjunct of `factor_entries_irreducible`'s `h_raw`.\n\nUnlike the #7738 entry-level theorem `factorWithBound_fastCore_entry_irreducible_of_forwardInputs`,\nwhich proves the *recorded* `factorWithBound` entries irreducible, this exposes\nthe *raw* fast-factor array directly: it identifies that array as the\nnormalization reassembly of the successful core output `hinputs.expectedFactors`\n(`Hex.factorFastFactorsWithBound_eq_some_of_core_success`), proves each core\nfactor irreducible through the scheduled-loop forward-input wrapper\n`factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs_on_schedule`,\nand lifts that across the reassembly via\n`Hex.reassemblePolynomialFactors_factor_irreducible_of_complete_and_core_irreducible`.\n\nThe first-success certificates are threaded as hypotheses at the precision the\ncaller carries them: `hcore` pins the *actual* executable loop output (started\nat `Hex.initialHenselPrecision a`, not at a cap precision) to\n`hinputs.expectedFactors`.  This does not force equality with any cap recovery\narray, and the cut certificate `hcut` runs through the true-factor lift/cut\nstack rather than the refuted no-early-success or cap-determinism shortcuts. -/\ntheorem factorFastFactorsWithBound_raw_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs\n    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (hf_ne : f ≠ 0) (B : Nat)\n    (primeData : Hex.PrimeChoiceData)\n    (hB_pos : 1 ≤ B)\n    (hchoose :\n      Hex.choosePrimeData? (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore =\n        some primeData)\n    (hdeg :\n      (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore.degree?.getD 0 ≠ 0)\n    (hmulti : 1 < primeData.factorsModP.size)\n    (hquadratic :\n      B = 1 ∨\n        Hex.quadraticIntegerRootFactors?\n          (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore = none)\n    (hinputs :\n      BHKS.ForwardRecoveryInputs\n        (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore\n        (Hex.ZPoly.toMonicLiftData\n          (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore\n          (Hex.precisionForCoeffBound B primeData.p) primeData))\n    (hcore :\n      let a := Hex.precisionForCoeffBound B primeData.p\n      Hex.factorFastCoreWithBound (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore a\n        primeData (Hex.initialHenselPrecision a)\n        (Hex.ZPoly.quadraticDoublingSteps a + 2) =\n          some hinputs.expectedFactors)\n    (hcut :\n      BHKS.CutProjectionHypotheses\n        (BHKS.projectedRowsOfLiftData\n          (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore\n          (Hex.ZPoly.toMonicLiftData\n            (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore\n            (Hex.precisionForCoeffBound B primeData.p) primeData)\n          hinputs.rows_pos)\n        hinputs.trueSupports)\n    (hpartition :\n      (BHKS.supportPartitionByMinColumn hinputs.trueSupports).length =\n        (UniqueFactorizationMonoid.normalizedFactors\n          (HexPolyZMathlib.toPolynomial\n            (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore)).card)\n    (hcomplete :\n      Hex.reassemblyExpansionComplete (Hex.normalizeForFactor f)\n        hinputs.expectedFactors)\n    {rawFactors : Array Hex.ZPoly}\n    (hfast : Hex.factorFastFactorsWithBound f B = some rawFactors) :\n    ∀ raw ∈ rawFactors.toList, Hex.ZPoly.Irreducible raw := by\n  have hcore_lc_pos :\n      0 < Hex.DensePoly.leadingCoeff\n        (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore :=\n    Hex.squareFreeCore_leadingCoeff_pos_of_ne_zero f hf_ne\n  have hcore_ne : (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore ≠ 0 :=\n    zpoly_ne_zero_of_pos_lc hcore_lc_pos\n  have hcore_irr :\n      ∀ factor ∈ hinputs.expectedFactors.toList,\n        Hex.ZPoly.Irreducible factor :=\n    factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs_on_schedule\n      hcore_ne hinputs hcore hcut hpartition\n  have hfast_eq :=\n    Hex.factorFastFactorsWithBound_eq_some_of_core_success f B primeData\n      hinputs.expectedFactors hB_pos hchoose hdeg (by omega) hquadratic hcore\n  rw [hfast] at hfast_eq\n  have hraw_eq := (Option.some.inj hfast_eq)\n  intro raw hraw_mem\n  rw [hraw_eq] at hraw_mem\n  exact\n    Hex.reassemblePolynomialFactors_factor_irreducible_of_complete_and_core_irreducible\n      (Hex.normalizeForFactor f) hinputs.expectedFactors hcomplete hcore_irr\n      hraw_mem\n\nend HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/a49eee87-8386-4a95-acca-9a78b9195249.stdout:65:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_36","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '740,820p' HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean && sed -n '1260,1315p' HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Lattice.lean\"","aggregated_output":"    (hexpected_pos_lc : 0 < Hex.DensePoly.leadingCoeff expectedFactor)\n    (hexpected_degree : 0 < expectedFactor.degree?.getD 0)\n    (hdilated :\n      Hex.ZPoly.dilate (Hex.DensePoly.leadingCoeff f)\n          (Hex.centeredLiftPoly (Array.polyProduct selected) (d.p ^ d.k)) =\n        expectedFactor) :\n    ∃ quotient,\n      Hex.bhksIndicatorCandidate? f d indicator =\n        some (expectedFactor, quotient) :=\n  Hex.bhksIndicatorCandidate?_eq_some_of_dilatedCenteredLift\n    f d indicator selected expectedFactor hselected hdvd\n    hexpected_prim hexpected_sign hexpected_pos_lc hexpected_degree hdilated\n\n/--\nProof-facing inputs for the SPEC Group D forward-verification clause at one\nprecision/recovery call: `L' = W` (deliverable 1, supplied by issue #3034 at\nthe executable cap) plus the residual abstract obligations B7 (BHKS\nLemma 3.3 indicator identification) and A2 (Mignotte centred-residue\nreconstruction) that this layer treats as hypotheses.\n\nThe Mignotte precision side condition is the SPEC's\n`p^k > 2 · defaultFactorCoeffBound f`; the Group D obligation establishes\nthis whenever `Hex.factorFastPrecisionCap f ≤ k`, since the cap dominates\nthe Mignotte coefficient bound.  The two abstract obligations\n(`indicators_match`, `candidates_eq`) wrap the per-step BHKS Lemma 3.3 / A2\ncontent and are the natural follow-up tasks for later work.\n-/\nstructure ForwardRecoveryInputs (f : Hex.ZPoly) (d : Hex.LiftData) where\n  /-- Positive lattice dimension so the projected rows / recovery branch make\n      sense. -/\n  rows_pos : HasPositiveDimension f d\n  /-- True-factor supports backing the indicator lattice `W`. -/\n  trueSupports :\n    Set (Set (Fin (projectedRowsOfLiftData f d rows_pos).factorCount))\n  /-- BHKS `L' = W` at this lift data, supplied by #3034's\n      `BHKS.projectedRowSpan_eq_trueFactorIndicatorLattice_of_cap`. -/\n  lattice_eq_indicators :\n    BHKS.projectedRowSpanInt (projectedRowsOfLiftData f d rows_pos) =\n      BHKS.trueFactorIndicatorLattice trueSupports\n  /-- Mignotte-precision side condition: the modular precision exceeds twice\n      the executable Mignotte coefficient bound, so the centred-residue lift\n      is unique. -/\n  mignotte_precision :\n    2 * Hex.ZPoly.defaultFactorCoeffBound f < d.p ^ d.k\n  /-- BHKS Lemma 3.3 / B7 conclusion, left abstract: the executable\n      equivalence-class indicators agree with a target indicator list\n      `expectedIndicators`. -/\n  expectedIndicators : Array (Array Int)\n  indicators_match :\n    equivalenceClassIndicatorsOfLiftData f d rows_pos = expectedIndicators\n  /-- The chosen indicator partition is non-degenerate. -/\n  nondegenerate :\n    Hex.bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition\n        (projectedRowsOfLiftData f d rows_pos) expectedIndicators = false\n  /-- A2 + exact division, left abstract: each indicator reconstructs into a\n      verified integer factor.  `expectedFactors` is the resulting factor\n      array. -/\n  expectedFactors : Array Hex.ZPoly\n  candidates_eq :\n    Hex.bhksIndicatorCandidates? f d expectedIndicators = some expectedFactors\n  /-- Final product check: the verified factors multiply back to `f`. -/\n  product_eq : Array.polyProduct expectedFactors = f\n\nnamespace ForwardRecoveryInputs\n\n/--\nProof-facing package saying that `expectedFactors` is the true integer-factor\nlist for the current recovery target.  The per-factor fields are exactly the\nfacts needed by the A2 candidate reconstruction wrapper; `product_eq` is the\nfinal executable recovery guard.\n-/\nstructure ExpectedTrueFactors (f : Hex.ZPoly)\n    (expectedIndicators : Array (Array Int))\n    (expectedFactors : Array Hex.ZPoly) where\n  /-- The true-factor list has one factor for each expected indicator. -/\n  size_eq : expectedFactors.size = expectedIndicators.size\n  /-- Every expected true factor divides the recovery target. -/\n  divides :\n    ∀ i, i < expectedIndicators.size →\n      expectedFactors.getD i 0 ∣ f\n  /-- Expected true factors are primitive integer polynomials. -/\n            have := hbasis_norm\n            nlinarith [hbasis_norm]\n        _ ≤ (Hex.bhksCutRadiusSq4 L : ℚ) := hnorm\n    have hklt : k.val < Hex.bhksCutPrefixCount L reduced :=\n      bhksWithinGramSchmidtCut_lt_prefixCount L reduced k (by rw [← hdets]; exact hpass)\n    -- `c` vanishes at and above the retained prefix length.\n    have hc : ∀ i : Fin (L.factorCount + L.coeffWidth),\n        Hex.bhksCutPrefixCount L reduced ≤ i.val → c[i] = 0 := by\n      intro i hi\n      exact hzero_above i (by omega)\n    have := HexLLLMathlib.rowCombination_mem_prefixSubmodule_of_vector\n      reduced c (Hex.bhksCutPrefixCount L reduced) hc\n    rwa [hcv] at this\n\n/--\nCut hypotheses needed to connect the executable projected rows to the abstract\ntrue-factor supports.  Later B4/B5 work discharges `indicator_mem_projected`\nfrom the BHKS norm bound and Gram-Schmidt cut soundness.\n-/\nstructure CutProjectionHypotheses\n    (L : Hex.BhksProjectedRows) (trueSupports : Set (Set (Fin L.factorCount))) where\n  indicator_mem_projected :\n    ∀ S : trueSupports, indicatorVector S.1 ∈ projectedRowSpanInt L\n\n/-- Direct caller-facing form of the cut hypothesis for one true support. -/\ntheorem indicatorVector_mem_projectedRowSpan_of_cut\n    (L : Hex.BhksProjectedRows) (trueSupports : Set (Set (Fin L.factorCount)))\n    (hcut : CutProjectionHypotheses L trueSupports) (S : trueSupports) :\n    indicatorVector S.1 ∈ projectedRowSpanInt L :=\n  hcut.indicator_mem_projected S\n\n/--\nBHKS `W <= L'`: every vector in the true-factor indicator lattice is in the\ninteger row span represented by the executable projected rows.\n-/\ntheorem trueFactorIndicatorLattice_le_projectedRowSpan\n    (L : Hex.BhksProjectedRows) (trueSupports : Set (Set (Fin L.factorCount)))\n    (hcut : CutProjectionHypotheses L trueSupports) :\n    trueFactorIndicatorLattice trueSupports ≤ projectedRowSpanInt L := by\n  rw [trueFactorIndicatorLattice]\n  refine Submodule.span_le.mpr ?_\n  rintro v ⟨S, rfl⟩\n  exact hcut.indicator_mem_projected S\n\n/-- Membership form of `W <= L'`, useful after proving a vector lies in `W`. -/\ntheorem mem_projectedRowSpan_of_mem_trueFactorIndicatorLattice\n    (L : Hex.BhksProjectedRows) (trueSupports : Set (Set (Fin L.factorCount)))\n    (hcut : CutProjectionHypotheses L trueSupports)\n    {v : Fin L.factorCount → ℤ}\n    (hv : v ∈ trueFactorIndicatorLattice trueSupports) :\n    v ∈ projectedRowSpanInt L :=\n  trueFactorIndicatorLattice_le_projectedRowSpan L trueSupports hcut hv\n\n/--\nAbstract separation hypotheses for the BHKS `L' = W` step.\n\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/a49eee87-8386-4a95-acca-9a78b9195249.stdout:68:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_38","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '600,760p' HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean\"","aggregated_output":"  lattice_eq_indicators := lattice_eq_indicators\n  expectedIndicators := expectedIndicatorArrayOfSupports trueSupports\n  indicators_match :=\n    equivalenceClassIndicatorsOfLiftData_eq_expectedIndicatorArrayOfSupports\n      rows_pos trueSupports lattice_eq_indicators\n\nend EquivalenceClassRecoveryHypotheses\n\n/-- Under the equivalence-class recovery hypotheses, the executable\n`bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators` output is exactly the expected indicator\nlist. This is the BHKS Lemma 3.3 / B7 implication from `L' = W` to the\nexecutable indicator surface. -/\ntheorem bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators_eq_of_recovery\n    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (d : Hex.LiftData)\n    (h : EquivalenceClassRecoveryHypotheses f d) :\n    equivalenceClassIndicatorsOfLiftData f d h.rows_pos = h.expectedIndicators :=\n  h.indicators_match\n\n/-- BHKS Lemma 3.3 conclusion exposed at the raw projected rows, for callers\nthat already have the projected-rows value in hand. -/\ntheorem bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators_projectedRows_eq_of_recovery\n    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (d : Hex.LiftData)\n    (h : EquivalenceClassRecoveryHypotheses f d) :\n    Hex.bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators\n        (projectedRowsOfLiftData f d h.rows_pos) = h.expectedIndicators :=\n  h.indicators_match\n\n/--\nProof-facing hypotheses for forward BHKS recovery at fixed precision.\n\nThe fields encode the four guards inside the executable `Hex.bhksRecover?`:\npositive lattice dimension, non-degenerate equivalence-class partition,\nsuccessful per-indicator integer reconstruction, and exact product check.\nBy the SPEC Group D forward-verification argument, these guards hold\nsimultaneously when the precision dominates Mignotte and `L' = W` holds.\nThe discharge of each individual field from the abstract precision /\nseparation hypotheses is left to later follow-up work; this record packages\nexactly what the executable function needs to return `some`.\n-/\nstructure RecoveryHypotheses (f : Hex.ZPoly) (d : Hex.LiftData) where\n  /-- Positive lattice dimension so the recovery enters the recovery branch. -/\n  rows_pos : HasPositiveDimension f d\n  /-- The equivalence-class partition is non-degenerate, so the executable\n      function does not bail out via `bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition`. -/\n  nondegenerate :\n    Hex.bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition\n        (projectedRowsOfLiftData f d rows_pos)\n        (equivalenceClassIndicatorsOfLiftData f d rows_pos) = false\n  /-- The verified factor list returned by the executable recovery. -/\n  expectedFactors : Array Hex.ZPoly\n  /-- Per-indicator centred-residue reconstruction succeeds and divides `f`. -/\n  candidates_eq :\n    Hex.bhksIndicatorCandidates? f d\n        (equivalenceClassIndicatorsOfLiftData f d rows_pos) =\n      some expectedFactors\n  /-- The reconstructed factors multiply back to `f`, so the final product\n      check inside `Hex.bhksRecover?` succeeds. -/\n  product_eq : Array.polyProduct expectedFactors = f\n\n/--\nForward-verification theorem (SPEC Group D, scoped to one precision/recovery\ncall): under the executable-cap recovery hypotheses, `Hex.bhksRecover? f d`\nreturns `some <expected factors>`.\n\nThis composes with `BHKS.projectedRowSpan_eq_trueFactorIndicatorLattice_of_cap`\nfrom `TerminationBound.lean` (the executable-cap `L' = W` theorem closed in\nissue #3034): when the recovery hypotheses are constructed at any precision\nthat meets `Hex.factorFastPrecisionCap f`, the executable `bhksRecover?` call\ninside `Hex.factorFastCoreWithBound` returns `some _`, so that loop iteration\nexits via the success branch rather than the precision-doubling fallback.\n-/\ntheorem bhksRecover_eq_some_of_recovery\n    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (d : Hex.LiftData) (h : RecoveryHypotheses f d) :\n    Hex.bhksRecover? f d = some h.expectedFactors := by\n  have hrows : 1 ≤ (Hex.bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors).factorCount +\n      (Hex.bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors).coeffWidth := h.rows_pos\n  have hnondeg :\n      Hex.bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition\n          (Hex.bhksProjectedRows (Hex.bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors) hrows)\n          (Hex.bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators\n            (Hex.bhksProjectedRows\n              (Hex.bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors) hrows)) = false :=\n    h.nondegenerate\n  have hcand :\n      Hex.bhksIndicatorCandidates? f d\n          (Hex.bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators\n            (Hex.bhksProjectedRows\n              (Hex.bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors) hrows)) =\n        some h.expectedFactors :=\n    h.candidates_eq\n  have hprod := h.product_eq\n  exact Hex.bhksRecover?_eq_some_of_checks f d hrows hnondeg hcand hprod\n\n/-- Corollary form of `bhksRecover_eq_some_of_recovery` that drops the\nexpected-factors witness from the conclusion: under the recovery hypotheses,\n`Hex.bhksRecover? f d` is in the `some _` branch. -/\ntheorem bhksRecover_isSome_of_recovery\n    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (d : Hex.LiftData) (h : RecoveryHypotheses f d) :\n    (Hex.bhksRecover? f d).isSome := by\n  rw [bhksRecover_eq_some_of_recovery f d h]\n  rfl\n\n/--\nAssemble the forward-recovery candidate equality from per-indicator A2\nreconstruction facts.\n\nThis is the Mathlib-side wrapper around the Mathlib-free fold helper\n`Hex.bhksIndicatorCandidates?_eq_some_of_getD`: callers prove each expected\nindicator reconstructs and exactly divides `f`, then this theorem supplies the\nraw `ForwardRecoveryInputs.candidates_eq` equality.\n-/\ntheorem bhksIndicatorCandidates?_eq_some_of_forwardCandidates\n    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (d : Hex.LiftData)\n    (expectedIndicators : Array (Array Int)) (expectedFactors : Array Hex.ZPoly)\n    (hsize : expectedFactors.size = expectedIndicators.size)\n    (hcandidate :\n      ∀ i, i < expectedIndicators.size →\n        ∃ quotient,\n          Hex.bhksIndicatorCandidate? f d (expectedIndicators.getD i #[]) =\n            some (expectedFactors.getD i 0, quotient)) :\n    Hex.bhksIndicatorCandidates? f d expectedIndicators =\n      some expectedFactors :=\n  Hex.bhksIndicatorCandidates?_eq_some_of_getD\n    f d expectedIndicators expectedFactors hsize hcandidate\n\n/--\nSingle-indicator A2 reconstruction from the monic-transform coordinate. The\nselected lifted factors are supplied explicitly by `hselected`, while\n`hdilated` is the B7/A2-facing reconstruction fact: after centered lifting in\nthe monic-transform coordinate and dilating back by `lc(f)`, the result is the\nexpected integer factor.\n-/\ntheorem bhksIndicatorCandidate?_eq_some_of_dilatedCenteredLift\n    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (d : Hex.LiftData) (indicator : Array Int)\n    (selected : Array Hex.ZPoly) (expectedFactor : Hex.ZPoly)\n    (hselected :\n      Hex.bhksIndicatorSelectedFactors d.liftedFactors indicator = some selected)\n    (hdvd : expectedFactor ∣ f)\n    (hexpected_prim : Hex.ZPoly.Primitive expectedFactor)\n    (hexpected_sign : 0 ≤ Hex.DensePoly.leadingCoeff expectedFactor)\n    (hexpected_pos_lc : 0 < Hex.DensePoly.leadingCoeff expectedFactor)\n    (hexpected_degree : 0 < expectedFactor.degree?.getD 0)\n    (hdilated :\n      Hex.ZPoly.dilate (Hex.DensePoly.leadingCoeff f)\n          (Hex.centeredLiftPoly (Array.polyProduct selected) (d.p ^ d.k)) =\n        expectedFactor) :\n    ∃ quotient,\n      Hex.bhksIndicatorCandidate? f d indicator =\n        some (expectedFactor, quotient) :=\n  Hex.bhksIndicatorCandidate?_eq_some_of_dilatedCenteredLift\n    f d indicator selected expectedFactor hselected hdvd\n    hexpected_prim hexpected_sign hexpected_pos_lc hexpected_degree hdilated\n\n/--\nProof-facing inputs for the SPEC Group D forward-verification clause at one\nprecision/recovery call: `L' = W` (deliverable 1, supplied by issue #3034 at\nthe executable cap) plus the residual abstract obligations B7 (BHKS\nLemma 3.3 indicator identification) and A2 (Mignotte centred-residue\nreconstruction) that this layer treats as hypotheses.\n\nThe Mignotte precision side condition is the SPEC's\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

sessions/a49eee87-8386-4a95-acca-9a78b9195249.stdout:74:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_41","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '440,620p' HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/PartitionRefinement.lean\"","aggregated_output":"Unlike `factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_count_ge`, it does not route through\nan `ExpectedTrueFactors` package (whose construction needs the full `L' = W`).\n\nThe two executable-success facts it consumes isolate the remaining plumbing: the\nemitted factor count equals the equivalence-class count (`hsize`) and the\nsupport-partition length equals the integer-factor count (`hpartition`).  The\nsuccessful-branch premise `_h` is retained to mark the eventual source of those\nfacts once the executable recovery wiring discharges them. -/\ntheorem factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_count_ge_of_cut\n    {core : Hex.ZPoly} {B : Nat} {primeData : Hex.PrimeChoiceData}\n    {k fuel : Nat} {coreFactors : Array Hex.ZPoly}\n    {L : Hex.BhksProjectedRows}\n    (trueSupports : Set (Set (Fin L.factorCount)))\n    (_h : Hex.factorFastCoreWithBound core B primeData k fuel = some coreFactors)\n    (hcut : BHKS.CutProjectionHypotheses L trueSupports)\n    (hsize : coreFactors.size = (Hex.bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators L).size)\n    (hpartition :\n      (BHKS.supportPartitionByMinColumn trueSupports).length =\n        (UniqueFactorizationMonoid.normalizedFactors\n          (HexPolyZMathlib.toPolynomial core)).card) :\n    (UniqueFactorizationMonoid.normalizedFactors\n      (HexPolyZMathlib.toPolynomial core)).card ≤\n        (coreFactors.toList.map HexPolyZMathlib.toPolynomial).length := by\n  rw [List.length_map, Array.length_toList, hsize, ← hpartition]\n  exact\n    BHKS.supportPartitionByMinColumn_length_le_bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators_size\n      L trueSupports hcut\n\n/-- Forward-cut cardinality equality for a successful BHKS fast-core branch.\n\nPairs the executable upper bound `factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_count_le`\nwith the forward-inclusion lower bound\n`factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_count_ge_of_cut`, so the count equality is\nestablished from the forward inclusion `W ⊆ L'`\n(`BHKS.CutProjectionHypotheses`) alone.  Unlike\n`factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_count_eq`, it routes through no\n`ExpectedTrueFactors` package, and hence through no reverse `L' = W` separation\nor bad-vector resultant valuation.  The two executable-success facts `hsize`\nand `hpartition` isolate the remaining plumbing. -/\ntheorem factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_count_eq_of_cut\n    {core : Hex.ZPoly} {B : Nat} {primeData : Hex.PrimeChoiceData}\n    {k fuel : Nat} {coreFactors : Array Hex.ZPoly}\n    {L : Hex.BhksProjectedRows}\n    (trueSupports : Set (Set (Fin L.factorCount)))\n    (hcore_ne : core ≠ 0)\n    (h : Hex.factorFastCoreWithBound core B primeData k fuel = some coreFactors)\n    (hcut : BHKS.CutProjectionHypotheses L trueSupports)\n    (hsize : coreFactors.size = (Hex.bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators L).size)\n    (hpartition :\n      (BHKS.supportPartitionByMinColumn trueSupports).length =\n        (UniqueFactorizationMonoid.normalizedFactors\n          (HexPolyZMathlib.toPolynomial core)).card) :\n    (coreFactors.toList.map HexPolyZMathlib.toPolynomial).length =\n      (UniqueFactorizationMonoid.normalizedFactors\n        (HexPolyZMathlib.toPolynomial core)).card := by\n  apply le_antisymm\n  · exact factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_count_le hcore_ne h\n  · exact factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_count_ge_of_cut trueSupports h hcut\n      hsize hpartition\n\n/-- Irreducibility of every emitted factor from the forward-cut count equality.\n\nThis is the forward-inclusion analogue of\n`factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_irreducible`: it feeds the forward count\nequality `factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_count_eq_of_cut` into the UFD\npartition scaffold `factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_irreducible_of_count`,\nwith no dependency on the reverse `L' = W` separation. -/\ntheorem factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_irreducible_of_cut\n    {core : Hex.ZPoly} {B : Nat} {primeData : Hex.PrimeChoiceData}\n    {k fuel : Nat} {coreFactors : Array Hex.ZPoly}\n    {L : Hex.BhksProjectedRows}\n    (trueSupports : Set (Set (Fin L.factorCount)))\n    (hcore_ne : core ≠ 0)\n    (h : Hex.factorFastCoreWithBound core B primeData k fuel = some coreFactors)\n    (hcut : BHKS.CutProjectionHypotheses L trueSupports)\n    (hsize : coreFactors.size = (Hex.bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators L).size)\n    (hpartition :\n      (BHKS.supportPartitionByMinColumn trueSupports).length =\n        (UniqueFactorizationMonoid.normalizedFactors\n          (HexPolyZMathlib.toPolynomial core)).card) :\n    ∀ factor ∈ coreFactors.toList,\n      Irreducible (HexPolyZMathlib.toPolynomial factor) :=\n  factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_irreducible_of_count hcore_ne h\n    (factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_count_eq_of_cut trueSupports hcore_ne h\n      hcut hsize hpartition)\n\n/-- `Hex.ZPoly`-predicate form of\n`factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_irreducible_of_cut`: the forward-inclusion\nirreducibility wrapper used by fast-core callers that already carry a forward\ncut certificate. -/\ntheorem factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_cut\n    {core : Hex.ZPoly} {B : Nat} {primeData : Hex.PrimeChoiceData}\n    {k fuel : Nat} {coreFactors : Array Hex.ZPoly}\n    {L : Hex.BhksProjectedRows}\n    (trueSupports : Set (Set (Fin L.factorCount)))\n    (hcore_ne : core ≠ 0)\n    (h : Hex.factorFastCoreWithBound core B primeData k fuel = some coreFactors)\n    (hcut : BHKS.CutProjectionHypotheses L trueSupports)\n    (hsize : coreFactors.size = (Hex.bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators L).size)\n    (hpartition :\n      (BHKS.supportPartitionByMinColumn trueSupports).length =\n        (UniqueFactorizationMonoid.normalizedFactors\n          (HexPolyZMathlib.toPolynomial core)).card) :\n    ∀ factor ∈ coreFactors.toList, Hex.ZPoly.Irreducible factor :=\n  factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_count hcore_ne h\n    (factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_count_eq_of_cut trueSupports hcore_ne h\n      hcut hsize hpartition)\n\n/--\nIrreducibility wrapper that discharges the forward cut hypothesis directly from\nthe true-factor CLD-vector certificates and their tight norm bounds, via the\nBHKS prefix survivor-span lemma.  This replaces the retired `CutRetention`\nroute.\n-/\ntheorem factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_trueFactors\n    {core : Hex.ZPoly} {B : Nat} {primeData : Hex.PrimeChoiceData}\n    {k fuel : Nat} {coreFactors : Array Hex.ZPoly}\n    {L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis} {hrows : 1 ≤ L.factorCount + L.coeffWidth}\n    (trueSupports :\n      Set (Set (Fin (Hex.bhksProjectedRows L hrows).factorCount)))\n    (hcore_ne : core ≠ 0)\n    (h : Hex.factorFastCoreWithBound core B primeData k fuel = some coreFactors)\n    (hbasis : L.basis.independent)\n    (data : ∀ S : trueSupports, BHKS.TrueFactorCLDVectorData L S.1)\n    (tight : ∀ S : trueSupports, BHKS.TrueFactorCLDTightNormBound L S.1)\n    (hsize :\n      coreFactors.size =\n        (Hex.bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators (Hex.bhksProjectedRows L hrows)).size)\n    (hpartition :\n      (BHKS.supportPartitionByMinColumn trueSupports).length =\n        (UniqueFactorizationMonoid.normalizedFactors\n          (HexPolyZMathlib.toPolynomial core)).card) :\n    ∀ factor ∈ coreFactors.toList, Hex.ZPoly.Irreducible factor :=\n  factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_cut trueSupports\n    hcore_ne h\n    (BHKS.cutProjectionHypotheses_of_trueFactors L hrows hbasis trueSupports data tight)\n    hsize hpartition\n\n/--\nCapstone composition for the fast `h_raw` disjunct: per-candidate irreducibility\nof the first successful BHKS fast-core recovery, with the forward cut\ncertificates discharged from genuine true-factor lift data rather than taken as\nopaque hypotheses.\n\nFor each true support `S`, the per-support lift package `lift S`\n(`BHKS.TrueFactorLift`) and its semantic facts `sem S`\n(`BHKS.TrueFactorLiftSemantics`), together with the prime, Hensel-precision, and\nper-column CLD separation hypotheses, produce the tight per-column bound via\n`BHKS.tightColumnBound_of_lift`.  That feeds `BHKS.tightNormBound_of_lift` to the\ntight cut-radius certificate `TrueFactorCLDTightNormBound`; its loose companion\n(`toNormBound`) and the structural block-form coordinate identities assemble the\n`TrueFactorCLDVectorData` arm.  Both arms drive\n`factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_trueFactors`.\n\nThis is the first consumer to wire the analytic stack (#7650 product CLD\nidentity, #7674 semantic lift facts, #7712 tight column bound producer) through\nto the irreducibility endpoint.  The remaining residual is supplying the\nper-support `TrueFactorLift`/semantics/separation family for the first\nsuccessful recovery; the separation hypotheses `hp`, `hk`, `hsep` are the BHKS\nLemma 5.7 inputs at the first-success lift precision, threaded here verbatim\nrather than re-derived from the global Mignotte recovery bound. -/\ntheorem factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_lift\n    {core : Hex.ZPoly} {B : Nat} {primeData : Hex.PrimeChoiceData}\n    {k fuel : Nat} {coreFactors : Array Hex.ZPoly}\n    {L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis} {hrows : 1 ≤ L.factorCount + L.coeffWidth}\n    (trueSupports :\n      Set (Set (Fin (Hex.bhksProjectedRows L hrows).factorCount)))\n    (hcore_ne : core ≠ 0)\n    (h : Hex.factorFastCoreWithBound core B primeData k fuel = some coreFactors)\n    (hbasis : L.basis.independent)\n    (lift : ∀ S : trueSupports, BHKS.TrueFactorLift L S.1)\n    (sem : ∀ S : trueSupports, BHKS.TrueFactorLiftSemantics (lift S))\n    (hp : ∀ S : trueSupports, 2 ≤ (lift S).p)\n    (hk : ∀ S : trueSupports, 1 < (lift S).p ^ (lift S).a)\n    (hsep : ∀ S : trueSupports,\n      ∀ j, 2 * Hex.bhksCoeffBound (lift S).f j < (lift S).p ^ (lift S).a)\n    (hsize :\n      coreFactors.size =\n        (Hex.bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators (Hex.bhksProjectedRows L hrows)).size)\n    (hpartition :\n      (BHKS.supportPartitionByMinColumn trueSupports).length =\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/a49eee87-8386-4a95-acca-9a78b9195249.stdout:77:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_43","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '3288,3415p' HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean\"","aggregated_output":"    hcap_le C hC_nonneg hC\n    (capSeparationOfBridgeDataAtFactorFastCapLift\n      f primeData rows_pos localFactorIndex localFactorDegree H\n      trueSupports hcut bridge hp hcomparison)\n\n/--\nHO-4 leaf capstone: compose\n`ForwardRecoveryInputs.ofCapSeparationCanonicalIndicatorsAtPrecisionForCoeffBound`\nwith `factorFast_ne_none_of_forwardInputs_at_cap`.\n\nGiven cap-level BHKS separation on the normalized square-free core lift, B7\nnon-degeneracy of the canonical support-driven indicator partition, and the\nA2/exact-division candidate facts (a candidate-product witness per indicator,\nplus the final product check), the public `Hex.factorFast f` returns `some _`.\n\nThis is the natural HO-4 leaf entry point for callers that arrive with\nexecutable cap separation: the two-step\n`ForwardRecoveryInputs` construction and the `factorFast`-success forward\ninference fold into a single application.  See\n`ofCapSeparationCanonicalIndicatorsAtPrecisionForCoeffBound` for the producer\nside and `factorFast_ne_none_of_forwardInputs_at_cap` for the caller side.\nThe Mignotte-side parallel entry point is\n`factorFast_ne_none_of_mignottePrecisionCanonicalIndicatorsExpectedFactorsAtPrecisionForCoeffBound`.\n-/\ntheorem factorFast_ne_none_of_capSeparationCanonicalIndicatorsAtPrecisionForCoeffBound\n    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (primeData : Hex.PrimeChoiceData)\n    (rows_pos :\n      HasPositiveDimension\n        (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore\n        (factorFastCapLiftData f primeData))\n    (trueSupports :\n      Set (Set (Fin (projectedRowsOfLiftData\n        (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore\n        (factorFastCapLiftData f primeData)\n        rows_pos).factorCount)))\n    (localFactorIndex localFactorDegree : Nat) (H : Hex.ZPoly)\n    (hcap_le :\n      Hex.factorFastPrecisionCap (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore ≤\n        (factorFastCapLiftData f primeData).k)\n    (C : ℝ) (hC_nonneg : 0 ≤ C) (hC : C ≤ 2)\n    (hcap :\n      ExecutableCapSeparationHypotheses\n        (badVectorWitnessOfLiftData\n          (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore\n          (factorFastCapLiftData f primeData)\n          rows_pos localFactorIndex localFactorDegree H)\n        trueSupports)\n    (hB_pos : 1 ≤ Hex.factorFastPrecisionCap f)\n    (hchoose :\n      Hex.choosePrimeData? (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore = some primeData)\n    (hp : 2 ≤ (factorFastCapLiftData f primeData).p)\n    (hk :\n      (factorFastCapLiftData f primeData).k =\n        Hex.precisionForCoeffBound\n          (Hex.factorFastPrecisionCap\n            (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore)\n          (factorFastCapLiftData f primeData).p)\n    (nondegenerate :\n      Hex.bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition\n          (projectedRowsOfLiftData\n            (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore\n            (factorFastCapLiftData f primeData)\n            rows_pos)\n          (expectedIndicatorArrayOfSupports trueSupports) = false)\n    (expectedFactors : Array Hex.ZPoly)\n    (hsize : expectedFactors.size =\n      (expectedIndicatorArrayOfSupports trueSupports).size)\n    (hcandidate :\n      ∀ i, i < (expectedIndicatorArrayOfSupports trueSupports).size →\n        ∃ quotient,\n          Hex.bhksIndicatorCandidate?\n              (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore\n              (factorFastCapLiftData f primeData)\n              ((expectedIndicatorArrayOfSupports trueSupports).getD i #[]) =\n            some (expectedFactors.getD i 0, quotient))\n    (product_eq :\n      Array.polyProduct expectedFactors =\n        (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore) :\n    Hex.factorFast f ≠ none :=\n  factorFast_ne_none_of_forwardInputs_at_cap f primeData hB_pos hchoose\n    (ForwardRecoveryInputs.ofCapSeparationCanonicalIndicatorsAtPrecisionForCoeffBound\n      rows_pos trueSupports localFactorIndex localFactorDegree H\n      hcap_le C hC_nonneg hC hcap hp hk nondegenerate\n      expectedFactors hsize hcandidate product_eq)\n\n/--\nBridge-data form of\n`factorFast_ne_none_of_capSeparationCanonicalIndicatorsAtPrecisionForCoeffBound`.\n\nThis wrapper is the capstone-facing consumer for the bad-vector package built\nin `BadVector.lean`: callers provide `BadVectorBridgeData` plus the cut and\nresultant-bound side conditions, and the theorem assembles the\n`ExecutableCapSeparationHypotheses` required by the existing cap-separation\nrecovery theorem.\n-/\ntheorem factorFast_ne_none_of_capSeparationBridgeDataCanonicalIndicatorsAtPrecisionForCoeffBound\n    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (primeData : Hex.PrimeChoiceData)\n    (rows_pos :\n      HasPositiveDimension\n        (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore\n        (factorFastCapLiftData f primeData))\n    (trueSupports :\n      Set (Set (Fin (projectedRowsOfLiftData\n        (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore\n        (factorFastCapLiftData f primeData)\n        rows_pos).factorCount)))\n    (localFactorIndex localFactorDegree : Nat) (H : Hex.ZPoly)\n    (hcap_le :\n      Hex.factorFastPrecisionCap (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore ≤\n        (factorFastCapLiftData f primeData).k)\n    (C : ℝ) (hC_nonneg : 0 ≤ C) (hC : C ≤ 2)\n    (hcut :\n      CutProjectionHypotheses\n        (projectedRowsOfLiftData\n          (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore\n          (factorFastCapLiftData f primeData)\n          rows_pos)\n        trueSupports)\n    (bridge :\n      ExecutableBadVectorWitness.BadVectorBridgeData\n        (badVectorWitnessOfLiftData\n          (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore\n          (factorFastCapLiftData f primeData)\n          rows_pos localFactorIndex localFactorDegree H)\n        trueSupports)\n    (hlt :\n      (HexPolyZMathlib.l2norm\n            (badVectorWitnessOfLiftData\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

Compared to\n`factorFast_terminates_ofBridgeDataPointwiseAuxiliaryBoundsAndPaperThreshold`,\nthe caller supplies the two independent BHKS Theorem 5.2 sub-bounds\n(`coeffL2NormBound ^ aux.natDegree ≤ bhksPaperCoeffNormFactorReal core` and\n`auxiliaryBound ^ input.natDegree ≤ bhksPaperDegreeFactorReal core *\nbhksPaperConstantFactorReal core C * bhksPaperLogFactorReal core`) rather than\nthe combined product inequality.\n-/\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/a49eee87-8386-4a95-acca-9a78b9195249.stdout:85:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_47","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '2320,2395p' HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean\"","aggregated_output":"`Hex.bhksRecover? f d` is `some _`. -/\ntheorem bhksRecover_isSome_of_forwardInputs\n    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (d : Hex.LiftData) (h : ForwardRecoveryInputs f d) :\n    (Hex.bhksRecover? f d).isSome :=\n  bhksRecover_isSome_of_recovery f d h.toRecoveryHypotheses\n\n/--\nCap-level specialisation: compose `ForwardRecoveryInputs.ofCapSeparation`\nwith `bhksRecover_eq_some_of_forwardInputs` at a fixed `LiftData`.  Under\ncap-level BHKS separation and the residual B7/A2 obligations,\n`Hex.bhksRecover? f d` returns `some expectedFactors`.\n-/\ntheorem bhksRecover_eq_some_of_capSeparation\n    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (d : Hex.LiftData)\n    (rows_pos : HasPositiveDimension f d)\n    (trueSupports :\n      Set (Set (Fin (projectedRowsOfLiftData f d rows_pos).factorCount)))\n    (localFactorIndex localFactorDegree : Nat) (H : Hex.ZPoly)\n    (hcap_le : Hex.factorFastPrecisionCap f ≤ d.k)\n    (C : ℝ) (hC_nonneg : 0 ≤ C) (hC : C ≤ 2)\n    (hcap :\n      ExecutableCapSeparationHypotheses\n        (badVectorWitnessOfLiftData f d rows_pos localFactorIndex\n          localFactorDegree H)\n        trueSupports)\n    (mignotte_precision :\n      2 * Hex.ZPoly.defaultFactorCoeffBound f < d.p ^ d.k)\n    (expectedIndicators : Array (Array Int))\n    (indicators_match :\n      equivalenceClassIndicatorsOfLiftData f d rows_pos = expectedIndicators)\n    (nondegenerate :\n      Hex.bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition\n          (projectedRowsOfLiftData f d rows_pos) expectedIndicators = false)\n    (expectedFactors : Array Hex.ZPoly)\n    (hsize : expectedFactors.size = expectedIndicators.size)\n    (hcandidate :\n      ∀ i, i < expectedIndicators.size →\n        ∃ quotient,\n          Hex.bhksIndicatorCandidate? f d (expectedIndicators.getD i #[]) =\n            some (expectedFactors.getD i 0, quotient))\n    (product_eq : Array.polyProduct expectedFactors = f) :\n    Hex.bhksRecover? f d = some expectedFactors :=\n  bhksRecover_eq_some_of_forwardInputs f d\n    (ForwardRecoveryInputs.ofCapSeparation rows_pos trueSupports\n      localFactorIndex localFactorDegree H hcap_le C hC_nonneg hC hcap\n      mignotte_precision expectedIndicators indicators_match nondegenerate\n      expectedFactors hsize hcandidate product_eq)\n\n/--\nCompose the Mathlib-side forward-recovery inputs with the executable scheduled\nfast-path lemma: if a scheduled lift for the normalized square-free core\nsatisfies the forward-verification inputs, then the public `Hex.factorFast`\nentry point succeeds.\n-/\ntheorem factorFast_ne_none_of_forwardInputs_on_schedule\n    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (primeData : Hex.PrimeChoiceData)\n    {target : Nat}\n    (hB_pos : 1 ≤ Hex.factorFastPrecisionCap f)\n    (hchoose :\n      Hex.choosePrimeData? (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore = some primeData)\n    (hinputs :\n      ForwardRecoveryInputs\n        (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore\n        (Hex.ZPoly.toMonicLiftData\n          (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore target primeData))\n    (hmem :\n      let a := Hex.precisionForCoeffBound (Hex.factorFastPrecisionCap f) primeData.p\n      target ∈\n        Hex.henselPrecisionSchedule a\n          (Hex.initialHenselPrecision a)\n          (Hex.ZPoly.quadraticDoublingSteps a + 2)) :\n    Hex.factorFast f ≠ none := by\n  have hrecover :\n      Hex.bhksRecover? (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore\n          (Hex.ZPoly.toMonicLiftData\n            (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore target primeData) =\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

(f : ZPoly) (d : LiftData) : Option (Array ZPoly) :=\n6693:theorem bhksRecover?_eq_some_of_checks\n6698:      bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition\n6700:          (bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators\n6704:      bhksIndicatorCandidates? f d\n6705:          (bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators\n11379:        bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition\n11381:          (bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators\n11386:      cases hcand : bhksIndicatorCandidates? f d\n11387:          (bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators\n11406:        bhksIndicatorCandidates? f d indicators = some candidates →\n11417:    let indicators := bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators projected\n11418:    by_cases hdeg : bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition projected indicators = true\n11421:      cases hcand : bhksIndicatorCandidates? f d indicators with\n11439:    (fun hcand => bhksIndicatorCandidates?_normalizeFactorSign hcand) h\n11447:    (fun hcand => bhksIndicatorCandidates?_shouldRecord hcand) h\n11464:    let indicators := bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators projected\n11465:    by_cases hdeg : bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition projected indicators = true\n11468:      cases hcand : bhksIndicatorCandidates? f d indicators with\n11475:            exact bhksIndicatorCandidates?_dvd hcand\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/a49eee87-8386-4a95-acca-9a78b9195249.stdout:96:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_53","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '6630,6718p' HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean\"","aggregated_output":"  simpa [hindicator] using hcandidate\n\nprivate inductive BhksRecoveryResult where\n  | success (candidates : Array ZPoly)\n  | degenerate\n  | candidateFailure\n  | productMismatch (candidates : Array ZPoly)\nderiving DecidableEq\n\nprivate def BhksRecoveryResult.toOption : BhksRecoveryResult → Option (Array ZPoly)\n  | .success candidates => some candidates\n  | .degenerate => none\n  | .candidateFailure => none\n  | .productMismatch _ => none\n\nprivate def BhksRecoveryResult.isReconstructionFailure : BhksRecoveryResult → Bool\n  | .success _ => false\n  | .degenerate => false\n  | .candidateFailure => true\n  | .productMismatch _ => true\n\nprivate def BhksRecoveryResult.isLatticeFailure : BhksRecoveryResult → Bool\n  | .success _ => false\n  | .degenerate => true\n  | .candidateFailure => false\n  | .productMismatch _ => false\n\n/--\nRun the fixed-precision BHKS recovery pipeline.\n\nThis executable glue builds the CLD lattice for the lifted factors, runs LLL\nplus the Gram-Schmidt cut, extracts BHKS Lemma 3.3 equivalence-class\nindicators by RREF, reconstructs every indicated candidate by centred lifting,\nand accepts only when the verified candidates multiply back to `f`.\n-/\nprivate def bhksRecoverClassified (f : ZPoly) (d : LiftData) : BhksRecoveryResult :=\n  let L := bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors\n  if hrows : 1 ≤ L.factorCount + L.coeffWidth then\n    let projected := bhksProjectedRows L hrows\n    let indicators := bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators projected\n    if bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition projected indicators then\n      .degenerate\n    else\n      match bhksIndicatorCandidates? f d indicators with\n      | none => .candidateFailure\n      | some candidates =>\n          if Array.polyProduct candidates == f then\n            .success candidates\n          else\n            .productMismatch candidates\n  else\n    .degenerate\n\ndef bhksRecover? (f : ZPoly) (d : LiftData) : Option (Array ZPoly) :=\n  (bhksRecoverClassified f d).toOption\n\n/--\nIf the executable BHKS recovery guards all pass, `bhksRecover?` returns the\nverified candidate array.\n\nThis lemma is the public proof-facing surface for callers that should not\nunfold the private failure classifier used by the executable.\n-/\ntheorem bhksRecover?_eq_some_of_checks\n    (f : ZPoly) (d : LiftData) {candidates : Array ZPoly}\n    (hrows : 1 ≤ (bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors).factorCount +\n      (bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors).coeffWidth)\n    (hnondeg :\n      bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition\n          (bhksProjectedRows (bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors) hrows)\n          (bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators\n            (bhksProjectedRows\n              (bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors) hrows)) = false)\n    (hcand :\n      bhksIndicatorCandidates? f d\n          (bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators\n            (bhksProjectedRows\n              (bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors) hrows)) =\n        some candidates)\n    (hprod : Array.polyProduct candidates = f) :\n    bhksRecover? f d = some candidates := by\n  unfold bhksRecover?\n  rw [bhksRecoverClassified]\n  have hproductCheck : (Array.polyProduct candidates == f) = true := by\n    simpa [beq_iff_eq] using hprod\n  simp only [dif_pos hrows, hnondeg, Bool.false_eq_true, if_false, hcand,\n    hproductCheck, if_true, BhksRecoveryResult.toOption]\n\nprivate def bhksIndicatorGuardLift : LiftData :=\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/a49eee87-8386-4a95-acca-9a78b9195249.stdout:102:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_56","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '5270,5338p' HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean && sed -n '5988,6032p' HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean\"","aggregated_output":"  | [] => [(sig, [j])]\n  | (s, members) :: rest =>\n      if s = sig then (s, members ++ [j]) :: rest\n      else (s, members) :: bhksInsertSignatureClass sig j rest\n\nprivate def bhksClassIndicator (r : Nat) (members : List Nat) : Array Int :=\n  ((List.range r).map (fun i => if i ∈ members then (1 : Int) else 0)).toArray\n\n/--\nBHKS equivalence-class indicator vectors over the projected lattice rows\nof `L`.\n\nLifts the projected integer rows into a rational row-basis matrix, runs\n`Matrix.rref` over `Q`, and groups column indices `0, …, r - 1` by their\nechelon-column signature: indices `i` and `j` are equivalent iff every\nechelon row agrees at positions `i` and `j` (BHKS Lemma 3.3 / FLINT\nAlgorithm 8). Each equivalence class produces one compact `0/1` indicator\nof length `r`. Classes are emitted in the order they are first observed by\nascending column index.\n-/\ndef bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators (L : BhksProjectedRows) : Array (Array Int) :=\n  let n := L.projectedRows.size\n  let r := L.factorCount\n  let M : Matrix Rat n r := bhksProjectedRowsAsRatMatrix L.projectedRows n r\n  let D := Matrix.rref M\n  let echelonRows : Array (Array Rat) := D.echelon.toArray.map (·.toArray)\n  let groups : List (List Nat) :=\n    ((List.range r).foldl\n        (fun acc j =>\n          bhksInsertSignatureClass (bhksColumnSignature echelonRows j) j acc)\n        []).map Prod.snd\n  (groups.map (fun cls => bhksClassIndicator r cls)).toArray\n\nprivate def bhksTwoClassProjectedRows : BhksProjectedRows :=\n  { factorCount := 4\n    coeffWidth := 0\n    cutRadiusSq4 := 0\n    reducedRowCount := 1\n    projectedRows := #[#[1, 1, 0, 0]] }\n\n#guard bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators bhksTwoClassProjectedRows =\n  #[#[1, 1, 0, 0], #[0, 0, 1, 1]]\n\nprivate def bhksSingletonClassProjectedRows : BhksProjectedRows :=\n  { factorCount := 3\n    coeffWidth := 0\n    cutRadiusSq4 := 0\n    reducedRowCount := 0\n    projectedRows := #[] }\n\n#guard bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators bhksSingletonClassProjectedRows =\n  #[#[1, 1, 1]]\n\nprivate def bhksNoProgressProjectedRows : BhksProjectedRows :=\n  { factorCount := 3\n    coeffWidth := 0\n    cutRadiusSq4 := 0\n    reducedRowCount := 3\n    projectedRows := #[#[1, 0, 0], #[0, 1, 0], #[0, 0, 1]] }\n\n#guard bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators bhksNoProgressProjectedRows =\n  #[#[1, 0, 0], #[0, 1, 0], #[0, 0, 1]]\n\nprivate def liftModulus (d : LiftData) : Nat :=\n  d.p ^ d.k\n\ndef centeredLiftPoly (f : ZPoly) (m : Nat) : ZPoly :=\n  DensePoly.ofCoeffs <| f.toArray.map fun coeff => centeredModNat coeff m\n\n\ndef bhksIndicatorOneCount (r : Nat) (indicator : Array Int) : Nat :=\n  (List.range r).foldl\n    (fun count i => if indicator.getD i 0 == 1 then count + 1 else count)\n    0\n\ndef bhksIndicatorAllOnes (r : Nat) (indicator : Array Int) : Bool :=\n  indicator.size == r && bhksIndicatorOneCount r indicator == r\n\n/-- The recovery early-bailout predicate: the projected lattice is empty, the\nindicator partition is empty, or the indicator partition is the trivial\nall-ones single class. -/\ndef bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition\n    (L : BhksProjectedRows) (indicators : Array (Array Int)) : Bool :=\n  indicators.isEmpty ||\n    L.projectedRows.isEmpty ||\n    (indicators.size == 1 &&\n      bhksIndicatorAllOnes L.factorCount (indicators.getD 0 #[]))\n\nprivate def bhksIndicatorCandidatesStep\n    (f : ZPoly) (d : LiftData) :\n    Option (Array ZPoly) → Array Int → Option (Array ZPoly)\n  | none, _ => none\n  | some candidates, indicator =>\n      match bhksIndicatorCandidate? f d indicator with\n      | some candidate => some (candidates.push candidate.1)\n      | none => none\n\n/-- Reconstruct and verify every BHKS equivalence-class indicator candidate.\n\nFolds `bhksIndicatorCandidate?` over the list of indicator vectors, pushing the\nverified candidate factor onto the accumulator on success and short-circuiting\nto `none` on the first reconstruction failure. -/\ndef bhksIndicatorCandidates?\n    (f : ZPoly) (d : LiftData) (indicators : Array (Array Int)) :\n    Option (Array ZPoly) :=\n  indicators.foldl (bhksIndicatorCandidatesStep f d) (some #[])\n\nprivate theorem bhksIndicatorCandidatesStep_fold_none\n    (f : ZPoly) (d : LiftData) (indicators : List (Array Int)) :\n    List.foldl (bhksIndicatorCandidatesStep f d) none indicators = none := by\n  induction indicators with\n  | nil => rfl\n  | cons indicator indicators ih =>\n      rw [List.foldl_cons]\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

sessions/a49eee87-8386-4a95-acca-9a78b9195249.stdout:112:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_62","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"nl -ba HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Lattice.lean | sed -n '1276,1284p;1538,1556p'\"","aggregated_output":"  1276\ttrue-factor supports.  Later B4/B5 work discharges `indicator_mem_projected`\n  1277\tfrom the BHKS norm bound and Gram-Schmidt cut soundness.\n  1278\t-/\n  1279\tstructure CutProjectionHypotheses\n  1280\t    (L : Hex.BhksProjectedRows) (trueSupports : Set (Set (Fin L.factorCount))) where\n  1281\t  indicator_mem_projected :\n  1282\t    ∀ S : trueSupports, indicatorVector S.1 ∈ projectedRowSpanInt L\n  1283\t\n  1284\t/-- Direct caller-facing form of the cut hypothesis for one true support. -/\n  1538\tBuild `CutProjectionHypotheses` for a family of true-factor supports directly\n  1539\tfrom their CLD-vector certificates (`TrueFactorCLDVectorData`), their tight\n  1540\tnorm bounds (`TrueFactorCLDTightNormBound`), and independence of the BHKS basis.\n  1541\tEach true support's indicator vector is the first block of a genuine short\n  1542\tlattice vector, which the prefix survivor-span lemma places in the retained\n  1543\tprefix span; projecting to the first `factorCount` coordinates lands the\n  1544\tindicator in `projectedRowSpanInt`.  This route does **not** pass through\n  1545\t`CutRetention`.\n  1546\t-/\n  1547\tdef cutProjectionHypotheses_of_trueFactors\n  1548\t    (L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis) (hrows : 1 ≤ L.factorCount + L.coeffWidth)\n  1549\t    (hbasis : L.basis.independent)\n  1550\t    (trueSupports : Set (Set (Fin (Hex.bhksProjectedRows L hrows).factorCount)))\n  1551\t    (data : ∀ S : trueSupports, TrueFactorCLDVectorData L S.1)\n  1552\t    (tight : ∀ S : trueSupports, TrueFactorCLDTightNormBound L S.1) :\n  1553\t    CutProjectionHypotheses (Hex.bhksProjectedRows L hrows) trueSupports where\n  1554\t  indicator_mem_projected S := by\n  1555\t    set v := trueFactorCLDVector L S.1 with hv\n  1556\t    have hind : (Hex.bhksProjectedRowsTrace L hrows).reducedMatrix.independent := by\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/a49eee87-8386-4a95-acca-9a78b9195249.stdout:137:{"type":"item.started","item":{"id":"item_86","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"gh issue comment 7765 --body-file - <<'EOF'\nBlocked after premise check; I do not think the requested theorem is currently derivable from the stated fast-core success package without adding the BHKS bridge it is supposed to consume.\n\nConcrete evidence:\n\n- \"'`Hex.factorFastCoreWithBound` returns the **first** `.success factors` encountered on the Hensel schedule and hides the successful precision (`HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean:6935-6956`). A wrapper equality\n  ```lean\n  factorFastCoreWithBound core a primeData (initialHenselPrecision a) ... = some coreFactors\n  ```\n  only identifies the returned array; it does not expose a `target` precision, `ForwardRecoveryInputs`, `CutProjectionHypotheses`, true supports, or a partition-count certificate for that returned array.\n- The existing raw irreducibility theorem is still conditional on exactly those missing facts: `factorFastFactorsWithBound_raw_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs` requires `ForwardRecoveryInputs`, `CutProjectionHypotheses`, the support-partition count equality, and `reassemblyExpansionComplete` as hypotheses (`HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/PartitionRefinement.lean:1010-1055`). Its core dependency has the same shape (`..._some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs`, lines 722-740).\n- `ForwardRecoveryInputs` itself contains the substantive B7/A2/lattice/product data (`Recovery.lean:767-801`), not data implied by executable success. Existing constructors around `ofMignottePrecision...AtPrecisionForCoeffBound` target `Hex.factorFastPrecisionCap f` (`Recovery.lean:2034-2080`), while this issue needs the public default-bound fast branch (`factorFastFactorsWithBound f B`, with `B = defaultFactorCoeffBound f` for #6672). The cap-level termination wrappers around `factorFastCapLiftData` (`Recovery.lean:8039-8068`) do not provide the raw-factor irreducibility substrate for that default-bound branch.\n- The wrapper control flow confirms why a wrapper-level `some` theorem is too broad and why the core branch needs a separate certificate producer: `factorFastFactorsWithBound` has constant, small-mod singleton, quadratic, and BHKS-core success `some` branches (`HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean:7610-7643`), and `factorFastFactorsWithBound_eq_some_of_core_success` only reassembles a supplied core success; it does not prove the core output is the true-factor partition (`Basic.lean:7674-7690`).\n\nSmallest missing lemma shape I see:\n\n```lean\n-- name/signature sketch only\ntheorem factorFastCore_success_forwardCertificates\n    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (B : Nat) (primeData : Hex.PrimeChoiceData)\n    {coreFactors : Array Hex.ZPoly}\n    (hB_pos : 1 <= B)\n    (hchoose : Hex.choosePrimeData? (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore = some primeData)\n    (hdeg : (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore.degree?.getD 0 != 0)\n    (hmulti : 1 < primeData.factorsModP.size)\n    (hquadratic : B = 1 '\"\\\\/ Hex.quadraticIntegerRootFactors? (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore = none)\n    (hcore :\n      let a := Hex.precisionForCoeffBound B primeData.p\n      Hex.factorFastCoreWithBound (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore a primeData\n        (Hex.initialHenselPrecision a) (Hex.ZPoly.quadraticDoublingSteps a + 2) = some coreFactors) :\n    -- either expose the actual first-success target on the schedule, or prove\n    -- the certificates directly for \"'`coreFactors`\n    exists target, ...\n```\n\nThe conclusion needs to provide a `ForwardRecoveryInputs` package whose `expectedFactors = coreFactors`, the matching `CutProjectionHypotheses`, the support-partition count equality, and the `reassemblyExpansionComplete (normalizeForFactor f) coreFactors` producer at the actual first-success lift. Without that bridge, the proposed `factorFastCore_raw_irreducible_of_some` would have to silently assume the main BHKS correctness content.\n\nVerification performed before this comment:\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.PartitionRefinement` completed successfully (only pre-existing warnings in dependencies).\nEOF'","aggregated_output":"","exit_code":null,"status":"in_progress"}}
