A wrapper equality\n  ```lean\n  factorFastCoreWithBound core a primeData (initialHenselPrecision a) ... = some coreFactors\n  ```\n  only identifies the returned array; it does not expose a `target` precision, `ForwardRecoveryInputs`, `CutProjectionHypotheses`, true supports, or a partition-count certificate for that returned array.\n- The existing raw irreducibility theorem is still conditional on exactly those missing facts: `factorFastFactorsWithBound_raw_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs` requires `ForwardRecoveryInputs`, `CutProjectionHypotheses`, the support-partition count equality, and `reassemblyExpansionComplete` as hypotheses (`HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/PartitionRefinement.lean:1010-1055`). Its core dependency has the same shape (`..._some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_forwardInputs`, lines 722-740).\n- `ForwardRecoveryInputs` itself contains the substantive B7/A2/lattice/product data (`Recovery.lean:767-801`), not data implied by executable success. Existing constructors around `ofMignottePrecision...AtPrecisionForCoeffBound` target `Hex.factorFastPrecisionCap f` (`Recovery.lean:2034-2080`), while this issue needs the public default-bound fast branch (`factorFastFactorsWithBound f B`, with `B = defaultFactorCoeffBound f` for #6672). The cap-level termination wrappers around `factorFastCapLiftData` (`Recovery.lean:8039-8068`) do not provide the raw-factor irreducibility substrate for that default-bound branch.\n- The wrapper control flow confirms why a wrapper-level `some` theorem is too broad and why the core branch needs a separate certificate producer: `factorFastFactorsWithBound` has constant, small-mod singleton, quadratic, and BHKS-core success `some` branches (`HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean:7610-7643`), and `factorFastFactorsWithBound_eq_some_of_core_success` only reassembles a supplied core success; it does not prove the core output is the true-factor partition (`Basic.lean:7674-7690`).\n\nSmallest missing lemma shape I see:\n\n```lean\n-- name/signature sketch only\ntheorem factorFastCore_success_forwardCertificates\n    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (B : Nat) (primeData : Hex.PrimeChoiceData)\n    {coreFactors : Array Hex.ZPoly}\n    (hB_pos : 1 <= B)\n    (hchoose : Hex.choosePrimeData? (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore = some primeData)\n    (hdeg : (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore.degree?.getD 0 != 0)\n    (hmulti : 1 < primeData.factorsModP.size)\n    (hquadratic : B = 1 '\"\\\\/ Hex.quadraticIntegerRootFactors? (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore = none)\n    (hcore :\n      let a := Hex.precisionForCoeffBound B primeData.p\n      Hex.factorFastCoreWithBound (Hex.normalizeForFactor f).squareFreeCore a primeData\n        (Hex.initialHenselPrecision a) (Hex.ZPoly.quadraticDoublingSteps a + 2) = some coreFactors) :\n    -- either expose the actual first-success target on the schedule, or prove\n    -- the certificates directly for \"'`coreFactors`\n    exists target, ...\n```\n\nThe conclusion needs to provide a `ForwardRecoveryInputs` package whose `expectedFactors = coreFactors`, the matching `CutProjectionHypotheses`, the support-partition count equality, and the `reassemblyExpansionComplete (normalizeForFactor f) coreFactors` producer at the actual first-success lift. Without that bridge, the proposed `factorFastCore_raw_irreducible_of_some` would have to silently assume the main BHKS correctness content.\n\nVerification performed before this comment:\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.PartitionRefinement` completed successfully (only pre-existing warnings in dependencies).\nEOF'","aggregated_output":"https://github.com/kim-em/hex/issues/7765#issuecomment-4731125893\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/c91c960d-b268-49eb-bd9f-2fa8567d2cdd.stdout:87:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_46","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '7600,7905p' HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean\"","aggregated_output":"/-- When `recombinationSearchMod` succeeds on the lifted-factor list, the\n`recombineExhaustive` wrapper returns exactly the array of recovered factors.\nThis is the bridge that lets downstream irreducibility proofs replace a\n`recombineExhaustive` term with a concrete factor list once the search is\nknown to succeed. -/\ntheorem recombineExhaustive_eq_of_recombinationSearchMod_some\n    {f : ZPoly} {d : LiftData} {factors : List ZPoly}\n    (h : recombinationSearchMod f (liftModulus d) d.liftedFactors.toList = some factors) :\n    recombineExhaustive f d = factors.toArray := by\n  unfold recombineExhaustive\n  rw [h]\n\ntheorem exhaustiveCoreFactorsWithBound_normalizeFactorSign\n    (core : ZPoly) (B : Nat) (primeData : PrimeChoiceData)\n    (hcore : normalizeFactorSign core = core) :\n    ∀ factor ∈ (exhaustiveCoreFactorsWithBound core B primeData).toList,\n      normalizeFactorSign factor = factor := by\n  rw [exhaustiveCoreFactorsWithBound]\n  by_cases hB : B = 0\n  · simp [hB, hcore]\n  · simp only [hB, if_false]\n    by_cases hempty :\n        (recombineExhaustive core\n            (henselLiftData core (precisionForCoeffBound B primeData.p) primeData)).isEmpty\n    · simp [hempty, hcore]\n    · simp only [hempty]\n      exact recombineExhaustive_normalizeFactorSign core\n        (henselLiftData core (precisionForCoeffBound B primeData.p) primeData)\n\ntheorem exhaustiveCoreFactorsWithBound_shouldRecord\n    (core : ZPoly) (B : Nat) (primeData : PrimeChoiceData)\n    (hcore : shouldRecordPolynomialFactor core = true) :\n    ∀ factor ∈ (exhaustiveCoreFactorsWithBound core B primeData).toList,\n      shouldRecordPolynomialFactor factor = true := by\n  rw [exhaustiveCoreFactorsWithBound]\n  by_cases hB : B = 0\n  · simp [hB, hcore]\n  · simp only [hB, if_false]\n    by_cases hempty :\n        (recombineExhaustive core\n            (henselLiftData core (precisionForCoeffBound B primeData.p) primeData)).isEmpty\n    · simp [hempty, hcore]\n    · simp only [hempty]\n      exact recombineExhaustive_shouldRecord core\n        (henselLiftData core (precisionForCoeffBound B primeData.p) primeData)\n\nprivate theorem bhksRecoverClassified_success_product\n    {f : ZPoly} {d : LiftData} {candidates : Array ZPoly}\n    (hrecover : bhksRecoverClassified f d = .success candidates) :\n    Array.polyProduct candidates = f := by\n  rw [bhksRecoverClassified] at hrecover\n  by_cases hrows : 1 ≤ (bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors).factorCount +\n      (bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors).coeffWidth\n  · rw [dif_pos hrows] at hrecover\n    by_cases hdeg :\n        bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition\n          (bhksProjectedRows (bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors) hrows)\n          (bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators\n            (bhksProjectedRows (bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors)\n              hrows)) = true\n    · simp [hdeg] at hrecover\n    · simp only [hdeg, Bool.false_eq_true, if_false] at hrecover\n      cases hcand : bhksIndicatorCandidates? f d\n          (bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators\n            (bhksProjectedRows (bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors)\n              hrows)) with\n      | none => simp [hcand] at hrecover\n      | some cands =>\n          simp only [hcand] at hrecover\n          by_cases hprod : Array.polyProduct cands == f\n          · simp only [hprod, if_true] at hrecover\n            cases hrecover\n            simpa [beq_iff_eq] using hprod\n          · simp [hprod] at hrecover\n  · rw [dif_neg hrows] at hrecover\n    simp at hrecover\n\nprivate theorem bhksRecoverClassified_success_all_of_candidates\n    (P : ZPoly → Prop)\n    (hall :\n      ∀ {f : ZPoly} {d : LiftData} {indicators : Array (Array Int)}\n        {candidates : Array ZPoly},\n        bhksIndicatorCandidates? f d indicators = some candidates →\n          ∀ factor ∈ candidates.toList, P factor)\n    {f : ZPoly} {d : LiftData} {candidates : Array ZPoly}\n    (hrecover : bhksRecoverClassified f d = .success candidates) :\n    ∀ factor ∈ candidates.toList, P factor := by\n  rw [bhksRecoverClassified] at hrecover\n  by_cases hrows : 1 ≤ (bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors).factorCount +\n      (bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors).coeffWidth\n  · rw [dif_pos hrows] at hrecover\n    let projected :=\n      bhksProjectedRows (bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors) hrows\n    let indicators := bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators projected\n    by_cases hdeg : bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition projected indicators = true\n    · simp [projected, indicators, hdeg] at hrecover\n    · simp only [projected, indicators, hdeg, Bool.false_eq_true, if_false] at hrecover\n      cases hcand : bhksIndicatorCandidates? f d indicators with\n      | none => simp [projected, indicators, hcand] at hrecover\n      | some cands =>\n          simp only [projected, indicators, hcand] at hrecover\n          by_cases hprod : Array.polyProduct cands == f\n          · simp only [hprod, if_true] at hrecover\n            cases hrecover\n            exact hall hcand\n          · simp [hprod] at hrecover\n  · rw [dif_neg hrows] at hrecover\n    simp at hrecover\n\nprivate theorem bhksRecoverClassified_success_normalizeFactorSign\n    {f : ZPoly} {d : LiftData} {candidates : Array ZPoly}\n    (h : bhksRecoverClassified f d = .success candidates) :\n    ∀ factor ∈ candidates.toList, normalizeFactorSign factor = factor :=\n  bhksRecoverClassified_success_all_of_candidates\n    (fun factor => normalizeFactorSign factor = factor)\n    (fun hcand => bhksIndicatorCandidates?_normalizeFactorSign hcand) h\n\nprivate theorem bhksRecoverClassified_success_shouldRecord\n    {f : ZPoly} {d : LiftData} {candidates : Array ZPoly}\n    (h : bhksRecoverClassified f d = .success candidates) :\n    ∀ factor ∈ candidates.toList, shouldRecordPolynomialFactor factor = true :=\n  bhksRecoverClassified_success_all_of_candidates\n    (fun factor => shouldRecordPolynomialFactor factor = true)\n    (fun hcand => bhksIndicatorCandidates?_shouldRecord hcand) h\n\n/-- A successful BHKS recovery emits only candidates that divide `f`,\nsince each candidate has passed the executable exact-division check\ninside `bhksIndicatorCandidate?`.  The dependence of the conclusion on\n`f` prevents a one-liner via `bhksRecoverClassified_success_all_of_candidates`,\nso we unfold `bhksRecoverClassified` directly. -/\nprivate theorem bhksRecoverClassified_success_dvd\n    {f : ZPoly} {d : LiftData} {candidates : Array ZPoly}\n    (hrecover : bhksRecoverClassified f d = .success candidates) :\n    ∀ factor ∈ candidates.toList, factor ∣ f := by\n  rw [bhksRecoverClassified] at hrecover\n  by_cases hrows : 1 ≤ (bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors).factorCount +\n      (bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors).coeffWidth\n  · rw [dif_pos hrows] at hrecover\n    let projected :=\n      bhksProjectedRows (bhksLatticeBasis f d.p d.k d.liftedFactors) hrows\n    let indicators := bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators projected\n    by_cases hdeg : bhksDegenerateIndicatorPartition projected indicators = true\n    · simp [projected, indicators, hdeg] at hrecover\n    · simp only [projected, indicators, hdeg, Bool.false_eq_true, if_false] at hrecover\n      cases hcand : bhksIndicatorCandidates? f d indicators with\n      | none => simp [projected, indicators, hcand] at hrecover\n      | some cands =>\n          simp only [projected, indicators, hcand] at hrecover\n          by_cases hprod : Array.polyProduct cands == f\n          · simp only [hprod, if_true] at hrecover\n            cases hrecover\n            exact bhksIndicatorCandidates?_dvd hcand\n          · simp [hprod] at hrecover\n  · rw [dif_neg hrows] at hrecover\n    simp at hrecover\n\n/-- A successful BHKS recovery call preserves the polynomial product: when\n`bhksRecover? f d` returns `some candidates`, the candidates multiply back\nto `f` because the executable runs a final `Array.polyProduct candidates == f`\ncheck before reporting success. -/\nprivate theorem bhksRecover?_product\n    {f : ZPoly} {d : LiftData} {candidates : Array ZPoly}\n    (hrecover : bhksRecover? f d = some candidates) :\n    Array.polyProduct candidates = f := by\n  rw [bhksRecover?] at hrecover\n  cases hclass : bhksRecoverClassified f d with\n  | success cands =>\n      simp [BhksRecoveryResult.toOption, hclass] at hrecover\n      cases hrecover\n      exact bhksRecoverClassified_success_product hclass\n  | degenerate =>\n      simp [BhksRecoveryResult.toOption, hclass] at hrecover\n  | candidateFailure =>\n      simp [BhksRecoveryResult.toOption, hclass] at hrecover\n  | productMismatch cands =>\n      simp [BhksRecoveryResult.toOption, hclass] at hrecover\n\n/-- A successful fixed-precision BHKS fast-recombination loop preserves the\npolynomial product: every success branch comes from the classified BHKS\nrecovery success case, which already certifies `Array.polyProduct = core`. -/\ntheorem factorFastCoreWithBound_product\n    (core : ZPoly) (B : Nat) (primeData : PrimeChoiceData) :\n    ∀ k fuel coreFactors,\n      factorFastCoreWithBound core B primeData k fuel = some coreFactors →\n        Array.polyProduct coreFactors = core := by\n  intro k fuel\n  induction fuel generalizing k with\n  | zero =>\n      intro coreFactors hfast\n      simp [factorFastCoreWithBound] at hfast\n  | succ fuel ih =>\n      intro coreFactors hfast\n      rw [factorFastCoreWithBound] at hfast\n      cases hclass : bhksRecoverClassified core (henselLiftData core k primeData) with\n      | success xs =>\n          simp [hclass] at hfast\n          cases hfast\n          exact bhksRecoverClassified_success_product hclass\n      | degenerate =>\n          by_cases hk : k ≥ B\n          · simp [hclass, hk] at hfast\n          · simp [hclass, hk] at hfast\n            exact ih _ coreFactors hfast\n      | candidateFailure =>\n          by_cases hk : k ≥ B\n          · simp [hclass, hk] at hfast\n          · simp [hclass, hk] at hfast\n            exact ih _ coreFactors hfast\n      | productMismatch cands =>\n          by_cases hk : k ≥ B\n          · simp [hclass, hk] at hfast\n          · simp [hclass, hk] at hfast\n            exact ih _ coreFactors hfast\n\nprivate theorem factorFastCoreWithBound_some_all_of_recovery\n    (P : ZPoly → Prop)\n    (hrecover :\n      ∀ {core : ZPoly} {d : LiftData} {candidates : Array ZPoly},\n        bhksRecoverClassified core d = .success candidates →\n          ∀ factor ∈ candidates.toList, P factor)\n    (core : ZPoly) (B : Nat) (primeData : PrimeChoiceData) :\n    ∀ k fuel coreFactors,\n      factorFastCoreWithBound core B primeData k fuel = some coreFactors →\n        ∀ factor ∈ coreFactors.toList, P factor := by\n  intro k fuel\n  induction fuel generalizing k with\n  | zero =>\n      intro coreFactors hfast\n      simp [factorFastCoreWithBound] at hfast\n  | succ fuel ih =>\n      intro coreFactors hfast\n      rw [factorFastCoreWithBound] at hfast\n      cases hclass : bhksRecoverClassified core (henselLiftData core k primeData) with\n      | success xs =>\n          simp [hclass] at hfast\n          cases hfast\n          exact hrecover hclass\n      | degenerate =>\n          by_cases hk : k ≥ B\n          · simp [hclass, hk] at hfast\n          · simp [hclass, hk] at hfast\n            exact ih _ coreFactors hfast\n      | candidateFailure =>\n          by_cases hk : k ≥ B\n          · simp [hclass, hk] at hfast\n          · simp [hclass, hk] at hfast\n            exact ih _ coreFactors hfast\n      | productMismatch cands =>\n          by_cases hk : k ≥ B\n          · simp [hclass, hk] at hfast\n          · simp [hclass, hk] at hfast\n            exact ih _ coreFactors hfast\n\nprivate theorem factorFastCoreWithBound_some_normalizeFactorSign\n    {core : ZPoly} {B : Nat} {primeData : PrimeChoiceData}\n    {k fuel : Nat} {coreFactors : Array ZPoly}\n    (h : factorFastCoreWithBound core B primeData k fuel = some coreFactors) :\n    ∀ factor ∈ coreFactors.toList, normalizeFactorSign factor = factor :=\n  factorFastCoreWithBound_some_all_of_recovery\n    (fun factor => normalizeFactorSign factor = factor)\n    (fun hrecover => bhksRecoverClassified_success_normalizeFactorSign hrecover)\n    core B primeData k fuel coreFactors h\n\ntheorem factorFastCoreWithBound_some_shouldRecord\n    {core : ZPoly} {B : Nat} {primeData : PrimeChoiceData}\n    {k fuel : Nat} {coreFactors : Array ZPoly}\n    (h : factorFastCoreWithBound core B primeData k fuel = some coreFactors) :\n    ∀ factor ∈ coreFactors.toList, shouldRecordPolynomialFactor factor = true :=\n  factorFastCoreWithBound_some_all_of_recovery\n    (fun factor => shouldRecordPolynomialFactor factor = true)\n    (fun hrecover => bhksRecoverClassified_success_shouldRecord hrecover)\n    core B primeData k fuel coreFactors h\n\n/-- Every factor emitted by the BHKS fast-recombination loop divides the\ninput core. The success branch is the only branch that exits with\n`some coreFactors`, and `bhksRecoverClassified_success_dvd` certifies\ndivisibility for each candidate at that exit. -/\ntheorem factorFastCoreWithBound_some_dvd\n    (core : ZPoly) (B : Nat) (primeData : PrimeChoiceData) :\n    ∀ k fuel coreFactors,\n      factorFastCoreWithBound core B primeData k fuel = some coreFactors →\n        ∀ factor ∈ coreFactors.toList, factor ∣ core := by\n  intro k fuel\n  induction fuel generalizing k with\n  | zero =>\n      intro coreFactors hfast\n      simp [factorFastCoreWithBound] at hfast\n  | succ fuel ih =>\n      intro coreFactors hfast\n      rw [factorFastCoreWithBound] at hfast\n      cases hclass : bhksRecoverClassified core (henselLiftData core k primeData) with\n      | success xs =>\n          simp [hclass] at hfast\n          cases hfast\n          exact bhksRecoverClassified_success_dvd hclass\n      | degenerate =>\n          by_cases hk : k ≥ B\n          · simp [hclass, hk] at hfast\n          · simp [hclass, hk] at hfast\n            exact ih _ coreFactors hfast\n      | candidateFailure =>\n          by_cases hk : k ≥ B\n          · simp [hclass, hk] at hfast\n          · simp [hclass, hk] at hfast\n            exact ih _ coreFactors hfast\n      | productMismatch cands =>\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/05b08440-8fc9-412e-b095-1b23f024f8f0.stdout:14:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_6","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,200p' progress/20260617_aad44673.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Progress - 2026-06-17 (session aad44673, one-shot #7592)\n\n## Accomplished\n\nClaimed #7592 (feat: add BHKS cut-separation prefix certificate, critical-path)\nvia `pod once`. Split into its two staged deliverables: the executable prefix\ncut (\"lands first\") and the `CutRetention` proof. **Landed the executable\nprefix cut; rejected the `CutRetention` proof half as an unsound target** (same\ndefect #7575 already diagnosed, invariant under the prefix change), with a\nmachine-checked comment on the issue.\n\n### Executable prefix cut (landed)\n\n`HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`: added `bhksCutPrefixCount` (computes\n`t = (max passing GS index) + 1` by an increasing-order fold) and rewired\n`bhksCutProjectReducedRows` / `bhksRetainedRowIndices` to retain the contiguous\nprefix `b_0 … b_t` in original order, including earlier rows whose own GS norm\nexceeds the radius. This is the genuine BHKS Lemma 5.7 prefix and fixes the\nnon-prefix defect diagnosed on #7578 (per-row survivor set was not a GS-norm\nprefix under LLL).\n\nVerification:\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus` — clean.\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.Lattice` — clean (proof layer intact;\n  nothing constructs `CutRetention`, so the runtime change breaks no proof).\n- Regenerated `conformance-fixtures/HexBerlekampZassenhaus/bz.jsonl` via\n  `hexbz_emit_fixtures` — **byte-identical** to the committed flint-verified\n  ground truth. Factorization output is unchanged (survivor set only grows;\n  final factor multisets match flint).\n- Added-line forbidden-token scan: no sorry/axiom/native_decide/TODO/FIXME.\n\n### `CutRetention` proof half (rejected as unsound — premise defect)\n\n`CutRetention.mem_projected_of_norm_le` (`Lattice.lean:424`) quantifies over\n**arbitrary** `v : Fin r → ℤ` with only a norm bound and concludes membership in\nthe **projected** span `projectedRowSpanInt`. This is unsatisfiable — the same\ndefect #7575 posted (kernel-checked `IsEmpty (CutRetention P)`) — and the prefix\nchange does **not** repair it. Machine-checked under the new prefix code:\n`factorCount=1, coeffWidth=0, reduced=[[2]]` gives `cutRadiusSq4=4`,\n`bhksCutPrefixCount=0`, `bhksCutProjectReducedRows=#[]`, so\n`projectedRowSpanInt=⊥`, yet `v=![1]` has `‖v‖²=1 ≤ 4`. Membership in a\nsublattice never follows from a norm bound alone.\n\nThe issue's *Proof section* states the SOUND fact correctly (\"any\n**original-lattice** vector v with `‖v‖²≤radius` lies in the integer span of the\nretained prefix rows\"). The defect is only the packaging: the sound statement is\nabout lattice vectors of the `(r+n)`-dim reduced basis, not arbitrary `ℤ^r`\nvectors, and not the projected span. `CutRetention` drops the lattice hypothesis\nand projects — that is where soundness is lost.\n\n## Current frontier\n\nExecutable prefix cut is the substrate any sound survivor-span proof needs.\nLanded via `create-pr --partial` (issue → `replan`). Comment on #7592 gives the\ncorrected target.\n\n## Next step (for the replan)\n\nProve the full-lattice survivor-span lemma directly into `CutProjectionHypotheses`\n(NOT `CutRetention`, which should be retired along with\n`cutProjectionHypotheses_of_retention` / `..._of_retention`):\n- For `v` with `memLattice reducedMatrix v` and `‖v‖² ≤ R`, `v ∈` ℤ-span of the\n  prefix rows `b_0 … b_t`, via `basis_normSq` (gramDetVec ↔ `‖b*_i‖²`,\n  `HexGramSchmidtMathlib/Int.lean:1070`) and the integer-top-coefficient /\n  orthogonality lemmas (`normSq_latticeVec_ge_min_basis_normSq`,\n  `rowCombination_normSq_ge_of_orthogonal_coeff_sq_ge_one`,\n  `HexGramSchmidt/Int.lean`).\n- Then the factorization→lattice bridge #7578 listed as absent: true-support\n  indicators are projections of genuine short lattice vectors (CLD-vanishing for\n  true factors), so `CutProjectionHypotheses` holds at exactly the indicators it\n  is consumed at (`PartitionRefinement.lean:484`).\n\n## Blockers\n\nNone for the landed change. The proof half is blocked on a corrected,\nnon-`CutRetention` target (premise defect), routed to replan.\nNote: the worktree carries pre-existing unrelated `.claude/*` modifications from\nbefore this session; not touched or staged.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

The worktree still has an unrelated pre-existing modification\nto `.claude/CLAUDE.md`; this session did not touch or stage it.\n\n### progress/20260616T123807Z_ecc34fae.md\n# Progress - 2026-06-16T12:38:07Z (session ecc34fae, work)\n\n## Accomplished\n\nClaimed issue #7582 after the blocked directive claim was rejected by\ncoordination, and extended `HexModArithMathlib/Basic.lean` with the requested\n`ZMod64.toZMod` simp surface:\n\n- `ofZMod_one`\n- `toZMod_neg`\n- `toZMod_sub`\n- `toZMod_natCast`\n- `toZMod_intCast`\n- `toZMod_pow`\n\nThe new lemmas preserve the existing equivalence and round-trip API unchanged.\nSubtraction, negation, and powers are proved by casting the executable reduced\nrepresentative equations into `ZMod p`, avoiding stronger `ZMod.val_*`\nside conditions.\n\nVerification:\n- `lake build HexModArithMathlib`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- `rg -n \"\\\\bsorry\\\\b|\\\\baxiom\\\\b|\\\\bnative_decide\\\\b|TODO|FIXME\" HexModArithMathlib/Basic.lean`\n- `git diff --check`\n\n## Current frontier\n\n`HexModArithMathlib` now has direct projection normalization for the\nring-facing executable operations called out by #7582.\n\n## Next step\n\nDownstream Mathlib-side proofs can use the new simp lemmas to normalize\n`ZMod64.toZMod` over subtraction, negation, casts, and powers without routing\nmanually through the full equivalence.\n\n## Blockers\n\nNone for #7582. The worktree still contains a pre-existing unrelated\nmodification to `.claude/CLAUDE.md`; this session did not stage or edit it.\n\n### progress/20260616T125450Z_c36b14d6.md\n# Progress - 2026-06-16T12:54:50Z (session c36b14d6, work #7584)\n\n## Accomplished\n\nClaimed #7584 (one-shot) and produced the core-facts-only toMonic Hensel\ncorrespondence / lifted-subset partition producer surface, the corrected\nsuccessor to the #7550/#7569/#7573 chain.\n\nAfter #7550 narrowed `exists_subset` to sign-normalized factors, #7569/#7573\nremoved/guarded the false non-monic unscaled support field, and #7362 supplied\n`initialLiftedFactorSubsetPartitionEvidence_of_toMonicChoosePrimeData`, the last\ncaller obligations on the toMonic substrate were the `MonicDescentHypotheses`\ncarrier and the `InitialLiftedFactorSubsetPartitionEvidence` input. Both are now\ndischargeable, so I added three additive producers (153 lines, no deletions):\n\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean`:\n  `henselSubsetCorrespondenceHypotheses_of_toMonicPrimeData` — carrier-free\n  correspondence. At the `True True` instantiation the only `MonicDescentHypotheses`\n  fields the descent variant consumed (`lift_eq`, `successful_lift`) are\n  `rfl`/`trivial`, so no descent carrier is needed; existence/uniqueness come from\n  `toMonicLiftData_represents_lifted_of_modP` / `toMonicLiftData_unique_subset`.\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/IntReductionMod.lean`:\n  `liftedFactorSubsetPartition_of_toMonicPrimeData_complete` — concludes\n  `LiftedFactorSubsetPartition core (toMonicLiftData …) Finset.univ core` from a\n  `toMonicPrimeData?` selection witness plus standard core facts (lc>0, deg>0,\n  primitive, squarefree, B≠0, the monic-correspondent precision bound). Discharges\n  `hcorr` via the carrier-free producer and `hinitial` via\n  `initialLiftedFactorSubsetPartitionEvidence_of_toMonicChoosePrimeData`; derives\n  `hprecision` from the bound.\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/IntReductionMod.lean`:\n  `slowPathHenselSubstrate_of_toMonicPrimeData` — `SlowPathHenselSubstrate core B\n  primeData` from the same inputs alone, routing `corr`/`partition` through the two\n  producers above.\n\nThe non-monic path routes through the recovered/dilated `liftedRecoveryCandidate`\ncoordinate (deliverable 2); the monic-only unscaled\n`support_subset_of_dvd_recombinationCandidate` field stays guarded by\n`leadingCoeff core = 1` (deliverable 4, untouched).\n\nVerification (clean tree baseline was green before edits):\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.Basic`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.IntReductionMod`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py` (exit 0)\n- `git diff --check` (clean)\n- Added-line forbidden-token scan: no new `sorry`/`axiom`/`native_decide`/`TODO`/`FIXME`.\n\n## Current frontier\n\nThe toMonic correspondence / partition / slow-path substrate packages now have a\nthin producer from the executable `toMonicPrimeData?` selection witness and core\nfacts alone, with no analytic-recovery or descent-carrier caller obligation.\n\n## Next step\n\nDeliverable 3 (wire the producer into the fast-BHKS / slow-modular capstone call\nsites) has no actionable target in the current tree: the toMonic\n`SlowPathHenselSubstrate` chain is producer-side infrastructure with no downstream\nconsumer yet, and the slow-exhaustive branch (`liftedFactorSubsetPartition_outerBound_of_choosePrimeData`,\nIntReductionMod.lean:3803/4172) is keyed on `choosePrimeData? squareFreeCore`, a\ndifferent selection than `toMonicPrimeData?`. The capstones that will consume these\n(#6771, #7561) need their consumption points built (and #6771 remains blocked on\nthe separate cut heart #6779). The producers expose the exact\n`LiftedFactorSubsetPartition core d Finset.univ core` and `SlowPathHenselSubstrate\ncore B primeData` shapes those capstones require, so the wiring is a thin step once\nthe consumer side lands.\n\n## Blockers\n\nNone for this producer surface. Downstream capstone wiring waits on the consumer\nsites (#6771 → cut heart #6779; #7561 re-key).\n\n### progress/20260616T133736Z_cdc115b0.md\n# Progress - 2026-06-16T13:37:36Z (session cdc115b0, work #7590)\n\n## Accomplished\n\nClaimed #7590 after the higher-priority directive #2564 was rejected by\ncoordination as blocked. Audited the executable BHKS cut path in\n`HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`, the proof-facing\n`CutProjectionHypotheses` / `CutRetention` surfaces in\n`HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Lattice.lean`, and the forward-count consumers in\n`PartitionRefinement.lean` / `Recovery.lean`.\n\nChose the sound route for the BHKS cut semantics: keep the current per-row\nexecutable Gram-Schmidt cut in `Hex.bhksProjectedRows`, and make the needed\nprefix behavior explicit as a proof-facing `CutSeparation` / van-Hoeij gap\ncertificate. A first-fail prefix cut would still be false on the known\nLLL-reduced witness, and sorting rows by norm would require a new semantic proof\nfor Gram-Schmidt coordinates.\n\nCreated critical-path successor feature issues:\n\n- #7591: expose BHKS projected-row trace and LLL span bridge.\n- #7592: add BHKS cut-separation prefix certificate.\n- #7593: place true-factor CLD vectors in the BHKS lattice.\n- #7594: produce `CutProjectionHypotheses` from separation and true-factor\n  vectors.\n\nRecorded the decision and successor decomposition in\n`reports/hbz-bhks-d1-frontier.md`.\n\nVerification:\n\n- `git diff --check`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- Added-line forbidden-token scan on `reports/hbz-bhks-d1-frontier.md`\n\n## Current frontier\n\nThe false #7585 proof shape is no longer queued as an unconditional proof task.\nThe next executable worker target is #7591, which opens the trace/span surface\nneeded before the cut-separation theorem can be stated against real runtime\ndata.\n\n## Next step\n\nClaim #7591 and expose the public trace for `bhksProjectedRows` together with\nthe `lll.shortVectorsUnchecked` / `.toArray` lattice-span bridge. Once that\nlands, #7592 can state the gap-conditioned prefix theorem over actual reduced\nrows and retained indices.\n\n## Blockers\n\nNo blocker for #7591. #7592, #7593, and #7594 are intentionally blocked by the\ndependency chain created during this session.\n\n### progress/20260616T145659Z_0dd85598.md\n# Progress - 2026-06-16T14:56:59Z (session 0dd85598, summarize #7596)\n\n## Accomplished\n\nWrote the fifty-eighth Phase 4/5/6 checkpoint summary,\n`reports/phase4-5-checkpoint-58.md`, covering the 44 PRs merged after summarize\nissue #7459 closed (2026-06-15T15:02:49Z) through #7595 (2026-06-16T14:31).\n\nGrouped the landed work by theme rather than chronologically:\n\n- Slow-path toMonic / monic-descent substrate rebuild (the dominant theme:\n  #7504/#7522/#7524/#7528/#7529/#7531/#7535/#7536/#7543/#7545/#7546/#7556/\n  #7564/#7565/#7573/#7589/#7511/#7518).\n- BHKS §5 joint recovery + CLD bound chain (#7485/#7490/#7491/#7498/#7507/\n  #7510/#7523/#7547/#7552/#7553/#7562/#7566/#7571).\n- The new fast-path cut-separation route: #7595 planner PR + the #7590 decision\n  to retire the false first-fail prefix cut and route through a proof-facing\n  `CutSeparation` certificate (#7591 claimed → #7592/#7593/#7594).\n- Phase 6 API polish (#7586/#7587/#7588/#7567/#7493) and the doc/skill-gotcha,\n  review, and chore stream (#7501/#7526/#7548/#7554/#7557/#7570/#7576).\n\nRecorded current quality metrics (unchanged from checkpoint 57): 68 `sorry`,\nall in non-CI-built Mathlib-side layers; 0 `axiom`; 0 real `native_decide`\n(2 comment hits); 1 `TODO`/`FIXME` comment. The executable Mathlib-free stack\nremains proof-debt-free.\n\nCaptured the frontier: directive #2564 capstone (two headline `sorry`s in\nFactorSoundness.lean and HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean), with the\nslow-path substrate built-but-blocked at #6771/#6672 and the fast-path\ncut-separation chain (#7591 head) the only unblocked critical path.\n\n## Current frontier\n\nNo code touched. In `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/IntReductionMod.lean`, prove a default-bound\n   slow-modular raw-factor irreducibility theorem: if\n   `Hex.factorFastFactorsWithBound f (Hex.ZPoly.defaultFactorCoeffBound f) = none`\n   and\n   `Hex.factorSlowModularFactorsWithBound f (Hex.ZPoly.defaultFactorCoeffBound f) =\n    some rawFactors`, then every `raw ∈ rawFactors.toList` is\n   `Hex.ZPoly.Irreducible raw`.\n2. Route the exhaustive branch through the landed `toMonicPrimeData?` producer\n   stack and\n   `exhaustiveCoreFactorsWithBound_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_henselSubsetCorrespondence_of_bound`.\n   Handle the constant/quadratic shortcut arms with the existing branch-specific\n   irreducibility lemmas; do not add a new substrate assumption.\n3. Expose the theorem in the shape #6672 can plug directly into the middle\n   disjunct of `factor_entries_irreducible`'s `h_raw` hypothesis, but do not\n   assemble #6672 itself.\n\n## Library placement\n\nTarget file: `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/IntReductionMod.lean`.\n\nSPEC section: `SPEC/Libraries/hex-berlekamp-zassenhaus-mathlib.md` says the\nMathlib bridge must prove:\n\n> `theorem factor_irreducible_of_nonUnit (f : ZPoly) :\n>     ∀ (g, m) ∈ (factor f).factors, Hex.ZPoly.Irreducible g`\n\n1. **SPEC §:** this is a branch-level input to that bridge theorem, under the\n   `hex-berlekamp-zassenhaus-mathlib` factor irreducibility contract.\n2. **Does the natural strategy use Mathlib?** Yes. The proof consumes\n   `Squarefree`, polynomial irreducibility bridges, and the existing Mathlib-side\n   Hensel substrate, so it belongs in the bridge library.\n3. **Mathlib status:** no new upstream Mathlib result is expected; reuse the\n   in-repo bridge lemmas named above.\n4. **Missing infrastructure:** none known. If the worker finds that the current\n   executable branch classifier does not expose the selected `toMonicPrimeData?`\n   witness needed by the producer stack, stop and decompose that precise bridge\n   as the prerequisite rather than adding a hypothesis to the final theorem.\n\n## Context\n\n- Parent directive: #2564.\n- Consumer: #6672 (`factor_irreducible_of_nonUnit` capstone).\n- Stale predecessor: #7561, now closed after #7560/#7550/#7569/#7584 landed.\n- Relevant files:\n  - `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/IntReductionMod.lean`\n  - `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean`\n  - `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`\n- Roadmap: `PLAN/Phase5.md` implementation work loop; this is one theorem\n  cluster feeding the Phase 5/HO-1 proof capstone.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.IntReductionMod`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- `git diff --check`\n- Added-line forbidden-token scan over touched Lean files shows no new\n  `sorry`, `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, or `FIXME`.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- The fast BHKS prefix-cut chain (#7591/#7592/#7593/#7594/#6771).\n- Assembling `factor_irreducible_of_nonUnit` in #6672.\n- Changing executable factorization behavior or `factorSlowModularFactorsWithBound`.\n\n\n### #7601 feat: expose DensePoly eval additive homomorphism simp surface (eval_add/sub/neg)\nlibrary: HexPoly\n\n## Current state\n\n`HexPoly/Operations.lean` defines the generic dense-polynomial evaluation\nmap via Horner's method:\n\n```\ndef eval [Add R] [Mul R] (p : DensePoly R) (x : R) : R :=\n  p.toArray.toList.reverse.foldl (fun acc coeff => acc * x + coeff) (Zero.zero : R)\n```\n\nThe `coeff` surface for the ring operations is already at full simp/grind\nparity — `coeff_add_semiring`, `coeff_sub_ring`, `coeff_neg_ring`, and\n`coeff_zero` all carry `@[simp, grind =]` (Operations.lean:584/608/616/629),\neach with a generic carrier theorem (`coeff_add`/`coeff_sub`/`coeff_neg`)\nplus a `Lean.Grind.Semiring`/`Lean.Grind.Ring`-specialized wrapper.\n\nThe `eval` surface is **not** at parity. Only three eval lemmas exist:\n\n- `eval_zero` (Operations.lean:689, `@[simp, grind =]`)\n- `eval_C` / `eval_C_semiring` (Operations.lean:695/712)\n- `eval_monomial_semiring` (Operations.lean:747)\n\nThe additive ring-homomorphism laws for `eval` are **missing entirely** —\na repo-wide search finds no `eval_add`, `eval_sub`, or `eval_neg` anywhere\nin `HexPoly/`. So `eval (p + q) x` does not normalize to\n`eval p x + eval q x` under `simp`/`grind`, even though the analogous\n`coeff` rewrites do.\n\n`HexPoly/Operations.lean` does **not** import Mathlib (it builds on the\n`Lean.Grind.Semiring` / `Lean.Grind.Ring` abstractions, matching the existing\n`*_semiring` / `*_ring` wrappers). This is executable-layer work, not a\nMathlib-bridge file.\n\n## Deliverables\n\nAdd the additive evaluation homomorphism surface to\n`HexPoly/Operations.lean`, mirroring the existing `coeff_*` carrier +\n`*_semiring`/`*_ring` wrapper pattern:\n\n1. `eval_add` — generic carrier theorem\n   `eval (p + q) x = eval p x + eval q x` over `[Add R] [Mul R]` with the\n   explicit zero/distribution side-laws the generic interface needs (follow\n   the shape of `coeff_add` / `eval_C`), plus an `eval_add_semiring`\n   `@[simp, grind =]` wrapper specialized to `Lean.Grind.Semiring`.\n2. `eval_sub` carrier + `eval_sub_ring` `@[simp, grind =]` wrapper\n   (`Lean.Grind.Ring`), matching `coeff_sub` / `coeff_sub_ring`.\n3. `eval_neg` carrier + `eval_neg_ring` `@[simp, grind =]` wrapper, expressed\n   through subtraction from zero exactly as `coeff_neg` / `coeff_neg_ring`\n   are, so the negation normal form agrees with the coeff side.\n\n## Out of scope (do NOT include)\n\n- `eval_mul` (multiplicativity, `eval (p * q) x = eval p x * eval q x`) is a\n  substantial Horner/convolution theorem and belongs in its own issue. Do not\n  attempt it here.\n- `eval_one` and `eval_X`: the `One (DensePoly R)` instance lives downstream\n  in `HexPoly/Euclid.lean` and there is no generic `X` def on `DensePoly`, so\n  these do not belong in `Operations.lean`. Leave them out.\n- Do not change the `eval` definition or any runtime semantics.\n\n## Context\n\nThis is Phase 6 foundational-API polish, the same theme as the recently\nlanded simp-surface parity work (HexGF2 #7586, HexPolyZ #7587, ZMod64 #7588,\nZPoly toPolynomial bridge #7600) and the in-flight FpPoly eval surface #7599.\nIt is independent of all of those and of the active BHKS cut-separation and\ntoMonic substrate chains — it touches only `HexPoly/Operations.lean`. The\nproof style to imitate is the existing `coeff_add` / `coeff_add_semiring`\npair directly above where the new lemmas land; the `Lean.Grind.Semiring`\n/ `Lean.Grind.Ring` abstractions provide the `mul`/`add`/`sub` algebraic\nlaws the Horner fold induction needs.\n\nNote on FpPoly #7599: that issue tags `FpPoly.eval_*` lemmas that already\nexist as plain theorems in `HexPolyFp/Basic.lean`. This issue is for the\ngeneric `DensePoly.eval` in `HexPoly/Operations.lean`, where the additive\nlemmas do not exist at all yet. No overlap.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexPoly` (and confirm the clean-tree baseline was green first).\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py` (exit 0).\n- `git diff --check`.\n- Added-line forbidden-token scan: no new\n  `sorry`/`axiom`/`native_decide`/`TODO`/`FIXME`.\n- Confirm the new `*_semiring`/`*_ring` wrappers actually fire as `simp`\n  lemmas (e.g. a scratch `example` reducing `eval (p + q) x`), matching how\n  `coeff_add_semiring` behaves.\n\n\n### #7599 feat: expose the FpPoly evaluation simp surface (eval_zero/one/neg, eval_* @[simp])\nlibrary: HexPolyFp\n\n## Current state\n\n`HexPolyFp/Basic.lean` defines the executable prime-field dense polynomial type\n`FpPoly p` together with its evaluation map `eval f x : ZMod64 p`. The\nconstructor-facing `coeff_*` lemmas (`coeff_C`, `coeff_X_zero`, `coeff_X_one`,\n`coeff_X`) are already `@[simp]`, so `coeff` goals normalize cleanly. The\nevaluation surface is *not* at parity:\n\n- `eval_C`, `eval_X`, `eval_monomial`, `eval_add`, `eval_sub`, `eval_C_mul`,\n  and `eval_mul` all exist as plain theorems but **none carry `@[simp]`**.\n- `eval_one` exists but is `private` (so it cannot be used downstream or tagged).\n- There is **no** `eval_zero` and **no** `eval_neg` lemma at all.\n\nThe evaluation map `(eval · x) : FpPoly p → ZMod64 p` is a ring homomorphism, so\nits action on the ring leaves and constructors (`0`, `1`, `C`, `X`, `monomial`,\n`+`, `-`, `neg`, `·`, `C ·`) is the natural simp normal form — exactly mirroring\nthe already-`@[simp]` `coeff_*` surface. This is Phase 6 executable-API polish,\nin the same vein as PR #7587 (HexPolyZ unit/rational-cast API) and PR #7586\n(HexGF2 Euclid gcd/inverse API), but for a different, untouched file.\n\n## Deliverables\n\nAll changes confined to `HexPolyFp/Basic.lean`, additive (no statement weakening,\nno deletions beyond the `private` → public promotion):\n\n1. Add the two missing leaf lemmas next to the existing eval cluster:\n   - `eval_zero (x : ZMod64 p) : eval (0 : FpPoly p) x = 0`\n   - `eval_neg (f : FpPoly p) (x : ZMod64 p) : eval (-f) x = -(eval f x)`\n   Prove them from the existing eval/coeff infrastructure (e.g. via `eval_sub`\n   with `f = 0`, or directly); do not introduce `sorry`/`axiom`.\n2. Make `eval_one` public (drop `private`) so it can be tagged and reused.\n3. Promote the leaf/constructor evaluation lemmas to `@[simp]`:\n   `eval_zero`, `eval_one`, `eval_C`, `eval_X`, `eval_neg`, and `eval_monomial`.\n   These are unambiguously confluent and low-fallout.\n4. Promote the homomorphism evaluation lemmas `eval_add`, `eval_sub`,\n   `eval_C_mul`, and `eval_mul` to `@[simp]` **only if** the full `lake build`\n   stays green. If tagging `eval_mul`/`eval_add` as global simp breaks downstream\n   proofs (this is the realistic risk — these change normalization across every\n   importer), do **not** force fixes across many files in this issue: leave those\n   specific lemmas un-tagged, and add a one-line comment at each explaining it is\n   intentionally not `@[simp]` to avoid cross-file normalization churn. Land the\n   safe leaf/constructor simp surface (deliverables 1-3) regardless.\n\nKeep the change a single logical concern (the eval simp surface). Do not touch\n`degree`, `modByMonic`, `Irreducible`, or the multiplication-coefficient\nmachinery beyond what a simp-fallout fix in this file requires. If in-file\nfallout from the new simp lemmas would push the diff past ~200 lines, prefer to\nnarrow the promoted set rather than grow the issue.\n\n## Context\n\n- Phase 6 (proof polishing). `HexPolyFp` is the executable FpPoly layer and is\n  CI-built (it has an oracle entry in `scripts/ci/run_oracles.sh` and builds via\n  the root `lake build`), so unlike the `*Mathlib` bridge libraries there is no\n  un-built sorry-foundation hazard here — a clean `lake build HexPolyFp` is the\n  source of truth.\n- Does not overlap PR #7587 (`HexPolyZ/Basic.lean`, integer polynomials),\n  PR #7586 (`HexGF2`, GF(2) Euclid API), or the open issue #7598\n  (`HexPolyZMathlib` Mathlib-bridge `toPolynomial` projection simp surface).\n  This is the `HexPolyFp/Basic.lean` executable file, distinct from all three.\n- The `coeff_*` simp lemmas already in the file are the precedent for the simp\n  normal form you are extending to `eval`.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexPolyFp` (must be green; this is the parity check for deliverable 4).\n- `lake build` (whole-project, to catch any cross-file simp-normal-form fallout\n  from promoting the homomorphism eval lemmas).\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- `rg -n \"\\bsorry\\b|\\baxiom\\b|\\bnative_decide\\b|TODO|FIXME\" HexPolyFp/Basic.lean`\n  (no new occurrences).\n- `git diff --check` clean.\n\n\n### #7597 review: toMonic Hensel substrate producer soundness\nReview the toMonic Hensel substrate producer cluster for soundness.\n\n## Current state\n\nA chain of recent PRs (#7565, #7589, plus the #7569/#7573 narrowing of\n`exists_subset` to sign-normalized factors) landed a set of producers that\nbuild the toMonic Hensel correspondence / lifted-subset partition /\nslow-path Hensel substrate from the executable `toMonicPrimeData?` selection\nwitness and core facts alone, with no descent-carrier or analytic-recovery\ncaller obligation. The key producers (per the most recent progress notes) are:\n\n- `henselSubsetCorrespondenceHypotheses_of_toMonicPrimeData`\n  (`HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean`) — carrier-free correspondence.\n- `liftedFactorSubsetPartition_of_toMonicPrimeData_complete`\n  (`HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/IntReductionMod.lean`).\n- `slowPathHenselSubstrate_of_toMonicPrimeData`\n  (`HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/IntReductionMod.lean`).\n\nThese are load-bearing for the directive #2564 slow path\n(`factorSlow`), so their soundness gates the headline-theorem combinator.\n\n## Deliverables\n\nA review verdict (comment on the issue + the standard review artifact per\n`agent-worker-flow`) that checks, against the directive #2564 soundness\ndiscipline (\"does the signature admit only canonical witnesses; are there\nvacuous quantifiers or unused hypotheses\"):\n\n- Each producer's hypotheses are all genuinely consumed (no premise that is\n  silently unused, which would signal an over-strong or vacuous statement).\n- The conclusion shapes (`LiftedFactorSubsetPartition core … Finset.univ core`,\n  `SlowPathHenselSubstrate core B primeData`,\n  `HenselSubsetCorrespondenceHypotheses`) do not admit non-canonical witnesses\n  (e.g. a partition over the wrong index set, or a correspondence whose\n  uniqueness clause is vacuous at the `True True` instantiation).\n- The `rfl`/`trivial` discharges noted for the descent-variant\n  `MonicDescentHypotheses` fields (`lift_eq`, `successful_lift`) are genuinely\n  trivial at that instantiation and not hiding a dropped obligation.\n- No `sorry`/`axiom`/`native_decide` was introduced or smuggled in via a\n  downstream `*_of_*` wrapper.\n\nIf a soundness problem is found, comment with concrete evidence\n(counterexample shape or vacuous-hypothesis location) rather than only a\nverdict. If sound, say so explicitly and note the producers are ready for\ncapstone consumption.\n\n## Context\n\nScope to the two files above. Reuse the `hex-lean-mathlib-boundary` skill for\nboundary gotchas. CI does not build the `*Mathlib` libraries, so verify with\nexplicit `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.Basic` and\n`lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.IntReductionMod` against a clean-tree\nbaseline before trusting any failure.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.Basic HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.IntReductionMod`\n  (review is read-mostly; this confirms the baseline is green).\n- Review artifact written; verdict comment posted.\n\n\n### #7594 feat: produce BHKS cut projection from separation and true-factor vectors\nlibrary: HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib\n\n**RE-SCOPED (owner override, see #7590): consumes the PREFIX survivor-span from #7592, NOT a CutSeparation gap certificate.**\n\n## Goal\n\nFinal composition: produce `CutProjectionHypotheses` (or the narrow local wrapper consumed by the forward-count route in #6771) from the prefix-cut pieces. No separation certificate appears in the type.\n\n## Inputs (all prefix-route, no gap certificate)\n\n- #7591 — projected-row trace / LLL span bridge (`lll.shortVectorsUnchecked` → `.toArray` rows span the reduced lattice).\n- #7592 — prefix cut (executable: keep `b_0..b_t`, `t`=last passing GS index) + the BHKS Lemma 5.7 **prefix survivor-span** theorem (`‖v‖²≤radius ⟹ v ∈ span(retained prefix)`).\n- #7593 — true-factor CLD/lattice bridge: `(indicatorVector S | cldSum_S)` lies in the BHKS lattice with norm ≤ `B'`.\n\n## Deliverable\n\nCompose: true-factor vector has norm ≤ B' (#7593) ⟹ by Lemma 5.7 prefix survivor-span (#7592) it lies in the retained-prefix span ⟹ project to first `r` coords ⟹ `indicatorVector S ∈ projectedRowSpanInt` = `CutProjectionHypotheses` / true-indicator membership. Feed `cutProjectionHypotheses_of_retention` (`Lattice.lean`). This is the forward `W ⊆ L'` membership #6771's forward-count route consumes.\n\nDo NOT reintroduce `CutSeparation` / the gap certificate / #7585's unconditional separation framing.\n\ndepends-on: #7592\ndepends-on: #7593\n\n\n### #7593 feat: place true-factor CLD vectors in the BHKS lattice\nCurrent state:\nThe forward cut path needs the full BHKS lattice vector attached to a true lifted-factor support, not just the projected `0/1` indicator. Existing `CutProjectionHypotheses` only receives the projected indicator membership, while the missing proof must first show that `(indicatorVector S | cldSum_S)` is a vector in the BHKS lattice and satisfies the BHKS cut-radius bound.\n\nDecision context:\nThis is part of the #7590 gap-conditioned route. It feeds the cut-separation theorem without changing `bhksProjectedRows` runtime semantics.\n\ndepends-on: #7591\n\nDeliverables:\n- Define the true-factor CLD/lattice vector associated to a support `S` of lifted local factors: first `r` coordinates are `BHKS.indicatorVector S`; trailing CLD coordinates are the corresponding sum of executable CLD columns, including the diagonal correction coordinates already present in the BHKS lattice basis.\n- Prove this vector belongs to the integer row span/lattice of `Hex.bhksLatticeBasis f p k liftedFactors` under the existing true-factor/lift-data hypotheses.\n- Prove its projected first block is exactly `BHKS.indicatorVector S`.\n- Prove or package the required norm bound against `Hex.bhksCutRadiusSq4` using the existing BHKS B2/B3/B4 surfaces; if a lower-level CLD coefficient bound is still missing, make that dependency explicit rather than introducing a `sorry`.\n\nContext:\nRelevant files include `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`, `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Lattice.lean`, `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/BadVector.lean`, and `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean`. Check the prior diagonal lattice-row correction work before choosing the vector shape.\n\nVerification:\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.Lattice HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.BadVector HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.Recovery`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- `git diff --check`\n- Added-line forbidden-token scan.\n\n\n### #7592 feat: add BHKS cut-separation prefix certificate\nlibrary: HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib\n\n**RE-SCOPED (owner override, see #7590): prefix cut, NOT the CutSeparation gap certificate.** The previous body's `CutSeparation` \"all rows at/after t strictly above radius\" is the dead reverse-separation route and is dropped.\n\n## Goal\n\nMake the BHKS Gram-Schmidt cut a **prefix cut** (BHKS Lemma 5.7) so the forward `W ⊆ L'` survivor-span holds with no separation content.\n\n## Executable change (lands first)\n\n`Hex.bhksWithinGramSchmidtCut` / `bhksCutProjectReducedRows` (`HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`) currently retain each row independently iff its GS-norm² ≤ `bhksCutRadiusSq4` (per-row). Change to **prefix**: compute `t := max { i : ‖b*_i‖² ≤ bhksCutRadiusSq4 }` (the last passing GS index) and retain rows `0 … t` (original order, including earlier rows whose GS norm exceeds the radius). No sorting. Conformance + benchmark review (the survivor set only grows — it now keeps prefix rows the per-row cut wrongly dropped; flag any factorization/perf delta).\n\n## Proof (consume the prefix structure, via #7591's trace/LLL span bridge)\n\nProve the BHKS Lemma 5.7 survivor-span theorem for the prefix:\n\n```\nany original-lattice vector v with ‖v‖² ≤ bhksCutRadiusSq4 lies in the integer span of the retained prefix rows b_0 … b_t.\n```\n\nProof is the standard one-line GS contradiction: write `v = Σ λ_i b_i = Σ μ_i b*_i`; if `λ_i ≠ 0` for some `i > t`, the top such gives `μ_i ≠ 0`, so `‖v‖² ≥ ‖b*_i‖² > bhksCutRadiusSq4` (since `i > t` ⟹ `‖b*_i‖² > radius` **by definition of `t` as the max passing index** — no separation theorem needed). Contradiction. Reuse `hex-lll` Gram-Schmidt orthogonality identities ([HexLLL/Basic.lean]).\n\nExpose this in the form consumed by `BHKS.CutRetention` / `cutProjectionHypotheses_of_retention` (`HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Lattice.lean`). Do NOT keep the gap-conditioned / per-row framing.\n\ndepends-on: #7591\n\n## Verification\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.Lattice` ; `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`\n- conformance + bench (executable cut change)\n\n\n### #7591 feat: expose BHKS projected-row trace and LLL span bridge\nCurrent state:\n`Hex.bhksProjectedRows` computes `reducedRows := lll.shortVectorsUnchecked ...`, converts it through `.toArray`/`bhksRowsArrayToMatrix`, then applies the private per-row Gram-Schmidt cut. The proof-facing side only sees the final `BhksProjectedRows.projectedRows`, so workers cannot state or prove the needed lattice-membership/span facts for rows coming out of `lll.shortVectorsUnchecked`.\n\nDecision context:\nThis is part of the #7590 BHKS cut-semantics route: keep the current per-row executable cut, but add a proof-facing separation certificate that makes the retained rows behave as a prefix for the forward correctness path. Do not change runtime cut semantics in this issue.\n\nDeliverables:\n- Add a public, proof-facing trace surface for the data already computed by `Hex.bhksProjectedRows`: reduced rows, reduced matrix, Gram determinant vector, retained row indices, and projected retained rows. The existing `bhksProjectedRows` result should remain definitionally or theorem-level compatible with the trace projection.\n- Prove the `.toArray` bridge for `lll.shortVectorsUnchecked`: rows of the array/matrix returned by the unchecked LLL pipeline are the rows of the steered reduced matrix in the same order.\n- Prove the lattice/span bridge needed downstream: each reduced row lies in the integer row span of the original BHKS basis, and the reduced matrix spans the same lattice as the LLL output statement already provides or can expose.\n- Keep helper names short and namespace-local; avoid adding axioms or new sorries.\n\nContext:\nRelevant definitions are `lll.shortVectorsUnchecked` in `HexLLL/Basic.lean` and `bhksProjectedRows`, `bhksCutProjectReducedRows`, `bhksWithinGramSchmidtCut`, `bhksRowsArrayToMatrix` in `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`. The current private cut helpers may need small public wrappers or theorem-facing mirrors, but the executable output must not change.\n\nVerification:\n- `lake build HexLLL HexBerlekampZassenhaus HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.Lattice`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- `git diff --check`\n- Added-line scan for `sorry`, `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, `FIXME`.\n\n\n### #6868 HO-1 FactorSoundness: expose non-associated headline contract\n## Current state\n\nDirective #2564's headline theorem requires the default public factorization to\nhave no associated duplicate factors, not merely no syntactically duplicate\n`ZPoly` keys. The current public bundle in\n`HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/FactorSoundness.lean`,\n`factor_headline_contract_core`, still exposes:\n\n```lean\nList.Pairwise (fun a b : Hex.ZPoly × Nat => a.1 ≠ b.1)\n  (Hex.factor f).factors.toList\n```\n\n`HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean` already contains the stronger\nbridge:\n\n```lean\nfactor_entries_not_associated\n```\n\nbut it is conditional on the same selected-raw-branch primitive package that\nthe primitive headline issue closes. Once that package is closed, the final\nFactorSoundness wrapper should consume it and expose the SPEC headline\nnon-association clause.\n\nThis issue supports directive #2564 and is intentionally a packaging issue:\ndo not re-prove branch irreducibility or raw primitive facts here.\n\n## Deliverables\n\n1. Add a public `FactorSoundness.lean` theorem bundling the HO-1 headline\n   clauses already available after #6672 and the raw-primitive closure issue:\n   product preservation, primitive plus Mathlib irreducibility per entry,\n   positive multiplicities, pairwise non-association after transport to\n   `Polynomial ℤ`, and signed-content scalar.\n2. Replace the syntactic distinct-key clause in the new wrapper with\n   `List.Pairwise (fun a b => ¬ Associated (HexPolyZMathlib.toPolynomial a.1)\n   (HexPolyZMathlib.toPolynomial b.1))`.\n3. Keep `factor_headline_contract_core` and the primitive intermediate wrapper\n   available; this issue should add the stronger public headline surface rather\n   than breaking existing consumers.\n\n## Context\n\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/FactorSoundness.lean`:\n  `factor_headline_contract_core`, `factor_headline_contract_core_with_primitive`.\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean`:\n  `factor_entries_not_associated`,\n  `zpoly_not_associated_of_ne_of_primitive_pos_leading`.\n- #6672 closes `factor_irreducible_of_nonUnit`, which the headline wrapper\n  needs for the Mathlib irreducibility clause.\n- This issue depends on the raw primitive closure issue created in the same\n  planning cycle.\n\ndepends-on: #6672\ndepends-on: #6867\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.FactorSoundness`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- Added-line forbidden-token grep over touched Lean files finds no new\n  `sorry`, `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, or `FIXME`.\n\n\n### #6867 HO-1 FactorSoundness: close raw primitive package for public factor\n## Current state\n\nDirective #2564 requires the public HO-1 headline theorem to state that every\npolynomial factor emitted by `Hex.factor` is primitive and irreducible.\n`HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/FactorSoundness.lean` already has\n`factor_headline_contract_core_with_primitive`, but it is still conditional on\nan explicit raw-branch hypothesis:\n\n```lean\nh_raw :\n  forall rawFactors,\n    (fast rawFactors or slow-modular rawFactors or slow-trial rawFactors) ->\n      forall raw in rawFactors, Hex.ZPoly.Primitive raw\n```\n\nThe executable layer already exposes the dispatch-source shape through\n`Hex.factorWithBound_eq_factorizationOfFactors`,\n`Hex.factor_entry_primitive_of_chosen_raw_primitive`, and related helpers in\n`HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`. The remaining gap is a closed\nMathlib-side package proving the selected raw factor array is primitive for\neach actual branch of the default public factorization.\n\nThis issue supports directive #2564 and should not try to close the\nirreducibility `sorry` in `factor_irreducible_of_nonUnit`; that is tracked by\n#6672.\n\n## Deliverables\n\n1. In `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/FactorSoundness.lean` or the smallest\n   lower imported bridge file that fits the existing dependency direction,\n   prove a closed theorem for the default dispatch raw-source hypothesis used\n   by `factor_headline_contract_core_with_primitive`.\n2. Use that theorem to add a public primitive headline wrapper with no\n   `h_raw` parameter, packaging product preservation, primitive plus Mathlib\n   irreducibility per entry, positive multiplicities, the current syntactic\n   distinct-key clause, and the signed-content scalar clause.\n3. Preserve existing theorem names and leave `factor_headline_contract_core`\n   available for downstream callers that only need the weaker current bundle.\n\n## Context\n\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/FactorSoundness.lean`:\n  `factor_headline_contract_core_with_primitive`.\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean`:\n  `factor_entries_primitive_of_chosen_raw_primitive`.\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`:\n  `factorWithBound_eq_factorizationOfFactors`,\n  `factorWithBound_entry_primitive_of_chosen_raw_primitive`,\n  `factor_entries_primitive`.\n- Directive #2564 headline clause 2 needs primitive factors; #6672 separately\n  closes irreducibility of every emitted factor.\n\ndepends-on: #6672\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.FactorSoundness`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- Added-line forbidden-token grep over touched Lean files finds no new\n  `sorry`, `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, or `FIXME`.\n\n\n### #6779 feat: B-builder — BadVectorBridge / B8 separation over all of L' minus W (fast-BHKS)\n**DEMOTED TO HO-4 D1 (off the correctness critical path) — see #7461.** This B8 bad-vector separation feeds `projectedRowSpan_eq_trueFactorIndicatorLattice` (the `L' = W` reverse inclusion). That separation is **not** needed for the correctness capstone: fast-core irreducibility now rides the **forward** `W ⊆ L'` count bound (#7461) via #6771, and #6771 no longer depends on this issue.\n\nThe per-bad-vector resultant `p^(a·d) ∣ Res(f, aux)` this issue tries to construct is **mathematically false** — verified by direct computation (`x²+1, p=5, a=3`, bad vector `v=(2,−1) → H = x+46`, `v_p(Res)=0`, all bounds satisfied). The valuation is a *genuine-factor* property (true indicator `v=(1,0) → N=x+57`, `v_p=3`), not a bad-vector one. **Do not re-attempt the resultant-on-`aux` framing** (confirmed dead across #7163→#7232 + two second opinions).\n\nIf/when HO-4 **D1** (fast-path *succeeds* at the cap = performance) is taken up, the correct mechanism is the BHKS Lemma-3.2 **support-modified element** `g ∈ L' \\ W` (add true-factor generators to a retained vector so some local `f_i ∣ Φ(g)` but no global irreducible factor does), with the resultant contradiction taken on the **un-cut** `H := Φ(g) mod p^a` and the cut lattice supplying only the `‖H‖` norm bound (BHKS arXiv:math/0409510 Lemma 3.2 / Thm 4.3). Per SPEC §\"Group D\", D1 is off the correctness critical path; this issue is no longer a capstone blocker.\n\n---\nlibrary: HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib\n\nParent: #6771 (fast-BHKS coverage). Residual bridge-assembly finisher for B8 separation: produce `BadVectorBridgeData` / `ProjectedBadVectorSetupBridge` from the BHKS Lemma 3.2 analytic clauses, then lift to B8 separation over all of L' minus W.\n\n## Current state\n\nThis finisher packages two BHKS Lemma 3.2 analytic clauses carried as hypothesis fields by `IsBhksBadVectorSetup` / `ProjectedBadVectorSetupBridge` (`coprime_input_aux_over_rat`, `resultant_divisible_by_p_pow`).\n\n- **Rational coprimality** — landed. `coprime_input_aux_over_rat_of_forward` / `isBhksBadVectorSetup_of_forward` (`HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/BadVector.lean`) discharge coprimality from a `ForwardCorrespondence` hypothesis (via #6835 -> #6846 -> merged PR #6865).\n- **Modular resultant divisibility** — NOT landed, and the entire \"prove it on the cut auxiliary\" research line is now **dead**. The A-line lineage #6949 -> #7109 -> #7116 -> #7120 -> #7163 -> #7175 -> #7181 -> #7188 -> #7232 all closed by supersession, never landing a producer. The settled finding: `resultant_divisible_by_p_pow` is **false as typed** on the cut / down-scaled CLD short-vector aux. In genuine bad-vector instances `v_p(Res(input, aux_cut)) = 0` (executable sympy verification on #7232; counterexamples `x^2+1, p=5, k=3, g=x-57` and `x^4+1` at several `(p,k)`). The `k*d` exponent is relocated by `diag(p^{l_j})` onto the un-cut `N = cldQuotientMod`, where it already holds in-library via the merged #7075 producer `cld_syzygy_pow_dvd_resultant_of_congr`. The field is jointly contradictory with `coprime_input_aux_over_rat` on the cut aux, so no proof on the cut object exists.\n\nThe surviving path is **not** another resultant-on-cut-aux attempt. #7232's replan disposition established that the only viable direction is the **van Hoeij CLD lattice rollback**, which is exactly the open directive **#2564** (\"HO-1: BZ recombination implements wrong lattice — rollback and rewrite to van Hoeij CLD\"). #2564 restructures the recombination lattice so the cut-aux object — and the false obligation built on it — is retired by construction rather than proved. The hard-won `v_p = 0` evidence has been carried forward to #2564.\n\nThe lift to all of L' minus W is already present: `bad_setup_of_projected_not_indicator` (`BadVector.lean`) is universally quantified over `v`, and the forgetting/consumer chain (`toProjectedBadVectorSetupBridge`, `capSeparationOfBridgeData`, `ExecutableCapSeparationHypotheses.ofProjectedBadVectorSetupBridge`) is already wired.\n\n## Deliverable\n\nAfter #2564 lands the van Hoeij CLD restructure, re-derive this finisher against the restructured lattice (the cut-aux framing below is stale and will be reshaped by #2564). The persistent, route-independent goal is:\n\n- produce `BadVectorBridgeData` / `ProjectedBadVectorSetupBridge` from the resultant-divisibility clause that the #2564 restructure makes provable, plus the landed rational coprimality clause (`coprime_input_aux_over_rat_of_forward`) and the existing positive-degree / `auxiliary_eq` facts;\n- route through the already-present `toProjectedBadVectorSetupBridge` / `bad_setup_of_projected_not_indicator` / `capSeparationOfBridgeData` chain into the B8 separation data consumed by the fast-BHKS assembly.\n\nDo not re-prove the BHKS Lemma 3.2 analytic clauses here. Do not relocate the resultant assumption into a heavier bundle that hides the gap (the cut-aux obligation is false; packaging it as a bare assumption is unsound, not merely incomplete).\n\n## Context\n\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/BadVector.lean`: `IsBhksBadVectorSetup`, `ProjectedBadVectorSetupBridge`, `BadVectorBridgeData`, `isBhksBadVectorSetup_of_projected_not_indicator`, `bad_setup_of_projected_not_indicator`, `toProjectedBadVectorSetupBridge`.\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean`: `capSeparationOfBridgeData` and the `ProjectedBadVectorSetupBridge` specialiser.\n- #2564 owns the van Hoeij CLD lattice restructure that makes the resultant clause provable; this finisher is the bridge assembly on top of it, after the restructure settles the bad-vector object's shape.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- No new `sorry`, `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, or `FIXME` on added lines.\ndepends-on: #2564\n\n\n### #6771 feat: fast-BHKS assembly capstone via forward count\n**ARCHITECTURE CORRECTION.** Fast-core irreducibility uses the forward `W <= L'` count route, not the retired reverse `L' = W` / bad-vector-resultant route. The per-bad-vector resultant path is off the correctness critical path for this capstone.\n\nlibrary: HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib\n\nParent: #6672 (HO-1 capstone). The direct bounded\n`Factorization` wrappers are private even though the SPEC describes\n`factorWithBound` as `(factorFastWithBound f B).getD (factorSlowWithBound f B)`\nand several nearby proofs already reason through this map/getD layer.\n\nThis is a small Mathlib-free API-surface cleanup supporting #2564. Update the existing private helper lemmas that currently mention\n   `factorFastWithBound` so that any lemma useful to downstream\n   `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib` proof assembly is public and documented,\n   without broadening scope beyond the wrapper/map layer.\n\n## Context\n\n- Parent directive: #2564.\n- SPEC: `SPEC/Libraries/hex-berlekamp-zassenhaus.md`, especially\n  \"Top-level API\" and \"Recombination: conditional fast / unconditional slow /\n  fallback combinator\".\n- Phase plan: `PLAN/Phase1.md`, because `HexBerlekampZassenhaus` currently\n  reports ready for Phase 1 and the task is about matching the implemented\n  Lean API to the SPEC surface without adding placeholder definitions.\n- Nearby code:\n  - `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean:6292` private `factorSlowWithBound`\n  - `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean:6595` private `factorFastWithBound`\n  - `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean:6739` public `factorWithBound`\n  - `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean:6826` existing\n    `factorWithBound_eq_factorizationOfFactors`\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- `git diff --check`\n- Forbidden-token diff grep over `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean` finds no\n  added `sorry`, `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, or `FIXME`.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Changing `factorSlowFactorsWithBound`, `factorFastFactorsWithBound`,\n  `factorWithBound`, `factor`, or any recombination algorithm.\n- Proving Group A/B/C/D correctness theorems.\n- Editing `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`.\n- Editing `SPEC/`, top-level `PLAN.md`, or `AGENTS.md`.\n\ndepends-on: #2564\n\n\n### #4818 BZMathlib: prove executable ZPoly isIrreducible_iff\nlibrary: HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib\n\ndepends-on: #6672\n\n## Current state\n\n`Hex.ZPoly.isIrreducible_iff` has been re-homed to the bridge side. The old Mathlib-free umbrella/decomposition is stale: the constant-polynomial prerequisite from #4824 has landed, and the old all-factors capstone #4825 was closed as superseded by the current HO-1 capstone #6672.\n\nThe remaining live blocker for this issue is the bridge-side factor irreducibility capstone:\n\n```lean\ntheorem factor_irreducible_of_nonUnit (f : Hex.ZPoly) :\n    forall entry in (Hex.factor f).factors.toList,\n      Hex.ZPoly.Irreducible entry.1\n```\n\nor the current equivalent theorem in `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/FactorSoundness.lean`.\n\n## Deliverables\n\nAfter #6672 lands, replace the remaining `isIrreducible_iff` proof obligation with a real proof in `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`:\n\n- route the `isIrreducible` executable cases through the bridge-side factor soundness/completeness theorems;\n- preserve the public executable `isIrreducible` API unless a local bridge refactor is required;\n- do not introduce `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, `FIXME`, or new theorem-level `sorry`;\n- do not touch top-level `PLAN.md` or `AGENTS.md`.\n\n## Context\n\nRelevant files:\n\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`, around `class Irreducible`, `isIrreducible`, and the executable factor combinator.\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/FactorSoundness.lean`, especially #6672's `factor_irreducible_of_nonUnit` target.\n- `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean`, around the `isIrreducible` bridge consumer.\n\nThis is the residual final assembly from the old #4818 decomposition. #4824 is complete; #4825 is closed forward to #6672.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- `rg -n \"sorry|axiom|native_decide\" HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/FactorSoundness.lean` shows no new forbidden constructs and the target `sorry` removed.\n\n\n### #4172 HO-1 residual: settle executable irreducibility checker theorem\nlibrary: HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib\n\n## Current state\n\nThe remaining checker capstone is the localized bridge-side `sorry`:\n\n```lean\ntheorem Hex.ZPoly.isIrreducible_iff (f : Hex.ZPoly) :\n    Hex.ZPoly.isIrreducible f = true ↔ Hex.ZPoly.Irreducible f\n```\n\nThe theorem is currently in `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean`, near the public decidability surface. It cannot live in the Mathlib-free core because the forward direction depends on bridge-side factorization correctness and irreducibility arguments.\n\nThe old dependency map was stale:\n\n- #4170 was closed in favor of #6665, and #6665 was decomposed into #6670, #6671, and #6672. The final emitted-factor bridge needed here is now #6672, after its prerequisites land.\n- #4192, #4193, #4194, and #4173 are closed.\n- #4825 remains open for the broader emitted-factor irreducibility surface and should be checked before this capstone is attempted.\n\nThis issue should be worker-actionable only after the emitted-factor irreducibility path is available. In particular, `factor_irreducible_of_nonUnit` must be proved, not left as a local `sorry` consumed by this theorem.\n\n## Dependencies\n\ndepends-on: #4825\ndepends-on: #6672\n\n## Deliverables\n\n1. Keep `HexBerlekampZassenhaus` Mathlib-free; this capstone belongs in `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`.\n4. Do not change executable behavior.\n5. Do not introduce any `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, `FIXME`, or new theorem-level `sorry`.\n\n## Context\n\nThis is a residual decomposition of #2564.
