sessions/1e3abd4f-ffea-4381-944a-3d4ccbaf11bf.stdout:12:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_5","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc 'cat progress/20260618_656d42c5.md'","aggregated_output":"# Progress — 2026-06-18 (session 656d42c5, /work)\n\n## Accomplished\n\nPicked up via `/work`; exercised judgment across the unclaimed feature queue.\n\n### #7783 (executable per-member Berlekamp irreducibility) — diagnosed & skipped\n\nPremise-checked the issue's proof route and found it **impossible as stated**.\n`berlekampFactor` (`Factor.lean:114-118`) fixes the witness set to `f`'s kernel\n`(fixedSpaceKernel f hmonic).toList` and applies it to *every* factor; loop\ntermination gives, per factor `g`, `∀ w ∈ f-kernel, kernelWitnessSplit? g w =\nnone`. But the only completeness producer\n`irreducible_of_no_kernelWitnessSplit_squareFree` (`RabinSoundness.lean:2994`)\nrequires the factor's **own** kernel `(fixedSpaceKernel g _).toList`. No bridge\nbetween `f`'s kernel and a divisor's kernel exists in `HexBerlekamp/`. The\ntheorem is *true* (Berlekamp completeness) but needs a new generalized\ncompleteness theorem (CRT-lift of a g-kernel splitter into f's kernel +\nspan-mod-g linearity), not the thin per-factor composition. Posted evidence-rich\ncomment, `coordination skip 7783` (→ replan).\n\n### #7786 (BHKS fast-core forward-recovery via _of_lift) — deliverable 1 landed\n\n- **Deliverable 1 (landed):** added\n  `factorFastFactorsWithBound_raw_zpolyIrreducible_of_cut`\n  (`PartitionRefinement.lean`), the forward `factorFastFactorsWithBound`-level\n  raw-irreducibility wrapper with **no** `ForwardRecoveryInputs` param, routed\n  through `..._of_cut` (`:530`). Avoids the dead reverse `lattice_eq_indicators`\n  field (route-blocked behind #6779/#2564). Thin re-parameterization of the\n  `_of_forwardInputs` sibling (`:1010`).\n- **Deliverable 2 (blocked):** the producer feeding `_of_lift` needs the\n  per-support `∀ S, TrueFactorLift` family at first-success precision. Only the\n  single-subset constructors `trueFactorLiftOfSubset` /\n  `trueFactorLiftSemanticsOfToMonicSubset` (`LiftBridge.lean:78,104`) exist, and\n  they demand an exact `liftedFactorProduct d T = factor` witness per support —\n  the unproduced recovery/coverage direction. Every `∀ S, TrueFactorLift` is a\n  hypothesis, never a conclusion. Diagnosed on the issue with the missing-lemma\n  shape; partial PR.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.PartitionRefinement` → built the full\n  CI-gated library (3777 jobs), `Build completed successfully`, 0 `error:`.\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py` → exit 0.\n- `git diff --check` clean; no `sorry`/`axiom`/`native_decide`/`TODO`/`FIXME` on\n  added lines.\n\n## Current frontier\n\nDeliverable 1's `_of_cut` wrapper is the furthest-forward fast-route piece; it\ncomposes the instant a deliverable-2 coverage producer exists.\n\n## Next step\n\nReplan deliverable 2 as its own issue gated on a coverage substrate: a producer\nconcluding `∀ S : trueSupports, BHKS.TrueFactorLift L S` (+ semantics +\nseparation) from the first-success recovery (the per-support exact\n`liftedFactorProduct d T = factor`). An *unconditional* form (for #6672) also\nneeds the scheduled-loop determinism `hcore` (BHKS precision-soundness, not in\ntree). For #7783: replan as a generalized completeness theorem\n`irreducible_of_no_kernelWitnessSplit_squareFree_of_dvd` + thin loop assembly.\n\n## Blockers\n\n- #7783: missing generalized completeness theorem (witness-set bridge).\n- #7786 deliverable 2: missing per-support coverage producer; unconditional form\n  additionally blocked on scheduled-loop determinism.\n\nNote: worktree carries pre-existing unrelated `.claude/*` modifications from\nbefore this session; not touched or staged.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/1e3abd4f-ffea-4381-944a-3d4ccbaf11bf.stdout:16:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_7","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc 'cat progress/20260617_aad44673.md'","aggregated_output":"# Progress - 2026-06-17 (session aad44673, one-shot #7592)\n\n## Accomplished\n\nClaimed #7592 (feat: add BHKS cut-separation prefix certificate, critical-path)\nvia `pod once`. Split into its two staged deliverables: the executable prefix\ncut (\"lands first\") and the `CutRetention` proof. **Landed the executable\nprefix cut; rejected the `CutRetention` proof half as an unsound target** (same\ndefect #7575 already diagnosed, invariant under the prefix change), with a\nmachine-checked comment on the issue.\n\n### Executable prefix cut (landed)\n\n`HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`: added `bhksCutPrefixCount` (computes\n`t = (max passing GS index) + 1` by an increasing-order fold) and rewired\n`bhksCutProjectReducedRows` / `bhksRetainedRowIndices` to retain the contiguous\nprefix `b_0 … b_t` in original order, including earlier rows whose own GS norm\nexceeds the radius. This is the genuine BHKS Lemma 5.7 prefix and fixes the\nnon-prefix defect diagnosed on #7578 (per-row survivor set was not a GS-norm\nprefix under LLL).\n\nVerification:\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus` — clean.\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.Lattice` — clean (proof layer intact;\n  nothing constructs `CutRetention`, so the runtime change breaks no proof).\n- Regenerated `conformance-fixtures/HexBerlekampZassenhaus/bz.jsonl` via\n  `hexbz_emit_fixtures` — **byte-identical** to the committed flint-verified\n  ground truth. Factorization output is unchanged (survivor set only grows;\n  final factor multisets match flint).\n- Added-line forbidden-token scan: no sorry/axiom/native_decide/TODO/FIXME.\n\n### `CutRetention` proof half (rejected as unsound — premise defect)\n\n`CutRetention.mem_projected_of_norm_le` (`Lattice.lean:424`) quantifies over\n**arbitrary** `v : Fin r → ℤ` with only a norm bound and concludes membership in\nthe **projected** span `projectedRowSpanInt`. This is unsatisfiable — the same\ndefect #7575 posted (kernel-checked `IsEmpty (CutRetention P)`) — and the prefix\nchange does **not** repair it. Machine-checked under the new prefix code:\n`factorCount=1, coeffWidth=0, reduced=[[2]]` gives `cutRadiusSq4=4`,\n`bhksCutPrefixCount=0`, `bhksCutProjectReducedRows=#[]`, so\n`projectedRowSpanInt=⊥`, yet `v=![1]` has `‖v‖²=1 ≤ 4`. Membership in a\nsublattice never follows from a norm bound alone.\n\nThe issue's *Proof section* states the SOUND fact correctly (\"any\n**original-lattice** vector v with `‖v‖²≤radius` lies in the integer span of the\nretained prefix rows\"). The defect is only the packaging: the sound statement is\nabout lattice vectors of the `(r+n)`-dim reduced basis, not arbitrary `ℤ^r`\nvectors, and not the projected span. `CutRetention` drops the lattice hypothesis\nand projects — that is where soundness is lost.\n\n## Current frontier\n\nExecutable prefix cut is the substrate any sound survivor-span proof needs.\nLanded via `create-pr --partial` (issue → `replan`). Comment on #7592 gives the\ncorrected target.\n\n## Next step (for the replan)\n\nProve the full-lattice survivor-span lemma directly into `CutProjectionHypotheses`\n(NOT `CutRetention`, which should be retired along with\n`cutProjectionHypotheses_of_retention` / `..._of_retention`):\n- For `v` with `memLattice reducedMatrix v` and `‖v‖² ≤ R`, `v ∈` ℤ-span of the\n  prefix rows `b_0 … b_t`, via `basis_normSq` (gramDetVec ↔ `‖b*_i‖²`,\n  `HexGramSchmidtMathlib/Int.lean:1070`) and the integer-top-coefficient /\n  orthogonality lemmas (`normSq_latticeVec_ge_min_basis_normSq`,\n  `rowCombination_normSq_ge_of_orthogonal_coeff_sq_ge_one`,\n  `HexGramSchmidt/Int.lean`).\n- Then the factorization→lattice bridge #7578 listed as absent: true-support\n  indicators are projections of genuine short lattice vectors (CLD-vanishing for\n  true factors), so `CutProjectionHypotheses` holds at exactly the indicators it\n  is consumed at (`PartitionRefinement.lean:484`).\n\n## Blockers\n\nNone for the landed change. The proof half is blocked on a corrected,\nnon-`CutRetention` target (premise defect), routed to replan.\nNote: the worktree carries pre-existing unrelated `.claude/*` modifications from\nbefore this session; not touched or staged.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/1e3abd4f-ffea-4381-944a-3d4ccbaf11bf.stdout:27:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_14","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc 'gh issue view 7854 --comments --json number,title,state,labels,body,comments,url'","aggregated_output":"{\"body\":\"## Current state\\n\\nThis is the residual scope from #7846 after partial PR #7852. PR #7852 lands deliverable 1: it exposes the successful BHKS fast-core recovery precision, the `bhksIndicatorCandidates? ... = some coreFactors` equality, and per-emitted-candidate `RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift` witnesses.\\n\\nThe remaining work is not to re-expose those witnesses. It is to turn the per-candidate witness package into the exact `BHKS.TrueFactorLift` / `BHKS.TrueFactorLiftSemantics` family consumed by the existing fast-BHKS cut-projection producer.\\n\\n## Deliverables\\n\\n1. In `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`, derive the `RecoveredLift` / `TrueFactorLift` shape consumed by the existing constructors from the `RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift core d factor T` witnesses exposed by #7852.\\n2. Bridge the normalization mismatch noted by #7852: the recovered witness is over non-monic `core` and uses `primitivePart (dilate (leadingCoeff core) monicFactor) = factor`, while `recoveredLiftOfSubset` / `trueFactorLiftSemanticsOfToMonicSubset` are consumed in the `(toMonic core).monic` regime where the leading coefficient collapses to 1. Reuse the existing `centeredLiftPoly_*_eq_factor_of_recovery*` lemmas in `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean` rather than adding assumptions.\\n3. Reindex the per-candidate witnesses through `supportPartitionByMinColumn` / `liftedFactorSubsetsOfSupports` into the `forall S : trueSupports, ...` family shape expected by `BHKS.cutProjectionHypotheses_of_trueFactors`.\\n4. Add the thin composition into `factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_lift` / `factorFastFactorsWithBound_raw_zpolyIrreducible_of_cut` if the produced family has the required binder shape. Keep the final unconditional `factorFastRaw_irreducible_of_some` capstone out of scope.\\n\\nDo not add assumptions that simply restate `forall S, BHKS.TrueFactorLift ...`, `TrueFactorLiftSemantics`, or the desired cut projection hypothesis.\\n\\n## Context\\n\\n- Residual from: #7846.\\n- Partial PR with extractor work: #7852.\\n- Parent directive: #2564.\\n- Existing constructors/consumers:\\n  - `BHKS.trueFactorLiftOfSubset`\\n  - `BHKS.recoveredLiftOfSubset`\\n  - `BHKS.trueFactorLiftSemanticsOfToMonicSubset`\\n  - `BHKS.cutProjectionHypotheses_of_trueFactors`\\n  - `factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_lift`\\n  - `factorFastFactorsWithBound_raw_zpolyIrreducible_of_cut`\\n\\n## Verification\\n\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.Recovery`\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.PartitionRefinement`\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus`\\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\\n- `git diff --check`\\n- Added-line forbidden-token scan over touched Lean files: no new `sorry`, `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, or `FIXME`.\\n\",\"comments\":[{\"id\":\"IC_kwDOSKC1W88AAAABGlJwHg\",\"author\":{\"login\":\"kim-em\"},\"authorAssociation\":\"OWNER\",\"body\":\"Session: `a82b2d5b-7e5b-4e08-8606-2ed7d017e605` Branch: `agent/a82b2d5b`\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-06-18T00:01:13Z\",\"includesCreatedEdit\":false,\"isMinimized\":false,\"minimizedReason\":\"\",\"reactionGroups\":[],\"url\":\"https://github.com/kim-em/hex/issues/7854#issuecomment-4736577566\",\"viewerDidAuthor\":true},{\"id\":\"IC_kwDOSKC1W88AAAABGlLfxw\",\"author\":{\"login\":\"kim-em\"},\"authorAssociation\":\"OWNER\",\"body\":\"Claimed by session `a7ff6db1-66f5-4ccc-a1ff-2c2e31afa11d` on branch `agent/a7ff6db1`\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-06-18T00:05:24Z\",\"includesCreatedEdit\":false,\"isMinimized\":false,\"minimizedReason\":\"\",\"reactionGroups\":[],\"url\":\"https://github.com/kim-em/hex/issues/7854#issuecomment-4736606151\",\"viewerDidAuthor\":true},{\"id\":\"IC_kwDOSKC1W88AAAABGlOxFw\",\"author\":{\"login\":\"kim-em\"},\"authorAssociation\":\"OWNER\",\"body\":\"Stopping on this residual after reading the cited infrastructure: two concrete blockers, one a dependency-ordering problem and one a genuine structural gap. Posting evidence and leaving it claimable for a re-scoped version (per the \\\"directives are hypotheses\\\" rule).\\n\\n## Blocker 1 — deliverables 3 & 4 depend on unmerged #7852\\n\\nThe issue body keys deliverables 3/4 on \\\"callers that arrive via the executable `Hex.factorFastCoreWithBound_some_indicatorCandidates` extractor\\\" and the Mathlib `representsIntegerFactorAtLift_of_indicatorCandidates`. Both of those symbols live **only in PR #7852**, which is still open (CI in progress) and not merged. They are absent from current `main` (`e9fa34ff`):\\n\\n```\\n$ grep -rn \\\"factorFastCoreWithBound_some_indicatorCandidates\\\\\\n\\\\|representsIntegerFactorAtLift_of_indicatorCandidates\\\" \\\\\\n    HexBerlekampZassenhaus/ HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/\\n(no matches)\\n```\\n\\nSo deliverables 3/4 cannot be built on a main-based branch until #7852 lands. This residual should carry `depends-on: #7852` (or be sequenced after it).\\n\\n## Blocker 2 — the capstone needs `TrueFactorLift` (raw product); recovery only gives the centered `RecoveredLift` shape\\n\\nDeliverable 1 asks to \\\"derive the `RecoveredLift` / `TrueFactorLift` shape consumed by the existing constructors from the `RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift core d factor T` witnesses.\\\" The two shapes are not symmetric, and only the `RecoveredLift` half is derivable:\\n\\n- `RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift` = `Nonempty (RecoveredAtLift core d factor S)` (`Basic.lean:2382`). The carrier provides **only a mod-`p^k` congruence** plus a centered/dilated equality (`Basic.lean:2360-2372`):\\n  - `congr : reduceModPow (liftedFactorProduct d S) p k = reduceModPow monicFactor p k`\\n  - `dilate_eq : primitivePart (dilate (lc core) monicFactor) = factor`\\n  This already discharges the **centered** recovery `centeredLiftPoly (liftedFactorProduct d S) (p^k) = monicFactor` (`Basic.lean:3092`, inside `liftedRecoveryCandidate_eq_factor_of_congruence_of_bound`; packaged as `RecoveredAtLift.candidate_eq_of_monic_dvd`, `Basic.lean:3128`). That is exactly the `RecoveredLift.recovered_eq` field shape (`Lattice.lean:255-258`).\\n\\n- The capstone the issue targets — `factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_lift` (`PartitionRefinement.lean:601`) — takes `lift : ∀ S : trueSupports, BHKS.TrueFactorLift L S.1`. `TrueFactorLift.support_product_eq` requires the **raw** integer product `supportProduct L S = factor` (`Lattice.lean:231`), where `supportProduct` is `Array.polyProduct` with **no mod reduction** (`Lattice.lean:151-157`), plus `factor_mul : factor * cofactor = f` over ℤ.\\n\\nThese do not coincide. For a true support of size ≥ 2 over mod-`p^k` Hensel lifts, the raw product `liftedFactorProduct d S` has coefficients exceeding `p^k/2`, so it is **not** equal to its centered representative `monicFactor`; hence `supportProduct L S = factor` is false and `factor_mul` (raw product divides `f` over ℤ) also fails. The project documents this explicitly in the `RecoveredLift` docstring (`Lattice.lean:233-243`):\\n\\n> This is parallel to `TrueFactorLift`, but it deliberately does not assert the raw integer equality `supportProduct L S = factor`. The recovery algorithm only exposes the centered representative modulo `p^a` … Downstream lemmas … should consume this package instead of strengthening their hypotheses back to raw selected-product equality.\\n\\nSo `RecoveredLift` was introduced precisely because recovery cannot yield `TrueFactorLift`. No in-tree lemma concludes the raw exact product from recovery (`grep` finds `supportProduct L S = factor` / `liftedFactorProduct d S = factor` only as the structure field, the docstring, and hypothesis positions — never a producer). This is the same centered/raw (a.k.a. scaled/unscaled, #7479-class) exact-product gap the project already tracks as an open structural problem, and it is what blocks the whole fast-/slow-BHKS toMonic capstone.\\n\\nConsequence: deliverable 4 (\\\"thin composition into `factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_lift`\\\") is not thin — its `TrueFactorLift` family cannot be produced from the `RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift` witnesses this issue is scoped around. Deriving it requires the exact-product (monic-transform / unscaled-support) producer, which is a separate structural remodel, not a packaging step over the recovery witnesses.\\n\\n## Suggested re-scope\\n\\n1. Sequence after #7852 (`depends-on: #7852`) so the extractor symbols exist.\\n2. Narrow this issue to the **`RecoveredLift` half** of deliverable 2: a producer `RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift core d factor S → RecoveredLift …` (feeding `recoveredLiftOfSubset`) in the monic `(toMonic core).monic` regime. That is landable on `main` today and largely reuses `RecoveredAtLift.candidate_eq_of_monic_dvd` / the `centeredLiftPoly_*_of_recovery*` lemmas, as the body intends.\\n3. Move the `TrueFactorLift` exact-product family + capstone wiring (deliverables 3/4 toward `factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_lift`) under the existing exact-product / unscaled-support migration (#7479-class), since `TrueFactorLift` is unsatisfiable from recovery data over the fast-core Hensel lifts.\\n\\nNo code changes made; leaving claimable.\\n\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-06-18T00:13:57Z\",\"includesCreatedEdit\":false,\"isMinimized\":false,\"minimizedReason\":\"\",\"reactionGroups\":[],\"url\":\"https://github.com/kim-em/hex/issues/7854#issuecomment-4736659735\",\"viewerDidAuthor\":true},{\"id\":\"IC_kwDOSKC1W88AAAABGlO0aw\",\"author\":{\"login\":\"kim-em\"},\"authorAssociation\":\"OWNER\",\"body\":\"Skipped by session `a7ff6db1-66f5-4ccc-a1ff-2c2e31afa11d` (needs replan): Blocked: (1) deliverables 3/4 depend on unmerged #7852 extractor symbols (absent from main); (2) capstone needs TrueFactorLift raw-product family, but RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift only yields the centered RecoveredLift shape (Lattice.lean:233-243 documents recovery cannot give raw supportProduct=factor). #7479-class exact-product gap. See comment.\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-06-18T00:14:08Z\",\"includesCreatedEdit\":false,\"isMinimized\":false,\"minimizedReason\":\"\",\"reactionGroups\":[],\"url\":\"https://github.com/kim-em/hex/issues/7854#issuecomment-4736660587\",\"viewerDidAuthor\":true}],\"labels\":[{\"id\":\"LA_kwDOSKC1W88AAAACgZ_KIw\",\"name\":\"agent-plan\",\"description\":\"pod coordination\",\"color\":\"1D76DB\"},{\"id\":\"LA_kwDOSKC1W88AAAACgZ_Kng\",\"name\":\"replan\",\"description\":\"pod coordination\",\"color\":\"D93F0B\"},{\"id\":\"LA_kwDOSKC1W88AAAACgZ_-mA\",\"name\":\"feature\",\"description\":\"Planner-dispatched implementation work\",\"color\":\"0E8A16\"}],\"number\":7854,\"state\":\"OPEN\",\"title\":\"feat: derive TrueFactorLift family from fast-core recovery witnesses\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/kim-em/hex/issues/7854\"}\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/1e3abd4f-ffea-4381-944a-3d4ccbaf11bf.stdout:35:{"type":"item.started","item":{"id":"item_19","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc 'gh issue edit 7854 --title \"feat: derive RecoveredLift from fast-core recovery witnesses\" --body \"$(cat <<'\"'EOF'\n## Current state\n\nThis is the narrowed residual from #7846 after partial PR #7852 and the worker skip on the previous #7854 body.\n\nPR #7852 is still open and is expected to land the extractor side: successful BHKS fast-core recovery precision, the \"'`bhksIndicatorCandidates? ... = some coreFactors` equality, and per-emitted-candidate `RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift core d factor T` witnesses. This issue is sequenced after that extractor work.\n\ndepends-on: #7852\n\nThe previous body asked for a `TrueFactorLift` family and thin capstone wiring into `factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_lift`. That target is not derivable from recovery witnesses alone. `RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift` packages centered modular recovery data (`RecoveredAtLift`), while `TrueFactorLift.support_product_eq` requires raw integer selected-product equality. The project already separates these shapes in the `RecoveredLift` docstring in `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Lattice.lean`; recovery data should not be strengthened back into raw `supportProduct L S = factor` without the separate exact-product / unscaled-support remodel.\n\n## Deliverables\n\n1. In `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`, add the centered producer that turns a `RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift core d factor S` witness from the #7852 fast-core extractor path into the `BHKS.RecoveredLift` shape consumed by the existing recovery constructors.\n2. Bridge the normalization mismatch noted by #7852: the recovered witness is over non-monic `core` and uses `primitivePart (dilate (leadingCoeff core) monicFactor) = factor`, while the existing recovery helpers are consumed in the `(toMonic core).monic` regime where the leading coefficient collapses to 1. Reuse the existing `centeredLiftPoly_*_eq_factor_of_recovery*` and `RecoveredAtLift.candidate_eq_of_monic_dvd` infrastructure in `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean`; do not add assumptions for the desired equality.\n3. If #7852 exposes the per-candidate witnesses indexed through `supportPartitionByMinColumn` / `liftedFactorSubsetsOfSupports`, reindex only as far as a `RecoveredLift` family. Do not attempt to produce `TrueFactorLift`, `TrueFactorLiftSemantics`, or `CutProjectionHypotheses` here.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Producing `forall S, BHKS.TrueFactorLift ...` from recovery witnesses.\n- Wiring `factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_lift` or `factorFastFactorsWithBound_raw_zpolyIrreducible_of_cut` through a recovered family.\n- The final unconditional `factorFastRaw_irreducible_of_some` capstone.\n- Any new assumptions that simply restate `TrueFactorLift`, `TrueFactorLiftSemantics`, raw selected-product equality, or the desired cut projection hypothesis.\n\nThose require the separate exact-product / unscaled-support migration, not this centered recovery packaging issue.\n\n## Context\n\n- Residual from: #7846.\n- Extractor PR: #7852.\n- Parent directive: #2564.\n- Relevant declarations:\n  - `RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift`\n  - `RecoveredAtLift.candidate_eq_of_monic_dvd`\n  - `BHKS.RecoveredLift`\n  - `BHKS.recoveredLiftOfSubset`\n  - `centeredLiftPoly_*_eq_factor_of_recovery*`\n  - `supportPartitionByMinColumn`\n  - `liftedFactorSubsetsOfSupports`\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.Recovery`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.PartitionRefinement`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus`\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\n- `git diff --check`\n- Added-line forbidden-token scan over touched Lean files: no new `sorry`, `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, or `FIXME`.\nEOF\n)\"'","aggregated_output":"","exit_code":null,"status":"in_progress"}}

sessions/1e3abd4f-ffea-4381-944a-3d4ccbaf11bf.stdout:45:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_24","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc 'gh issue view 7854 --comments --json comments,labels,title,body'","aggregated_output":"{\"body\":\"## Current state\\n\\nThis is the narrowed residual from #7846 after partial PR #7852 and the worker skip on the previous #7854 body.\\n\\nPR #7852 is still open and is expected to land the extractor side: successful BHKS fast-core recovery precision, the `bhksIndicatorCandidates? ... = some coreFactors` equality, and per-emitted-candidate `RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift core d factor T` witnesses. This issue is sequenced after that extractor work.\\n\\ndepends-on: #7852\\n\\nThe previous body asked for a `TrueFactorLift` family and thin capstone wiring into `factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_lift`. That target is not derivable from recovery witnesses alone. `RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift` packages centered modular recovery data (`RecoveredAtLift`), while `TrueFactorLift.support_product_eq` requires raw integer selected-product equality. The project already separates these shapes in the `RecoveredLift` docstring in `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Lattice.lean`; recovery data should not be strengthened back into raw `supportProduct L S = factor` without the separate exact-product / unscaled-support remodel.\\n\\n## Deliverables\\n\\n1. In `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`, add the centered producer that turns a `RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift core d factor S` witness from the #7852 fast-core extractor path into the `BHKS.RecoveredLift` shape consumed by the existing recovery constructors.\\n2. Bridge the normalization mismatch noted by #7852: the recovered witness is over non-monic `core` and uses `primitivePart (dilate (leadingCoeff core) monicFactor) = factor`, while the existing recovery helpers are consumed in the `(toMonic core).monic` regime where the leading coefficient collapses to 1. Reuse the existing `centeredLiftPoly_*_eq_factor_of_recovery*` and `RecoveredAtLift.candidate_eq_of_monic_dvd` infrastructure in `HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Basic.lean`; do not add assumptions for the desired equality.\\n3. If #7852 exposes the per-candidate witnesses indexed through `supportPartitionByMinColumn` / `liftedFactorSubsetsOfSupports`, reindex only as far as a `RecoveredLift` family. Do not attempt to produce `TrueFactorLift`, `TrueFactorLiftSemantics`, or `CutProjectionHypotheses` here.\\n\\n## Out of scope\\n\\n- Producing `forall S, BHKS.TrueFactorLift ...` from recovery witnesses.\\n- Wiring `factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_lift` or `factorFastFactorsWithBound_raw_zpolyIrreducible_of_cut` through a recovered family.\\n- The final unconditional `factorFastRaw_irreducible_of_some` capstone.\\n- Any new assumptions that simply restate `TrueFactorLift`, `TrueFactorLiftSemantics`, raw selected-product equality, or the desired cut projection hypothesis.\\n\\nThose require the separate exact-product / unscaled-support migration, not this centered recovery packaging issue.\\n\\n## Context\\n\\n- Residual from: #7846.\\n- Extractor PR: #7852.\\n- Parent directive: #2564.\\n- Relevant declarations:\\n  - `RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift`\\n  - `RecoveredAtLift.candidate_eq_of_monic_dvd`\\n  - `BHKS.RecoveredLift`\\n  - `BHKS.recoveredLiftOfSubset`\\n  - `centeredLiftPoly_*_eq_factor_of_recovery*`\\n  - `supportPartitionByMinColumn`\\n  - `liftedFactorSubsetsOfSupports`\\n\\n## Verification\\n\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.Recovery`\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.PartitionRefinement`\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib`\\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus`\\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py`\\n- `git diff --check`\\n- Added-line forbidden-token scan over touched Lean files: no new `sorry`, `axiom`, `native_decide`, `TODO`, or `FIXME`.\",\"comments\":[{\"id\":\"IC_kwDOSKC1W88AAAABGlJwHg\",\"author\":{\"login\":\"kim-em\"},\"authorAssociation\":\"OWNER\",\"body\":\"Session: `a82b2d5b-7e5b-4e08-8606-2ed7d017e605` Branch: `agent/a82b2d5b`\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-06-18T00:01:13Z\",\"includesCreatedEdit\":false,\"isMinimized\":false,\"minimizedReason\":\"\",\"reactionGroups\":[],\"url\":\"https://github.com/kim-em/hex/issues/7854#issuecomment-4736577566\",\"viewerDidAuthor\":true},{\"id\":\"IC_kwDOSKC1W88AAAABGlLfxw\",\"author\":{\"login\":\"kim-em\"},\"authorAssociation\":\"OWNER\",\"body\":\"Claimed by session `a7ff6db1-66f5-4ccc-a1ff-2c2e31afa11d` on branch `agent/a7ff6db1`\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-06-18T00:05:24Z\",\"includesCreatedEdit\":false,\"isMinimized\":false,\"minimizedReason\":\"\",\"reactionGroups\":[],\"url\":\"https://github.com/kim-em/hex/issues/7854#issuecomment-4736606151\",\"viewerDidAuthor\":true},{\"id\":\"IC_kwDOSKC1W88AAAABGlOxFw\",\"author\":{\"login\":\"kim-em\"},\"authorAssociation\":\"OWNER\",\"body\":\"Stopping on this residual after reading the cited infrastructure: two concrete blockers, one a dependency-ordering problem and one a genuine structural gap. Posting evidence and leaving it claimable for a re-scoped version (per the \\\"directives are hypotheses\\\" rule).\\n\\n## Blocker 1 — deliverables 3 & 4 depend on unmerged #7852\\n\\nThe issue body keys deliverables 3/4 on \\\"callers that arrive via the executable `Hex.factorFastCoreWithBound_some_indicatorCandidates` extractor\\\" and the Mathlib `representsIntegerFactorAtLift_of_indicatorCandidates`. Both of those symbols live **only in PR #7852**, which is still open (CI in progress) and not merged. They are absent from current `main` (`e9fa34ff`):\\n\\n```\\n$ grep -rn \\\"factorFastCoreWithBound_some_indicatorCandidates\\\\\\n\\\\|representsIntegerFactorAtLift_of_indicatorCandidates\\\" \\\\\\n    HexBerlekampZassenhaus/ HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/\\n(no matches)\\n```\\n\\nSo deliverables 3/4 cannot be built on a main-based branch until #7852 lands. This residual should carry `depends-on: #7852` (or be sequenced after it).\\n\\n## Blocker 2 — the capstone needs `TrueFactorLift` (raw product); recovery only gives the centered `RecoveredLift` shape\\n\\nDeliverable 1 asks to \\\"derive the `RecoveredLift` / `TrueFactorLift` shape consumed by the existing constructors from the `RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift core d factor T` witnesses.\\\" The two shapes are not symmetric, and only the `RecoveredLift` half is derivable:\\n\\n- `RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift` = `Nonempty (RecoveredAtLift core d factor S)` (`Basic.lean:2382`). The carrier provides **only a mod-`p^k` congruence** plus a centered/dilated equality (`Basic.lean:2360-2372`):\\n  - `congr : reduceModPow (liftedFactorProduct d S) p k = reduceModPow monicFactor p k`\\n  - `dilate_eq : primitivePart (dilate (lc core) monicFactor) = factor`\\n  This already discharges the **centered** recovery `centeredLiftPoly (liftedFactorProduct d S) (p^k) = monicFactor` (`Basic.lean:3092`, inside `liftedRecoveryCandidate_eq_factor_of_congruence_of_bound`; packaged as `RecoveredAtLift.candidate_eq_of_monic_dvd`, `Basic.lean:3128`). That is exactly the `RecoveredLift.recovered_eq` field shape (`Lattice.lean:255-258`).\\n\\n- The capstone the issue targets — `factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_lift` (`PartitionRefinement.lean:601`) — takes `lift : ∀ S : trueSupports, BHKS.TrueFactorLift L S.1`. `TrueFactorLift.support_product_eq` requires the **raw** integer product `supportProduct L S = factor` (`Lattice.lean:231`), where `supportProduct` is `Array.polyProduct` with **no mod reduction** (`Lattice.lean:151-157`), plus `factor_mul : factor * cofactor = f` over ℤ.\\n\\nThese do not coincide. For a true support of size ≥ 2 over mod-`p^k` Hensel lifts, the raw product `liftedFactorProduct d S` has coefficients exceeding `p^k/2`, so it is **not** equal to its centered representative `monicFactor`; hence `supportProduct L S = factor` is false and `factor_mul` (raw product divides `f` over ℤ) also fails. The project documents this explicitly in the `RecoveredLift` docstring (`Lattice.lean:233-243`):\\n\\n> This is parallel to `TrueFactorLift`, but it deliberately does not assert the raw integer equality `supportProduct L S = factor`. The recovery algorithm only exposes the centered representative modulo `p^a` … Downstream lemmas … should consume this package instead of strengthening their hypotheses back to raw selected-product equality.\\n\\nSo `RecoveredLift` was introduced precisely because recovery cannot yield `TrueFactorLift`. No in-tree lemma concludes the raw exact product from recovery (`grep` finds `supportProduct L S = factor` / `liftedFactorProduct d S = factor` only as the structure field, the docstring, and hypothesis positions — never a producer). This is the same centered/raw (a.k.a. scaled/unscaled, #7479-class) exact-product gap the project already tracks as an open structural problem, and it is what blocks the whole fast-/slow-BHKS toMonic capstone.\\n\\nConsequence: deliverable 4 (\\\"thin composition into `factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_lift`\\\") is not thin — its `TrueFactorLift` family cannot be produced from the `RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift` witnesses this issue is scoped around. Deriving it requires the exact-product (monic-transform / unscaled-support) producer, which is a separate structural remodel, not a packaging step over the recovery witnesses.\\n\\n## Suggested re-scope\\n\\n1. Sequence after #7852 (`depends-on: #7852`) so the extractor symbols exist.\\n2. Narrow this issue to the **`RecoveredLift` half** of deliverable 2: a producer `RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift core d factor S → RecoveredLift …` (feeding `recoveredLiftOfSubset`) in the monic `(toMonic core).monic` regime. That is landable on `main` today and largely reuses `RecoveredAtLift.candidate_eq_of_monic_dvd` / the `centeredLiftPoly_*_of_recovery*` lemmas, as the body intends.\\n3. Move the `TrueFactorLift` exact-product family + capstone wiring (deliverables 3/4 toward `factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_lift`) under the existing exact-product / unscaled-support migration (#7479-class), since `TrueFactorLift` is unsatisfiable from recovery data over the fast-core Hensel lifts.\\n\\nNo code changes made; leaving claimable.\\n\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-06-18T00:13:57Z\",\"includesCreatedEdit\":false,\"isMinimized\":false,\"minimizedReason\":\"\",\"reactionGroups\":[],\"url\":\"https://github.com/kim-em/hex/issues/7854#issuecomment-4736659735\",\"viewerDidAuthor\":true},{\"id\":\"IC_kwDOSKC1W88AAAABGlO0aw\",\"author\":{\"login\":\"kim-em\"},\"authorAssociation\":\"OWNER\",\"body\":\"Skipped by session `a7ff6db1-66f5-4ccc-a1ff-2c2e31afa11d` (needs replan): Blocked: (1) deliverables 3/4 depend on unmerged #7852 extractor symbols (absent from main); (2) capstone needs TrueFactorLift raw-product family, but RepresentsIntegerFactorAtLift only yields the centered RecoveredLift shape (Lattice.lean:233-243 documents recovery cannot give raw supportProduct=factor). #7479-class exact-product gap. See comment.\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-06-18T00:14:08Z\",\"includesCreatedEdit\":false,\"isMinimized\":false,\"minimizedReason\":\"\",\"reactionGroups\":[],\"url\":\"https://github.com/kim-em/hex/issues/7854#issuecomment-4736660587\",\"viewerDidAuthor\":true},{\"id\":\"IC_kwDOSKC1W88AAAABGlPfcQ\",\"author\":{\"login\":\"kim-em\"},\"authorAssociation\":\"OWNER\",\"body\":\"Replan triage: narrowed this issue to the centered  producer that is actually derivable from  recovery data, recorded the open #7852 dependency, and moved the raw /capstone scope out of this issue because it requires the separate exact-product / unscaled-support migration.\",\"createdAt\":\"2026-06-18T00:16:24Z\",\"includesCreatedEdit\":false,\"isMinimized\":false,\"minimizedReason\":\"\",\"reactionGroups\":[],\"url\":\"https://github.com/kim-em/hex/issues/7854#issuecomment-4736671601\",\"viewerDidAuthor\":true}],\"labels\":[{\"id\":\"LA_kwDOSKC1W88AAAACgZ_KIw\",\"name\":\"agent-plan\",\"description\":\"pod coordination\",\"color\":\"1D76DB\"},{\"id\":\"LA_kwDOSKC1W88AAAACgZ_-mA\",\"name\":\"feature\",\"description\":\"Planner-dispatched implementation work\",\"color\":\"0E8A16\"}],\"title\":\"feat: derive RecoveredLift from fast-core recovery witnesses\"}\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/b3648b20-dbeb-4ec6-9d5f-7596450f9b1f.stdout:15:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_6","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' progress/20260617_aad44673.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Progress - 2026-06-17 (session aad44673, one-shot #7592)\n\n## Accomplished\n\nClaimed #7592 (feat: add BHKS cut-separation prefix certificate, critical-path)\nvia `pod once`. Split into its two staged deliverables: the executable prefix\ncut (\"lands first\") and the `CutRetention` proof. **Landed the executable\nprefix cut; rejected the `CutRetention` proof half as an unsound target** (same\ndefect #7575 already diagnosed, invariant under the prefix change), with a\nmachine-checked comment on the issue.\n\n### Executable prefix cut (landed)\n\n`HexBerlekampZassenhaus/Basic.lean`: added `bhksCutPrefixCount` (computes\n`t = (max passing GS index) + 1` by an increasing-order fold) and rewired\n`bhksCutProjectReducedRows` / `bhksRetainedRowIndices` to retain the contiguous\nprefix `b_0 … b_t` in original order, including earlier rows whose own GS norm\nexceeds the radius. This is the genuine BHKS Lemma 5.7 prefix and fixes the\nnon-prefix defect diagnosed on #7578 (per-row survivor set was not a GS-norm\nprefix under LLL).\n\nVerification:\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhaus` — clean.\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.Lattice` — clean (proof layer intact;\n  nothing constructs `CutRetention`, so the runtime change breaks no proof).\n- Regenerated `conformance-fixtures/HexBerlekampZassenhaus/bz.jsonl` via\n  `hexbz_emit_fixtures` — **byte-identical** to the committed flint-verified\n  ground truth. Factorization output is unchanged (survivor set only grows;\n  final factor multisets match flint).\n- Added-line forbidden-token scan: no sorry/axiom/native_decide/TODO/FIXME.\n\n### `CutRetention` proof half (rejected as unsound — premise defect)\n\n`CutRetention.mem_projected_of_norm_le` (`Lattice.lean:424`) quantifies over\n**arbitrary** `v : Fin r → ℤ` with only a norm bound and concludes membership in\nthe **projected** span `projectedRowSpanInt`. This is unsatisfiable — the same\ndefect #7575 posted (kernel-checked `IsEmpty (CutRetention P)`) — and the prefix\nchange does **not** repair it. Machine-checked under the new prefix code:\n`factorCount=1, coeffWidth=0, reduced=[[2]]` gives `cutRadiusSq4=4`,\n`bhksCutPrefixCount=0`, `bhksCutProjectReducedRows=#[]`, so\n`projectedRowSpanInt=⊥`, yet `v=![1]` has `‖v‖²=1 ≤ 4`. Membership in a\nsublattice never follows from a norm bound alone.\n\nThe issue's *Proof section* states the SOUND fact correctly (\"any\n**original-lattice** vector v with `‖v‖²≤radius` lies in the integer span of the\nretained prefix rows\"). The defect is only the packaging: the sound statement is\nabout lattice vectors of the `(r+n)`-dim reduced basis, not arbitrary `ℤ^r`\nvectors, and not the projected span. `CutRetention` drops the lattice hypothesis\nand projects — that is where soundness is lost.\n\n## Current frontier\n\nExecutable prefix cut is the substrate any sound survivor-span proof needs.\nLanded via `create-pr --partial` (issue → `replan`). Comment on #7592 gives the\ncorrected target.\n\n## Next step (for the replan)\n\nProve the full-lattice survivor-span lemma directly into `CutProjectionHypotheses`\n(NOT `CutRetention`, which should be retired along with\n`cutProjectionHypotheses_of_retention` / `..._of_retention`):\n- For `v` with `memLattice reducedMatrix v` and `‖v‖² ≤ R`, `v ∈` ℤ-span of the\n  prefix rows `b_0 … b_t`, via `basis_normSq` (gramDetVec ↔ `‖b*_i‖²`,\n  `HexGramSchmidtMathlib/Int.lean:1070`) and the integer-top-coefficient /\n  orthogonality lemmas (`normSq_latticeVec_ge_min_basis_normSq`,\n  `rowCombination_normSq_ge_of_orthogonal_coeff_sq_ge_one`,\n  `HexGramSchmidt/Int.lean`).\n- Then the factorization→lattice bridge #7578 listed as absent: true-support\n  indicators are projections of genuine short lattice vectors (CLD-vanishing for\n  true factors), so `CutProjectionHypotheses` holds at exactly the indicators it\n  is consumed at (`PartitionRefinement.lean:484`).\n\n## Blockers\n\nNone for the landed change. The proof half is blocked on a corrected,\nnon-`CutRetention` target (premise defect), routed to replan.\nNote: the worktree carries pre-existing unrelated `.claude/*` modifications from\nbefore this session; not touched or staged.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/b3648b20-dbeb-4ec6-9d5f-7596450f9b1f.stdout:16:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_7","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' progress/20260617_162408_8df0d1a6.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Progress - session 8df0d1a6 (/work → /feature)\n\n## Accomplished\n\n### #7782 — FpPoly→Mathlib irreducibility transport (landed)\n\nAdded `irreducible_toMathlibPolynomial_of_fpPolyIrreducible`\n(`HexBerlekampMathlib/Basic.lean`, after\n`isUnit_toMathlibPolynomial_of_isUnitPolynomial`):\n\n```lean\ntheorem irreducible_toMathlibPolynomial_of_fpPolyIrreducible\n    [Fact (Nat.Prime p)] {f : Hex.FpPoly p}\n    (hpos : 0 < (toMathlibPolynomial f).natDegree)\n    (hirr : Hex.FpPoly.Irreducible f) :\n    Irreducible (toMathlibPolynomial f)\n```\n\nChose the natDegree positive-degree form (the issue's permitted \"natDegree\nform\") since it makes the non-unit leg a one-liner\n(`Polynomial.not_isUnit_of_natDegree_pos`) and needs no monicity. The premise\ncheck holds: the hypothesis excludes the constant-unit case where\n`FpPoly.Irreducible` is vacuously true. Factorization leg pulls `a, b` back\nthrough `fpPolyEquiv.symm`, applies `hirr.2` to get a degree-0 factor, and\ntransports it to a Mathlib unit via\n`isUnit_toMathlibPolynomial_of_isUnitPolynomial`.\n\nLeft a pointer comment at the in-scope-excluded `irreducible_of_mem_berlekampFactor`\nsorry naming the new primitive and the remaining executable gap (no per-member\n`FpPoly.Irreducible` theorem; only `berlekampFactor_singleton_irreducible`).\n\nVerification: `lake build HexBerlekampMathlib` clean. Sorry count in the file\nunchanged (2 → 2, both pre-existing). No forbidden tokens in added lines.\n\n### #7772 — premise-rejected (separate, earlier in this session)\n\nDiagnosed and `skip`ped #7772 (BHKS fast-core forward-recovery producer): it\nrequires inhabiting `ForwardRecoveryInputs.lattice_eq_indicators` (full `L'=W`),\nwhose reverse inclusion routes through the dead `resultant_divisible_by_p_pow`\nclause (#6779/#2564) — yet that field is unused downstream (`_of_forwardInputs`\nproves irreducibility via the forward `_of_cut`). Recommended re-targeting at the\nforward `_of_lift` route. Full evidence on the issue comment.\n\n## Current frontier\n\n`irreducible_of_mem_berlekampFactor` sorry now has a clean Mathlib leg; the\nremaining gap is the executable per-member irreducibility theorem.\n\n## Next step\n\nA successor can prove \"every `g ∈ (berlekampFactor f hmonic).factors` is\n`FpPoly.Irreducible`\" (executable side), then compose with the new primitive to\ndischarge the `irreducible_of_mem_berlekampFactor` sorry.\n\n## Blockers\n\nNone for #7782 (landed). #7772 blocked on #6779/#2564 (van Hoeij CLD rollback).\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
sessions/b3648b20-dbeb-4ec6-9d5f-7596450f9b1f.stdout:18:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_8","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' progress/20260618_656d42c5.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Progress — 2026-06-18 (session 656d42c5, /work)\n\n## Accomplished\n\nPicked up via `/work`; exercised judgment across the unclaimed feature queue.\n\n### #7783 (executable per-member Berlekamp irreducibility) — diagnosed & skipped\n\nPremise-checked the issue's proof route and found it **impossible as stated**.\n`berlekampFactor` (`Factor.lean:114-118`) fixes the witness set to `f`'s kernel\n`(fixedSpaceKernel f hmonic).toList` and applies it to *every* factor; loop\ntermination gives, per factor `g`, `∀ w ∈ f-kernel, kernelWitnessSplit? g w =\nnone`. But the only completeness producer\n`irreducible_of_no_kernelWitnessSplit_squareFree` (`RabinSoundness.lean:2994`)\nrequires the factor's **own** kernel `(fixedSpaceKernel g _).toList`. No bridge\nbetween `f`'s kernel and a divisor's kernel exists in `HexBerlekamp/`. The\ntheorem is *true* (Berlekamp completeness) but needs a new generalized\ncompleteness theorem (CRT-lift of a g-kernel splitter into f's kernel +\nspan-mod-g linearity), not the thin per-factor composition. Posted evidence-rich\ncomment, `coordination skip 7783` (→ replan).\n\n### #7786 (BHKS fast-core forward-recovery via _of_lift) — deliverable 1 landed\n\n- **Deliverable 1 (landed):** added\n  `factorFastFactorsWithBound_raw_zpolyIrreducible_of_cut`\n  (`PartitionRefinement.lean`), the forward `factorFastFactorsWithBound`-level\n  raw-irreducibility wrapper with **no** `ForwardRecoveryInputs` param, routed\n  through `..._of_cut` (`:530`). Avoids the dead reverse `lattice_eq_indicators`\n  field (route-blocked behind #6779/#2564). Thin re-parameterization of the\n  `_of_forwardInputs` sibling (`:1010`).\n- **Deliverable 2 (blocked):** the producer feeding `_of_lift` needs the\n  per-support `∀ S, TrueFactorLift` family at first-success precision. Only the\n  single-subset constructors `trueFactorLiftOfSubset` /\n  `trueFactorLiftSemanticsOfToMonicSubset` (`LiftBridge.lean:78,104`) exist, and\n  they demand an exact `liftedFactorProduct d T = factor` witness per support —\n  the unproduced recovery/coverage direction. Every `∀ S, TrueFactorLift` is a\n  hypothesis, never a conclusion. Diagnosed on the issue with the missing-lemma\n  shape; partial PR.\n\n## Verification\n\n- `lake build HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib.PartitionRefinement` → built the full\n  CI-gated library (3777 jobs), `Build completed successfully`, 0 `error:`.\n- `python3 scripts/check_dag.py` → exit 0.\n- `git diff --check` clean; no `sorry`/`axiom`/`native_decide`/`TODO`/`FIXME` on\n  added lines.\n\n## Current frontier\n\nDeliverable 1's `_of_cut` wrapper is the furthest-forward fast-route piece; it\ncomposes the instant a deliverable-2 coverage producer exists.\n\n## Next step\n\nReplan deliverable 2 as its own issue gated on a coverage substrate: a producer\nconcluding `∀ S : trueSupports, BHKS.TrueFactorLift L S` (+ semantics +\nseparation) from the first-success recovery (the per-support exact\n`liftedFactorProduct d T = factor`). An *unconditional* form (for #6672) also\nneeds the scheduled-loop determinism `hcore` (BHKS precision-soundness, not in\ntree). For #7783: replan as a generalized completeness theorem\n`irreducible_of_no_kernelWitnessSplit_squareFree_of_dvd` + thin loop assembly.\n\n## Blockers\n\n- #7783: missing generalized completeness theorem (witness-set bridge).\n- #7786 deliverable 2: missing per-support coverage producer; unconditional form\n  additionally blocked on scheduled-loop determinism.\n\nNote: worktree carries pre-existing unrelated `.claude/*` modifications from\nbefore this session; not touched or staged.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

  indicator_mem_projected S := by
    set v := (data S).vector with hv
    have hind : (Hex.bhksProjectedRowsTrace L hrows).reducedMatrix.independent := by
      rw [Hex.bhksProjectedRowsTrace_reducedMatrix_eq]
      exact Hex.lllSteered_independent L.basis (3 / 4) Hex.lll_delta_lower
        Hex.lll_delta_upper hrows hbasis
    have hmemRed :
        Hex.Matrix.memLattice (Hex.bhksProjectedRowsTrace L hrows).reducedMatrix v :=
      (traceReducedMatrix_memLattice_iff L hrows v).mpr (by
        simpa [hv] using (data S).memLattice)
    have hnorm :
        4 * ((Hex.Vector.normSq v : Int) : ℚ) ≤ (Hex.bhksCutRadiusSq4 L : ℚ) := by
      have ht := (data S).four_mul_sq_norm_le
      rw [← hv] at ht
      have hsum :
          (∑ i : Fin (L.factorCount + L.coeffWidth), (((v[i] : Int) : ℝ) ^ 2))
            = ((Hex.Vector.normSq v : Int) : ℝ) := by
        rw [normSq_eq_sum]; push_cast; ring
      rw [hsum] at ht
      have hZ : 4 * (Hex.Vector.normSq v : Int) ≤ (Hex.bhksCutRadiusSq4 L : Int) := by
        have hr : ((4 * Hex.Vector.normSq v : Int) : ℝ)
            ≤ ((Hex.bhksCutRadiusSq4 L : Int) : ℝ) := by push_cast at ht ⊢; linarith
        exact_mod_cast hr
      exact_mod_cast hZ
    have hpref := mem_prefixSubmodule_of_normSq_le L
      (Hex.bhksProjectedRowsTrace L hrows).reducedMatrix hind v hmemRed hnorm
    have hmem := projFirst_mem_projectedRowSpanInt_of_mem_prefixSubmodule L hrows
      (HexMatrixMathlib.vectorEquiv v) hpref
    have hproj : projFirst L.factorCount L.coeffWidth (HexMatrixMathlib.vectorEquiv v)
        = indicatorVector S.1 := by
      funext i
      rw [projFirst_apply, HexMatrixMathlib.vectorEquiv_apply]
      exact (data S).project_eq i
    rwa [hproj] at hmem

/--
**True-factor cut-projection producer.**

Build `CutProjectionHypotheses` for a family of true-factor supports directly
from their CLD-vector certificates (`TrueFactorCLDVectorData`), their tight
norm bounds (`TrueFactorCLDTightNormBound`), and independence of the BHKS basis.
Each true support's indicator vector is the first block of a genuine short
lattice vector, which the prefix survivor-span lemma places in the retained
prefix span; projecting to the first `factorCount` coordinates lands the
indicator in `projectedRowSpanInt`.  This route does **not** pass through
`CutRetention`.
-/
def cutProjectionHypotheses_of_trueFactors
    (L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis) (hrows : 1 ≤ L.factorCount + L.coeffWidth)
    (hbasis : L.basis.independent)
    (trueSupports : Set (Set (Fin (Hex.bhksProjectedRows L hrows).factorCount)))
    (data : ∀ S : trueSupports, TrueFactorCLDVectorData L S.1)
    (tight : ∀ S : trueSupports, TrueFactorCLDTightNormBound L S.1) :
    CutProjectionHypotheses (Hex.bhksProjectedRows L hrows) trueSupports :=
  cutProjectionHypotheses_of_shortVectors L hrows hbasis trueSupports
    (fun S =>
      { vector := trueFactorCLDVector L S.1
        memLattice := trueFactorCLDVector_memLattice L S.1
        project_eq := (data S).project_eq
        four_mul_sq_norm_le := (tight S).four_mul_sq_norm_le })

/-- Each projected rational row is one of the generators of the rational row space. -/
theorem projectedRow_mem_projectedRowSpaceRat
    (L : Hex.BhksProjectedRows) (i : Fin L.projectedRows.size) :
    Matrix.row (projectedRowsRatMatrix L) i ∈ projectedRowSpaceRat L := by
  exact Submodule.subset_span ⟨i, rfl⟩

/-- `intVectorToRat` of the zero vector is the zero rational vector. -/
@[simp] theorem intVectorToRat_zero {r : Nat} :
    intVectorToRat (0 : Fin r → ℤ) = 0 := by
  funext i
  simp [intVectorToRat]

/-- `intVectorToRat` commutes with pointwise addition. -/
@[simp] theorem intVectorToRat_add {r : Nat} (u v : Fin r → ℤ) :
    intVectorToRat (u + v) = intVectorToRat u + intVectorToRat v := by
  funext i
  simp [intVectorToRat, Pi.add_apply]

/-- `intVectorToRat` of an integer-scalar multiple is the rational-cast scalar
times the rational vector. -/

 succeeded in 0ms:
    exact_mod_cast hnat
  exact hnorm.trans hfactor_le_cut

/-- Projected-row form of `indicatorVector_sq_sum_le_bhksCutRadiusSq4`, stated
against the cut-radius field stored by the executable Gram-Schmidt cut output. -/
theorem indicatorVector_sq_sum_le_projectedRows_cutRadiusSq4
    (L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis)
    (hrows : 1 ≤ L.factorCount + L.coeffWidth)
    (S : Set (Fin (Hex.bhksProjectedRows L hrows).factorCount)) :
    (∑ i : Fin (Hex.bhksProjectedRows L hrows).factorCount,
        ((((indicatorVector S i : ℤ) : ℝ) ^ 2))) ≤
      ((Hex.bhksProjectedRows L hrows).cutRadiusSq4 : ℝ) := by
  simpa [Hex.bhksProjectedRows] using
    (indicatorVector_sq_sum_le_bhksCutRadiusSq4 L S)

/-- A support of lifted local-factor indices for a BHKS lattice basis. -/
abbrev LiftedFactorSupport (L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis) :=
  Set (Fin L.factorCount)

/-- The `0/1` indicator vector attached to a lifted-factor support. -/
def liftedFactorIndicator (L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis) (S : LiftedFactorSupport L) :
    Fin L.factorCount → ℤ :=
  indicatorVector S

/--
Product of the lifted factors selected by a support, using the factor order
stored in the BHKS lattice basis.  The definition indexes by `factorCount`
rather than the raw array size so it remains well-typed for abstract
`BhksLatticeBasis` values; `TrueFactorLift.basis_eq` ties these together for
the executable basis.
-/
def supportProduct (L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis) (S : LiftedFactorSupport L) :
    Hex.ZPoly :=
  by
    classical
    exact Array.polyProduct <|
      (((List.finRange L.factorCount).filter fun i => decide (i ∈ S)).map
        fun i => L.liftedFactors.getD i.val 1).toArray

/--
Sum of the per-selected-factor executable CLD quotients, taken over the same
factor order as `supportProduct`.  Its `j`-th coefficient is the pre-`psiCut`,
pre-indicator column-`j` entry of the true-factor CLD vector; the centering
(`psiCut`) and indicator weighting are layered on top by the tight-column work
(`#7651`).
-/
def supportCldSum (L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis) (S : LiftedFactorSupport L)
    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (p a : Nat) : Hex.ZPoly :=
  by
    classical
    exact
      (((List.finRange L.factorCount).filter fun i => decide (i ∈ S)).map
        fun i => Hex.cldQuotientMod f (L.liftedFactors.getD i.val 1) p a).sum

/--
Selected lifted factors as an executable array, in the same support order used
by `supportProduct` and `supportCldSum`.
-/
def selectedFactors (L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis) (S : LiftedFactorSupport L) :
    Array Hex.ZPoly :=
  by
    classical
    exact
      (((List.finRange L.factorCount).filter fun i => decide (i ∈ S)).map
        fun i => L.liftedFactors.getD i.val 1).toArray

/--
Definitional bridge for the aggregate CLD tail helper: it cuts once after
folding the selected `cldQuotientMod` polynomials into one quotient sum.
-/
theorem aggregateCldTail_eq_foldl
    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (p a j : Nat) (selected : Array Hex.ZPoly) :
    Hex.aggregateCldTail f p a j selected =
      Hex.psiCut p a (Hex.bhksCoeffCutThreshold p f j)
        ((selected.foldl
          (fun acc g => acc + Hex.cldQuotientMod f g p a) (0 : Hex.ZPoly)).coeff j) := by
  rfl

/--
Support-specialised form of `aggregateCldTail_eq_foldl`, using the canonical
selected-factor array attached to a proof support.
-/
theorem aggregateCldTail_selectedFactors_eq
    (L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis) (S : LiftedFactorSupport L)
    (f : Hex.ZPoly) (p a j : Nat) :
    Hex.aggregateCldTail f p a j (selectedFactors L S) =
      Hex.psiCut p a (Hex.bhksCoeffCutThreshold p f j)
        (((selectedFactors L S).foldl
          (fun acc g => acc + Hex.cldQuotientMod f g p a) (0 : Hex.ZPoly)).coeff j) := by
  rfl

/--
Proof-facing package saying that a BHKS lattice basis and lifted-factor support
come from concrete lift data for an integer polynomial.

The field `basis_eq` is the structural bridge: it pins `L` to the executable
`Hex.bhksLatticeBasis f p a liftedFactors`, so `cldRows` and cut thresholds are
the rows produced by `Hex.cldCoeffs`/`Hex.psiCut`, not arbitrary arrays.  The
fields `factor`, `cofactor`, `factor_mul`, and `support_product_eq` identify
the selected lifted factors with a genuine factor of `f`.
-/
structure TrueFactorLift
    (L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis) (S : LiftedFactorSupport L) where
  f : Hex.ZPoly
  p : Nat
  a : Nat
  liftedFactors : Array Hex.ZPoly
  basis_eq : L = Hex.bhksLatticeBasis f p a liftedFactors
  factor : Hex.ZPoly
  cofactor : Hex.ZPoly
  factor_mul : factor * cofactor = f
  support_product_eq : supportProduct L S = factor

/--
Proof-facing package for a true factor recovered from the selected lifted-factor
product by the executable centered/dilated recovery path.

This is parallel to `TrueFactorLift`, but it deliberately does not assert the
raw integer equality `supportProduct L S = factor`.  The recovery algorithm
only exposes the centered representative modulo `p ^ a`, dilated by the
leading coefficient of `f`, as the recovered integer factor.  Downstream lemmas
that only need the BHKS support, factor/cofactor identity, and recovered product
shape should consume this package instead of strengthening their hypotheses back
to raw selected-product equality.
-/
structure RecoveredLift
    (L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis) (S : LiftedFactorSupport L) where
  f : Hex.ZPoly
  p : Nat
  a : Nat
  liftedFactors : Array Hex.ZPoly
  basis_eq : L = Hex.bhksLatticeBasis f p a liftedFactors
  factor : Hex.ZPoly
  cofactor : Hex.ZPoly
  factor_mul : factor * cofactor = f
  recovered_eq :
    Hex.ZPoly.dilate (Hex.DensePoly.leadingCoeff f)
        (Hex.centeredLiftPoly (supportProduct L S) (p ^ a)) =
      factor

/--
Semantic lift facts needed to interpret the executable CLD rows as genuine
logarithmic-derivative columns.

`TrueFactorLift` itself only ties the BHKS row basis to concrete lifted factors
and identifies the selected product with an integer factor.  The CLD quotient
lemmas also need monicity, positive degree, and a modular cofactor witness for
each selected lifted factor.  This package keeps those hypotheses explicit so
downstream aggregation lemmas cannot silently recover them from the weaker
structural bridge.
-/
structure TrueFactorLiftSemantics
    {L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis} {S : LiftedFactorSupport L}
    (D : TrueFactorLift L S) where
  factor_monic : Hex.DensePoly.Monic D.factor
  selected_monic :
    ∀ i : Fin L.factorCount, i ∈ S →
      Hex.DensePoly.Monic (D.liftedFactors.getD i.val 1)
  selected_pos_degree :
    ∀ i : Fin L.factorCount, i ∈ S →
      0 < (D.liftedFactors.getD i.val 1).degree?.getD 0
  selectedCofactor : ∀ i : Fin L.factorCount, i ∈ S → Hex.ZPoly
  selected_congr :
    ∀ (i : Fin L.factorCount) (hi : i ∈ S),
      Hex.ZPoly.congr D.f
        ((D.liftedFactors.getD i.val 1) * selectedCofactor i hi)
        (D.p ^ D.a)

/-- A left fold accumulating `g` over a list equals the running accumulator plus
the sum of the mapped list; the start-from-`acc` form used to read a fold-sum off
at `acc = 0`. -/
private theorem foldl_add_eq_acc_add_listSum {M : Type*} [AddCommMonoid M]
    {α : Type*} (l : List α) (g : α → M) (acc : M) :
    l.foldl (fun acc i => acc + g i) acc = acc + (l.map g).sum := by
  induction l generalizing acc with
  | nil => simp
  | cons x xs ih =>
      simp only [List.foldl_cons, List.map_cons, List.sum_cons]
      rw [ih, add_assoc]

/-- A fold-sum over `List.finRange n` equals the finite sum over `Fin n`. -/
private theorem finRange_foldl_add_eq_sum {M : Type*} [AddCommMonoid M] {n : Nat}
    (g : Fin n → M) :
    (List.finRange n).foldl (fun acc i => acc + g i) 0 = ∑ i, g i := by
  rw [foldl_add_eq_acc_add_listSum, zero_add, Fin.sum_univ_def]

/-- The entry of a row combination at column `k` is the explicit finite sum, over
the row index, of the column-`k` matrix entries weighted by the coefficients. -/
theorem rowCombination_getElem_eq_sum {n m : Nat}
    (M : Hex.Matrix ℤ n m) (c : Vector ℤ n) (k : Fin m) :
    (Hex.Matrix.rowCombination M c)[k] = ∑ i : Fin n, M[i][k] * c[i] := by

 succeeded in 0ms:
This derives the `count_ge` half from the *forward* inclusion `W ⊆ L'`
(`CutProjectionHypotheses`) through the partition-refinement argument
(`supportPartitionByMinColumn_length_le_bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators_size`),
with no reverse `L' = W` separation and hence no bad-vector resultant valuation.
Unlike `factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_count_ge`, it does not route through
an `ExpectedTrueFactors` package (whose construction needs the full `L' = W`).

The two executable-success facts it consumes isolate the remaining plumbing: the
emitted factor count equals the equivalence-class count (`hsize`) and the
support-partition length equals the integer-factor count (`hpartition`).  The
successful-branch premise `_h` is retained to mark the eventual source of those
facts once the executable recovery wiring discharges them. -/
theorem factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_count_ge_of_cut
    {core : Hex.ZPoly} {B : Nat} {primeData : Hex.PrimeChoiceData}
    {k fuel : Nat} {coreFactors : Array Hex.ZPoly}
    {L : Hex.BhksProjectedRows}
    (trueSupports : Set (Set (Fin L.factorCount)))
    (_h : Hex.factorFastCoreWithBound core B primeData k fuel = some coreFactors)
    (hcut : BHKS.CutProjectionHypotheses L trueSupports)
    (hsize : coreFactors.size = (Hex.bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators L).size)
    (hpartition :
      (BHKS.supportPartitionByMinColumn trueSupports).length =
        (UniqueFactorizationMonoid.normalizedFactors
          (HexPolyZMathlib.toPolynomial core)).card) :
    (UniqueFactorizationMonoid.normalizedFactors
      (HexPolyZMathlib.toPolynomial core)).card ≤
        (coreFactors.toList.map HexPolyZMathlib.toPolynomial).length := by
  rw [List.length_map, Array.length_toList, hsize, ← hpartition]
  exact
    BHKS.supportPartitionByMinColumn_length_le_bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators_size
      L trueSupports hcut

/-- Forward-cut cardinality equality for a successful BHKS fast-core branch.

Pairs the executable upper bound `factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_count_le`
with the forward-inclusion lower bound
`factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_count_ge_of_cut`, so the count equality is
established from the forward inclusion `W ⊆ L'`
(`BHKS.CutProjectionHypotheses`) alone.  Unlike

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "git show origin/main:HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/PartitionRefinement.lean | sed -n '650,760p'" in /Users/kim/projects/lean/hex
 succeeded in 0ms:
tight cut-radius certificate `TrueFactorCLDTightNormBound`; its loose companion
(`toNormBound`) and the structural block-form coordinate identities assemble the
`TrueFactorCLDVectorData` arm.  Both arms drive
`factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_trueFactors`.

This is the first consumer to wire the analytic stack (#7650 product CLD
identity, #7674 semantic lift facts, #7712 tight column bound producer) through
to the irreducibility endpoint.  The remaining residual is supplying the
per-support `TrueFactorLift`/semantics/separation family for the first
successful recovery; the separation hypotheses `hp`, `hk`, `hsep` are the BHKS
Lemma 5.7 inputs at the first-success lift precision, threaded here verbatim
rather than re-derived from the global Mignotte recovery bound. -/
theorem factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_lift
    {core : Hex.ZPoly} {B : Nat} {primeData : Hex.PrimeChoiceData}
    {k fuel : Nat} {coreFactors : Array Hex.ZPoly}
    {L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis} {hrows : 1 ≤ L.factorCount + L.coeffWidth}
    (trueSupports :
      Set (Set (Fin (Hex.bhksProjectedRows L hrows).factorCount)))
    (hcore_ne : core ≠ 0)
    (h : Hex.factorFastCoreWithBound core B primeData k fuel = some coreFactors)
    (hbasis : L.basis.independent)
    (lift : ∀ S : trueSupports, BHKS.TrueFactorLift L S.1)
    (sem : ∀ S : trueSupports, BHKS.TrueFactorLiftSemantics (lift S))
    (hp : ∀ S : trueSupports, 2 ≤ (lift S).p)
    (hk : ∀ S : trueSupports, 1 < (lift S).p ^ (lift S).a)
    (hsep : ∀ S : trueSupports,
      ∀ j, 2 * Hex.bhksCoeffBound (lift S).f j < (lift S).p ^ (lift S).a)
    (hsize :
      coreFactors.size =
        (Hex.bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators (Hex.bhksProjectedRows L hrows)).size)
    (hpartition :
      (BHKS.supportPartitionByMinColumn trueSupports).length =
        (UniqueFactorizationMonoid.normalizedFactors
          (HexPolyZMathlib.toPolynomial core)).card) :
    ∀ factor ∈ coreFactors.toList, Hex.ZPoly.Irreducible factor := by
  have tight : ∀ S : trueSupports, BHKS.TrueFactorCLDTightNormBound L S.1 :=
    fun S =>
      BHKS.tightNormBound_of_lift (lift S)
        (BHKS.tightColumnBound_of_lift (lift S) (sem S) (hp S) (hk S) (hsep S))
  have data : ∀ S : trueSupports, BHKS.TrueFactorCLDVectorData L S.1 :=
    fun S =>
      { project_eq := fun i =>
          BHKS.trueFactorCLDVector_project_of_blockForm S.1 (lift S).blockForm i
        coeff_eq := fun j =>
          BHKS.trueFactorCLDVector_coeff_of_blockForm S.1 (lift S).blockForm j
        norm_bound := (tight S).toNormBound }
  exact factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_trueFactors
    trueSupports hcore_ne h hbasis data tight hsize hpartition

/--
Capstone composition for the fast `h_raw` disjunct via the **aggregate-tail CLD
lattice path** (issue #7876).  Where
`factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_lift` needs a
`TrueFactorLift` (raw per-factor integer divisibility), this consumes the weaker
`RecoveredLift` that the executable fast-core recovery actually exposes: each
support's `RecoveredLift` produces a period-adjusted `SupportShortVectorData`
(`BHKS.supportShortVectorData_of_recoveredLift`, in the monic coordinate), which
`BHKS.cutProjectionHypotheses_of_shortVectors` turns into the forward cut
certificate.  This is the route that survives the CLD period trap
(#7866/#7867): the per-factor column bound is unavailable, but the
period-reduced aggregate column is bounded by the aggregate residue alone.

The monic-coordinate hypothesis `hf_lc` (`leadingCoeff f = 1`) and the
precision/threshold separations `hk`/`hsep`/`hthr` are the BHKS Lemma 5.7 inputs
at the first-success lift precision, threaded here verbatim; `hfac` is the
Hensel-factorisation datum `∏ gᵢ ≡ f (mod pᵃ)` per selected factor. -/
theorem factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_recoveredLift
    {core : Hex.ZPoly} {B : Nat} {primeData : Hex.PrimeChoiceData}
    {k fuel : Nat} {coreFactors : Array Hex.ZPoly}
    {L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis} {hrows : 1 ≤ L.factorCount + L.coeffWidth}
    (trueSupports :
      Set (Set (Fin (Hex.bhksProjectedRows L hrows).factorCount)))
    (hcore_ne : core ≠ 0)
    (h : Hex.factorFastCoreWithBound core B primeData k fuel = some coreFactors)
    (hbasis : L.basis.independent)
    (lift : ∀ S : trueSupports, BHKS.RecoveredLift L S.1)
    (hf_lc : ∀ S : trueSupports, Hex.DensePoly.leadingCoeff (lift S).f = 1)
    (hfactor_monic : ∀ S : trueSupports,
      (HexPolyMathlib.toPolynomial (lift S).factor).Monic)
    (hp : ∀ S : trueSupports, 2 ≤ (lift S).p)
    (hk : ∀ S : trueSupports, 1 < (lift S).p ^ (lift S).a)
    (hsep : ∀ S : trueSupports,
      ∀ j, 2 * Hex.bhksCoeffBound (lift S).f j < (lift S).p ^ (lift S).a)
    (hthr : ∀ S : trueSupports,
      ∀ j, Hex.bhksCoeffCutThreshold (lift S).p (lift S).f j ≤ (lift S).a)
    (hfac : ∀ S : trueSupports, ∀ i : Fin L.factorCount, i ∈ S.1 →
        ∃ g : Hex.ZPoly,
          Hex.DensePoly.Monic (L.liftedFactors.getD i.val 1) ∧
          0 < (L.liftedFactors.getD i.val 1).degree?.getD 0 ∧
          Hex.ZPoly.congr (lift S).f ((L.liftedFactors.getD i.val 1) * g)
            ((lift S).p ^ (lift S).a))
    (hsize :
      coreFactors.size =
        (Hex.bhksEquivalenceClassIndicators (Hex.bhksProjectedRows L hrows)).size)
    (hpartition :
      (BHKS.supportPartitionByMinColumn trueSupports).length =
        (UniqueFactorizationMonoid.normalizedFactors
          (HexPolyZMathlib.toPolynomial core)).card) :
    ∀ factor ∈ coreFactors.toList, Hex.ZPoly.Irreducible factor := by
  have hcut := BHKS.cutProjectionHypotheses_of_shortVectors L hrows hbasis trueSupports
    (fun S => BHKS.supportShortVectorData_of_recoveredLift (lift S) (hf_lc S)
      (hfactor_monic S) (hp S) (hk S) (hsep S) (hthr S) (hfac S))
  exact factorFastCoreWithBound_some_factor_zpolyIrreducible_of_cut trueSupports
    hcore_ne h hcut hsize hpartition

/--
Fast-core irreducibility wrapper from a monic recovered-lift family produced by
`recoveredLift_subtypeFamily_of_indicatorCandidates`.

`hsize`, `hpartition`, and the CLD separation/threshold hypotheses), but derives

 succeeded in 0ms:
/--
Coefficient vector selecting the top BHKS lattice rows attached to a
lifted-factor support.  The first `factorCount` entries are the support
indicator; the diagonal correction-row coefficients are zero.
-/
def trueFactorRowCoeffs (L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis) (S : LiftedFactorSupport L) :
    Vector ℤ (L.factorCount + L.coeffWidth) :=
  Vector.ofFn fun i =>
    if hi : i.val < L.factorCount then indicatorVector S ⟨i.val, hi⟩ else 0

/--
Full BHKS lattice vector attached to a lifted-factor support.  It is the sum of
the selected top rows of the all-coefficients BHKS lattice basis; equivalently,
its first block is the `0/1` support indicator and its coefficient block is the
corresponding CLD-column sum, with zero diagonal correction coefficients.
-/
def trueFactorCLDVector (L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis) (S : LiftedFactorSupport L) :
    Vector ℤ (L.factorCount + L.coeffWidth) :=
  Hex.Matrix.rowCombination L.basis (trueFactorRowCoeffs L S)

/-- The true-factor CLD vector is, by construction, in the integer row lattice
of the all-coefficients BHKS basis. -/
theorem trueFactorCLDVector_memLattice
    (L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis) (S : LiftedFactorSupport L) :
    Hex.Matrix.memLattice L.basis (trueFactorCLDVector L S) := by
  exact ⟨trueFactorRowCoeffs L S, rfl⟩

/-- Explicit package for the full BHKS cut-radius bound on a true-factor CLD
vector.  This is intentionally a separate certificate: proving the coefficient
tail bound requires the lower-level CLD magnitude estimates, while membership
and projection are structural facts about the lattice basis. -/
structure TrueFactorCLDNormBound
    (L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis) (S : LiftedFactorSupport L) where
  sq_norm_le :
    (∑ i : Fin (L.factorCount + L.coeffWidth),
      ((((trueFactorCLDVector L S)[i] : ℤ) : ℝ) ^ 2)) ≤
        (Hex.bhksCutRadiusSq4 L : ℝ)

/--
Certificate surface for the true-factor full BHKS vector.  Membership in the
integer row lattice is structural for `trueFactorCLDVector`; the remaining
fields isolate the canonical-basis obligations: the projected block is the
support indicator, the coefficient block is the executable CLD sum, and the
full vector satisfies the BHKS cut-radius bound.
-/
structure TrueFactorCLDVectorData
    (L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis) (S : LiftedFactorSupport L) where
  project_eq :
    ∀ i : Fin L.factorCount,
      (trueFactorCLDVector L S)[
          (⟨i.val, Nat.lt_add_right L.coeffWidth i.isLt⟩ :
            Fin (L.factorCount + L.coeffWidth))] =
        indicatorVector S i
  coeff_eq :
    ∀ j : Fin L.coeffWidth,
      (trueFactorCLDVector L S)[
          (⟨L.factorCount + j.val, Nat.add_lt_add_left j.isLt L.factorCount⟩ :
            Fin (L.factorCount + L.coeffWidth))] =
        ∑ i : Fin L.factorCount,
          indicatorVector S i * (L.cldRows.getD i.val #[]).getD j.val 0
  norm_bound : TrueFactorCLDNormBound L S

namespace TrueFactorCLDVectorData

/-- The packaged full vector belongs to the all-coefficients BHKS row lattice. -/
theorem memLattice
    {L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis} {S : LiftedFactorSupport L}
    (_D : TrueFactorCLDVectorData L S) :
    Hex.Matrix.memLattice L.basis (trueFactorCLDVector L S) :=
  trueFactorCLDVector_memLattice L S

open Classical in
/--
Raw cut-sum CLD column for the recovered support at tail index `j`: the support
sum of per-factor high-bit cuts.  This is exactly the trailing
`trueFactorCLDVector` coordinate (`trueFactorCoord_eq_recoveredColumnSum`), and
the quantity the #7869 carry lemma reduces modulo the period.
-/
def recoveredColumnSum
    {L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis} {S : LiftedFactorSupport L}
    (D : RecoveredLift L S) (j : Nat) : ℤ :=
  ∑ i ∈ Finset.univ.filter (fun i : Fin L.factorCount => i ∈ S),
    Hex.psiCut D.p D.a (Hex.bhksCoeffCutThreshold D.p D.f j)
      ((Hex.cldQuotientMod D.f (L.liftedFactors.getD i.val 1) D.p D.a).coeff j)

open Classical in
/--
Existence of an integer period multiple reducing `recoveredColumnSum` to a short
value `2·|col − t·p^(a−ℓ_j)| ≤ |S|`.  This is the conclusion of the #7869
aggregate carry lemma; the recovered norm-bound producer discharges it from a
`RecoveredLift` plus Hensel-factorisation data.
-/
def recoveredPeriodReducible
    {L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis} {S : LiftedFactorSupport L}
    (D : RecoveredLift L S) (j : Nat) : Prop :=
  ∃ t : ℤ,
    2 * (recoveredColumnSum D j
      - t * ((D.p : ℤ) ^ (D.a - Hex.bhksCoeffCutThreshold D.p D.f j))).natAbs
      ≤ (Finset.univ.filter (fun i : Fin L.factorCount => i ∈ S)).card

open Classical in
/--
Period-correction coefficient for the recovered CLD vector at tail column `j`.

It is the (negated) integer multiple `t_j` of the period `p^(a−ℓ_j)` that the
#7869 aggregate carry lemma provides: the reduction making the cut-sum column
short.  Defined hypothesis-free via `Classical.choose` of
`recoveredPeriodReducible`, defaulting to `0` when no such witness exists; the
recovered norm-bound producer discharges the existential from a `RecoveredLift`
plus the Hensel-factorisation data, landing in the true branch.  The sign is
negated so that adding the diagonal contribution `p^(a−ℓ_j)·(−t_j)` reduces the
column to `col_j − t_j·p^(a−ℓ_j)`.
-/
noncomputable def recoveredPeriodCoeff
    {L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis} {S : LiftedFactorSupport L}
    (D : RecoveredLift L S) (j : Nat) : ℤ :=
  if h : recoveredPeriodReducible D j then -(Classical.choose h) else 0

/--
Row-coefficient vector for the period-corrected recovered CLD vector: the `0/1`
support indicator on the first `factorCount` rows, and the period multiple
`recoveredPeriodCoeff` on each diagonal (period) row `factorCount + j`.
-/
noncomputable def recoveredRowCoeffs
    {L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis} {S : LiftedFactorSupport L}
    (D : RecoveredLift L S) : Vector ℤ (L.factorCount + L.coeffWidth) :=
  Vector.ofFn fun i =>
    if hi : i.val < L.factorCount then indicatorVector S ⟨i.val, hi⟩
    else recoveredPeriodCoeff D (i.val - L.factorCount)

/--
Period-corrected recovered CLD vector: the integer row-lattice combination of
the BHKS basis with the period-correction coefficients.  Unlike
`trueFactorCLDVector` (zero diagonal coefficients), the diagonal period rows
`diag(p^(a−ℓ_j))` absorb the large part of the raw cut-sum, leaving a short
column while preserving the support projection.
-/
noncomputable def recoveredCLDVector
    {L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis} {S : LiftedFactorSupport L}
    (D : RecoveredLift L S) : Vector ℤ (L.factorCount + L.coeffWidth) :=
  Hex.Matrix.rowCombination L.basis (recoveredRowCoeffs D)

/-- Entry of the recovered row-coefficient vector. -/
theorem recoveredRowCoeffs_getElem
    {L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis} {S : LiftedFactorSupport L}
    (D : RecoveredLift L S) (x : Fin (L.factorCount + L.coeffWidth)) :
    (recoveredRowCoeffs D)[x]
      = if hi : x.val < L.factorCount then indicatorVector S ⟨x.val, hi⟩
        else recoveredPeriodCoeff D (x.val - L.factorCount) := by
  show (recoveredRowCoeffs D).get x = _
  unfold recoveredRowCoeffs
  rw [Vector.get_ofFn]

/-- The recovered CLD vector is, by construction, in the integer row lattice of
the all-coefficients BHKS basis. -/
theorem recoveredCLDVector_memLattice
    {L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis} {S : LiftedFactorSupport L}
    (D : RecoveredLift L S) :
    Hex.Matrix.memLattice L.basis (recoveredCLDVector D) :=
  ⟨recoveredRowCoeffs D, rfl⟩

/--
**Projection preserved (deliverable 1).**  The first `factorCount` coordinates
of `recoveredCLDVector` are the support indicator — identical to
`trueFactorCLDVector` — because the period rows are zero on the first block
(`bhksLatticeEntry` bottom-left).  Downstream `indicator_mem_projected` is
unaffected by the period correction.
-/
theorem recoveredCLDVector_project
    {L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis} {S : LiftedFactorSupport L}
    (D : RecoveredLift L S) (i : Fin L.factorCount) :
    (recoveredCLDVector D)[
        (⟨i.val, Nat.lt_add_right L.coeffWidth i.isLt⟩ :
          Fin (L.factorCount + L.coeffWidth))]
      = indicatorVector S i := by
  unfold recoveredCLDVector
  rw [rowCombination_proj_eq D.blockForm (recoveredRowCoeffs D) i,
    recoveredRowCoeffs_getElem,
    dif_pos (show (Fin.castAdd L.coeffWidth i).val < L.factorCount from i.isLt)]
  congr 1

/--
CLD-block identity for the recovered vector: each tail coordinate is the
corresponding `trueFactorCLDVector` coordinate plus the diagonal period
contribution `p^(a−ℓ_j)·recoveredPeriodCoeff`.
-/
theorem recoveredCLDVector_coeff
    {L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis} {S : LiftedFactorSupport L}
    (D : RecoveredLift L S) (j : Fin L.coeffWidth) :
    (recoveredCLDVector D)[Fin.natAdd L.factorCount j]
      = (trueFactorCLDVector L S)[
          (⟨L.factorCount + j.val, Nat.add_lt_add_left j.isLt L.factorCount⟩ :
            Fin (L.factorCount + L.coeffWidth))]
        + Int.ofNat (L.p ^ (L.precision - L.cutThresholds.getD j.val 0))
            * recoveredPeriodCoeff D j.val := by
  unfold recoveredCLDVector
  rw [rowCombination_tail_coord D.blockForm (recoveredRowCoeffs D) j]
  rw [show (trueFactorCLDVector L S)[
        (⟨L.factorCount + j.val, Nat.add_lt_add_left j.isLt L.factorCount⟩ :
          Fin (L.factorCount + L.coeffWidth))]
      = ∑ i : Fin L.factorCount,
          indicatorVector S i * (L.cldRows.getD i.val #[]).getD j.val 0 from
    trueFactorCLDVector_coeff_of_blockForm S D.blockForm j]
  congr 1
  · refine Finset.sum_congr rfl ?_
    intro i _
    rw [recoveredRowCoeffs_getElem,
      dif_pos (show (Fin.castAdd L.coeffWidth i).val < L.factorCount from i.isLt)]
    congr 2
  · congr 1
    rw [recoveredRowCoeffs_getElem, dif_neg (by simp only [Fin.val_natAdd]; omega)]
    congr 1
    simp only [Fin.val_natAdd, Nat.add_sub_cancel_left]

/--
The trailing `trueFactorCLDVector` coordinate equals the raw recovered cut-sum
column `recoveredColumnSum`: the support indicator collapses the block-form CLD
sum to a support-restricted sum of executable high-bit cuts.
-/
theorem trueFactorCoord_eq_recoveredColumnSum
    {L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis} {S : LiftedFactorSupport L}
    (D : RecoveredLift L S) (j : Fin L.coeffWidth) :
    ((trueFactorCLDVector L S)[
        (⟨L.factorCount + j.val, Nat.add_lt_add_left j.isLt L.factorCount⟩ :
          Fin (L.factorCount + L.coeffWidth))] : ℤ)
      = recoveredColumnSum D j.val := by
  rw [trueFactorCLDVector_coeff_of_blockForm S D.blockForm j]
  unfold recoveredColumnSum
  rw [Finset.sum_filter]
  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
  by_cases hi : i ∈ S
  · rw [indicatorVector_apply_mem S hi, one_mul, if_pos hi]
    have hjlt : j.val < D.f.degree?.getD 0 := j.isLt.trans_eq D.coeffWidth_eq
    have hisz : i.val < D.liftedFactors.size := by
      rw [← D.factorCount_eq]; exact i.isLt
    rw [D.cldRows_eq,
      show (D.liftedFactors.map (fun g => Hex.cldCoeffs D.f D.p D.a g)).getD i.val #[]
          = Hex.cldCoeffs D.f D.p D.a (D.liftedFactors.getD i.val 1) from by
        simp [Array.getD, hisz],
      Hex.cldCoeffs_getD_of_lt D.f D.p D.a (D.liftedFactors.getD i.val 1) j.val hjlt,
      ← D.liftedFactors_eq]
  · rw [indicatorVector_apply_not_mem S hi, zero_mul, if_neg hi]

/--
**Tight cut-radius certificate for the period-corrected recovered CLD vector
(deliverable 2).**  The `TrueFactorCLDTightNormBound`-shaped statement
`4·‖recoveredCLDVector D‖² ≤ bhksCutRadiusSq4`, derived from a `RecoveredLift`
(monic coordinate) via the #7869/#7872 aggregate carry route, never the raw
`support_product_eq`/`TrueFactorLift` route.  The producer lives in
`CLDColumnBound` (downstream of `Lattice`), like
`trueFactorCLDTightNormBound_of_lift`.
-/
structure RecoveredCLDTightNormBound
    {L : Hex.BhksLatticeBasis} {S : LiftedFactorSupport L}
    (D : RecoveredLift L S) where
  four_mul_sq_norm_le :
    4 * (∑ i : Fin (L.factorCount + L.coeffWidth),
      ((((recoveredCLDVector D)[i] : ℤ) : ℝ) ^ 2)) ≤ (Hex.bhksCutRadiusSq4 L : ℝ)

/--
The true-factor indicator lattice `W`, generated by the indicator vectors of
the true integer factors' lifted-factor supports.
-/
def trueFactorIndicatorLattice {r : Nat} (trueSupports : Set (Set (Fin r))) :
    Submodule ℤ (Fin r → ℤ) :=
  Submodule.span ℤ (Set.range fun S : trueSupports => indicatorVector S.1)

/-- Two lifted-factor columns have the same support signature across all true
factor supports. -/
def supportEquivalent {r : Nat} (trueSupports : Set (Set (Fin r)))
    (j k : Fin r) : Prop :=

/bin/zsh -lc "git show origin/main:HexBerlekampZassenhausMathlib/Recovery.lean | sed -n '400,470p'" in /Users/kim/projects/lean/hex
